boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Ohava vs. Murgath
description:
One is a senior grand cleric in the service of the Great Divines (the good Gods), and the other is a brash, self-absorbed, ruthless warlord who literally cannot put down his blessed blade. The two clash in the next set to join the infamous party at our beloved tavern.

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies - Ohava vs. Murgath introduces two new characters, each with their own unique mechanics and flavor. The set includes everything you need to add these characters to your game, but you will need to have at least one of the The Red Dragon Inn base sets to use them.

Ohava, the Grand Cleric: Ohava has been the matron of the Great Temple of Greyport for longer than some of our heroes have been alive. Among her duties are healing, miracle work, team-building exercises, and earning the All-Mother's blessings by trying to keep all four of the Great Divines in harmony with one another.


    The Good: The blessings of the All-Mother are a powerful incentive to Ohava's good works.
    The Bad: Getting four fickle deities to agree on anything takes a LOT of convincing.


Murgath the Blessed: We all know the story of the adventurer who has the misfortuyne of being stuck with a cursed item. But what about the villain who is stuck with a blessed item? Murgath is a vile, ruthless Blackguard who can't get rid of the holy sword Banefall. Banefall does everything in her power to stay Murgath's hand and interfere with his evil plans. She can’t completely stop him, but she can give her blessings to those unfortunate targets of his wrath.


    The Bad: Murgath is a fearsome enemy.
    The Worse: He is this powerful even with Banefall holding him back!


-description from back of the box (with minor corrections)

This game is based on Mackinac Island, a popular tourist spot in Michigan.

From the Publisher:
It's the simple game of familiar sights and fun surprises!

If you've ever been to Mackinac Island you'll certainly remember the fudge, Arch Rock and the Grand Hotel. All those things and more will show up in this quick game for the family.

Players travel the Island hoping to acquire the most Fun chips. Every location and event card will have directions to take more chips or pass them around. Your only choice will be when to stop rolling the die. Do you push your luck hoping for more Fun? Be careful not to Mack' Out!

The small box contains 11 cards, one die, one pawn, 50 Fun chips and rules.

boardgame: Master of Orion: The Board Game – Pollution Processor Promo Card

Originally available at Dice Tower Con

Reveal 5 cards from the top of the deck. Discard one of these cards, gain its exploitation effect, and shuffle the rest of the cards to the deck.

Champions of Arathus is a tabletop game for 3 to 6 players based on RPGs with medieval scenery, but without all those complicated rules of the genre. In this game, you and your friends assume the role of the adventurer at an unknown island in a quest the maximum glory of being the next Champion of Arathus.
Every match the players must fulfill a determined quest (which will be set at the beginning of the match), ranging from defeating unknown beasts to defending cities that have not been destroyed. It can put players against each other or make them work together to achieve a goal, forcing heroes to put their differences aside for a greater good.
Choose your weapon, wear your armor and discover this new and challenging land to become the new Champion of Arathus. Finally yet importantly, have fun!

Turn the tables on your enemies with disruptive new mechanics that let you attack and defend the base of your Staks!
16 new Bots across 60 cards bring a new wave of controlled chaos as you strive to manipulate and destroy all of your opponent's cards.

This is an expansion, but it does not require the original game to play (by using the online rules for the original).

Go Figure is a fast and simple children dexterity game. Every card, picturing animal, object or person, consist mainly from three types of figures and three colors. Game has four unique play variants for different ages - find what is on a picture for youngest players, find unique figure and absent figure for older kids, and find the best combination for mixed ages. Like many dexterity games, Go Figure is easy to teach and deliver fun not only for kids, but for people of all ages!

boardgame: Global War 1936-1945: Ordnance III – Chemical Weapons
description:
This set features a number of new chemical and biological ordnance for use with Global War -2nd edition.  It is a testament to the horrific effects of these weapons that they considered too brutal for use in the Second World War. However, these weapons were at times used clandestinely by the Axis. The Japanese used them against China and the Italians used gas against the Abyssinians.

On the eve of World War II the production capacity of many nations far outstripped its World War I capabilities.  Germany, which could produce about 40,000 tons of gas per year in World War I had the capacity to create 184,000  tons by 1939.  Germany had also excelled in the development of new gases such as Tabun and Sarin gas with near 10 time the toxic potential of Mustard Gas (which itself was responsible for a quarter of all US casualties in WW I).

In these set we will explore the use of weapons in select theatres as well as the grim possibility of their full-scale use.   In researching this set, I can’t begin to describe the abject horror and brutality of these weapons and the experiments used to test them.

—description from the publisher

Alien Frontiers: Factions expands the award-winning Alien Frontiers core game, including new Alien Tech cards to expand your technological arsenal, new Agenda cards that earn you Victory Points for completing hidden objectives, and individual Faction boards that give their owners an exclusive bonus as well as providing another facility that anyone can use...for a price.

The Definitive Edition combines the following products:

They believe that the greatest successes can only be achieved with a diversity of minds and talents. As such, the Angels provide bonuses for owners who have a variety of ships, making them especially useful during the crucial opening stages of the game.

5 different planes start to fly to the center of the board. Each player has is own plane in front of him/her. Shuffle the deck and deal 5 cards to each player. In his turn player may play any plane on the table near the same plane (to fly with this plane).

A touch-and-feel game where players take turns rolling the die and racing to "feed" their bait pieces to the hungry shark. The first player to run out of pieces wins.

Includes zippered plush shark, 16 sturdy bait pieces, die, and instructions for two ways to play.

Murder in a small town! Join a band of loyal friends seeking to uncover the real villain in the ghastly murder of the beloved Nurse Judy. This Homebrewed Cthulhu-esque board-game uses cooperative play on a 3d terrain map of a small New England town from the 1920's.

In this cooperative game, players move around the game board uncovering clues and revealing alibis for the members of the Town Council, all of whom are suspects of the murder. Work with the other players to deduce which Town Elders committed the murder, and reveal their hidden motive.

As players move around the board, they may draw cards from two decks, a Event deck, and a Town Deck. Event cards reflect general occurrences in the town, as well as the actions of the Villain as they attempt to hinder the investigation. The Town cards contain rumors and gossip that allows the players to discover who the real culprit and their secret motive through a process of elimination.

Players have the same number of colored cards. In turns, they must put a card over the discard pile, but when an "Atenti" card is played, the player must choose somebody, and he then must say which cards are in the discard deck, in the right order.
The winner is who plays first all his cards.

boardgame: Deal: American Dream – Hells Dogs MC
description:
A promotional 7th clan that was published as a stretch goal during the Kickstarter campaign.

From the publisher‘s website:

Founded in California by WW2 veterans, the Hells Dogs MC quickly spread all across the U.S. and Canada. They now count chapters in all the major cities of North America. These bikers tasted the joy of the 70s by being better at consuming the product than distributing it. Until they decided to use their fondness for immoderate violence to get in the business. Progressively, they began to contest the established monopoly of drug dealers in their cities, and soon emerged as some of the major players of drug trafficking in the continent. The Hells Dogs are a collegiate organization; meaning that their president is not omnipotent and that all the important decisions need to be voted unanimously by the inner circle.

These nomadic riders are present in all of North America; thus allowing the Hells Dogs to start their operations from Illinois, California as well as British Columbia.

After years of receiving and listening to our customers from around the world and compiling untold pages of feedback we have produced a full revision of our Global War rules, map and added a lot.


New units include:


·      Tank Destroyers

·      Self-Propelled Artillery

·      Heavy Battleships

·      Heavy Carriers

·      Fast Battleships

·      Battlecruisers

·      Light Cruisers

·      FEC Gurkhas


The revised map is very exciting.  We’ve continued to update place names and features to reflect historical accuracy.  To our map we have now printed air and naval bases, coastal artillery and fortifications onto the map directly.  You will also find new types of air bases as well as Major and Minor shipyards, Minor Ports, and Dockyards.   The railways now are designated as standard gauge (1,435mm) and broad gauge (1520mm and wider).   We’ve added deserts, and jungles, channel ports, and more!

We have also updated significantly the rules for convoy raiding and escorts and added many new rules, new technologies, and revised all the play aids and National Reference sheets for greater clarify and playability.   We have fixed some loop holes and added clarity and examples everywhere we could.  In short, this has been a community effort with people from many countries contributing their knowledge, time and experise for the love of the game.

Global War is, and will continue to be the most expanded game of its time with some 30 expansions and counting already produced we will soon add linking games to 1946 and 1914 with a flurry of expansions for each.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Squadron Strike: Romance of the Seven Realms
description:
The Lucasian Cluster balances on the knife edge of war. The Fisher Queen tries to rally the Seven Realms before the YENSIDI HEGEMONY grows too strong to resist.

Throwing the Hegemonic forces off her trail are the brave Knights Orbitale, piloting heirloom fighters from a bygone age, striking where the Hegemonic forces are weakest, moving on before reprisals target the innocent.

The Dark Lady K’thleen marshals her most feared lieutenants in a hunt from star to star for the Fisher Queen, leading fighter patrols beyond counting.

Numbers alone are no match for chivalry, or the mystical powers of “Élan”....

This product has box miniatures for space fighters, special rules for tailing in combat, Knightly Orders, and Élan powers. In addition to the Squadron Strike material, it also has a semi-cooperative game about the Fisher Queen’s campaign against the Hegemony!

“Do not try to do too much with your own hands. Better the Arabs do it tolerably than that you do it perfectly. It is their war, and you are to help them, not to win it for them. Actually, also, under the very odd conditions of Arabia, your practical work will not be as good as, perhaps, you think it is.”
T.E. Lawrence, Arab Bulletin, August 20, 1917

The Arab Spring that began in 2010 spread across the greater Middle East and North Africa. With little exception, it has not been resolved peacefully or resulted in greater democratic representation. Syria remains embroiled in a civil war that started in March 2011 and the immature Iraqi government was easily contested by the growth in Islamic State in Syria and Levant (ISIS).

Nothing is more horrific than the actions of ISIS, also known as DAESH (Dawla al-Islamiya al-Iraq al-Sham), and this game is not intended to glorify them in any way. ISIS is a radical extreme with megalomaniac goals that has fed off the turmoil and instability of this region inside the Fertile Crescent. Their actions fall into three broad categories: atrocities in Syria or Iraq, atrocities inspired by their radical ideas occurring outside the region, and other external ISIS planned and executed events—largely in Europe.
Western powers have attempted a multitude of approaches with each of the revolutionary uprisings but the global ambitions of ISIS placed them in direct confrontation. Of all the contestants, ISIS is the most aggressive, far reaching and extreme in their methods; ensuring that they cannot be ignored or contained without direct intervention.

—description from the publisher

Our climate is changing and the elements that prevail in the forest are unstable. We experience this by dealing with the effects of violent weather phenomena such as: drought, storm or heavy rainfall. We have no direct influence on them, but we can predict the effects they cause and try to prevent them. We can counteract some of these changes, others need to be adapted to.  The way we manage water and how we protect forests against fires is crucial in adapting forests to climate changes.  Keeping forests in good condition is your task now.
It is your decisions and your actions that determine whether you manage the elements of fire and water.
"Żywioły Lasu" is an original board game designed to be played individually among several people. The game balances the aspect of luck and the impact of player's decisions on the course of the game. Clear and intuitive rules allow you to quickly feel the vibe of the game regardless of age or experience with board games. Protect your forest from the effects of the forces of the elements, be ready for possible disasters and make sure the forest lasts longer for the benefit of people and nature.

In each round, players simultaneously pick the card with action which they will perform on the board and then pass the cards to the next player. The action is to place their marker in one of the forest zones: red, blue or neutral respectively.  At the end of the game, after an appropriate number of rounds, the players gain points for the advantages in the regions or the proper arrangement of their markers in relation to each other and the selected cards during the game. The winner is the player who collects the most points.

description:
RATH
Race Against The Horde

Goal: To be the first survivor of the zombie outbreak to complete 3 Survival Missions.
 Premise: You are one of the few surviving humans in a zombie infested world.  Your town, once full of beautiful trees and parks is now decaying and run down. There are a few groups of fellow survivors with base camps set up around the town.  Through your walkie-talkie a distress call is heard requesting that a supply run be done as soon as possible. You accept the task and start off on your journey. Along the way you come across floods, fires, zombie hordes, and military fall out.  At the end of the journey, you are the hero that helped save mankind from annihilation.
     
Game Board: Movement can only be done on spaces that have grid lines. Players cannot move through bushes and trees or walk on water. The buildings can be accessed by any space surrounding the border.  
Daily Update Cards:  The Daily Update cards effect game play in different areas of the board.  Hazards in this zombie infested city slow down a players’ travel time, move them back to their home base, or force detours from their original path.  Multiple grids on the map can be effected at once and each player must comply.
Survival Mission Cards:  Each player will have one Survival Mission at a time to fulfill.  Each player has a separate base camp of survivors they are trying to keep alive.  By completing the missions on the cards, each player saves human lives by delivering life sustaining goods such as food, water, weapons, or building supplies to repair dilapidated structures.
To Start: Shuffle Survival Missions cards and place on corresponding space on board. Shuffle the Daily Update cards and place on the corresponding space on board. Place each colored playing piece on the corresponding colored home base buildings on the map. This means each player will have a different starting position based on the color of their piece. 
Each player picks a random Survival Mission at the beginning of the game. The mission does not start until the player reaches the first building to pick up supplies and the mission does not end until the player reaches the destination to drop off supplies. Once complete, the player turns the card over and picks another Survival Mission to start on the next day/turn.  Once a player has completed 3 successful Survival Missions, they are the winner of the game.
Player Movement: Each player can move a maximum of 5 spaces per day (turn) unless specified on the Daily Update card.  Players can move horizontal, vertical, and diagonal. 
Game Play: One (1) Daily Update card is drawn per day (turn) and effects each player on the board for that day/turn only.   
The youngest player at the board is the first to draw a Daily Update card.  Each player moves based on the Daily Update card in a clockwise manner, starting with the card drawer first.  For example, player one reads the Daily Update card and is slowed from maximum movement of 5 spaces to 2 spaces. Their turn is over and now player two reads the same Daily Update card and makes their move.

Solo Una Noche is a luchador themed card game for 2-4 players. Featuring 1v1, tag team, and melee modes, this game plays in about 10 minuets per player. Simple math to calculate damage and cards that encourage narrative and excitement make this a great choice for everyone 9 and up. 
Solo Una Noche features turn based card play in which players compose attacks from their hand of cards to damage the other player(s). The defending player has the opportunity to respond. Damage is applied and then change turns. Play continues until one player can be pinned, submitted or knocked out. 
Solo Una Noche punches above it’s weight class as a great go to game for quick take that fun while you wait for your friends to finish that other long game or for everyone else to show up to game night.

boardgame: World in Flames Collector's Edition Super Deluxe
description:
It is the dawn of September 1939, and the dark clouds of war hover over the globe. China and Japan have already been at war for more than 2 years and now a Continental war is about to become a worldwide war, a World in Flames.

You, as leader of your nation, must guide your country through its darkest days, enhancing your economy, working with allies, building the units you need and then employing them effectively to defeat your manifold foes.

You are in charge of your country from 1939 until 1945 or until you conquer or fall. How you use your assets is up to you. Each country has its own special abilities and capabilities based on  your country's skills aptitude and leadership. And of course there is the all important field of diplomacy where how you negotiate the minefield of international relations can be the difference between victory and defeat.

Only one player can win, but you have to be on the winning side to be that player. So you want to help your allies JUST enough so your side wins but not so much that it is not you that gains all the glory you clearly deserve. Give my chums all support short of help is my war-winning strategy.

World in Flames shows the entire world and will be the world's biggest boardgame. That's right, for the first time ever all maps and displays will be hard-mounted boards with bound edges.

Furthermore all the counters are now die-cut not knife cut so no more flash and no more wrestling with the cardboard stem before it gives up its precious offspring counters.  The game is sumptuous, with some of the most beautiful counters ever printed. Each one lovingly hand-crafted and individually assessed and valued. The overall look of the game and counters is a work of art.

World in Flames can be played from 2 to 6 players (and can easily be played solitaire too). There are 20 campaigns you can play, from the hour long Victory in The West campaign to the 150 hour Global War mega campaign. World in Flames is the ultimate gaming challenge.

The World in Flames Collector's edition Super Deluxe Games' Set consists of the:

        World in Flames Collector’s Edition Deluxe game (CS 1-9, 18-22, 24 & 46-55)
        America in Flames game (CS 26-28);

        Khaki in Flames kit (CS 40-45), (only required for America in Flames and Patton in Flames, all World in Flames Collector's edition Commonwealth counters are now in Khaki);
        Free 2 year licence to download the World in Flames Collector's edition (Virtual) companion (which includes high quality images of all the counters, maps and rules graphics and text as well as all the technical data and production specs on all the combat aircraft and naval units of world war II), available now
        most recent 2 Annuals ~ currently Millenium WiF Annual (incl. CS 30, Politics in Flames) & 2008 WiF Annual (incl. CS 39, Factories in Flames);


for a total of 2 magazines, 4 games, 7 expansions, 1 virtual companion, 15 maps and 9240 counters (and a couple of pages of rules:)!

Roygbiv is a tavern at the end of the rainbow. As it is hard to reach, only wizards generally gather there. Whenever there is a dispute among wizards, there is - of course - only one way to resolve this: a magical fight to the death.

Team up with friends (or don't) and cast spells at each other until the first offending wizard has perished. Choose from 7 archetypes with each 12 unique and themed spells. Build your initial deck, and pick up new spells to stop enemy strategies or expand on your own, as you play.

Cast magic missiles, boost your allies' spells and suppress those of your opponents. Summon lightning totems, goats or obese wisps. Swamp your foes in dark magic or drain them from their life force. Gain spell power to increase your strength, or cower behind a barrier.

Start the game by choosing a wizard (this gives you a unique ability, and choice from a specific set of cards at the start). Then, pick your spell cards to assemble your deck. Take turns to gain mana, cast spells (paying that mana) and pick up new spells (unrestricted) to add to your deck. The first wizard to remove the last white "life" mana from another wizard's pool (effectively killing them), is the winner!

boardgame: The Partisans: An Extra Politics Board Game
description:
The Partisans is a tabletop game for 4-6 players based on the Extra Credits mini-series, Extra Politics. In the game, you play a political ideology attempting to move the government towards your goals. Of course, other players have different ideas about what the government should be doing.

Players take turns as the Committee Chair and work together to craft bills from amendment cards. All the players then vote on weather or not to pass the bill. VP are scored if players vote with the ideals of their faction and players gain extra power by voting with their lobbies.

When the game ends, a round of final scoring takes place based on the currant priorities of the government. The player with the most points is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Going Nuclear, Historical Board Gaming’s nuclear war expansion for Global War 2nd edition. This is the first of many 3D printed piece sets to supplement HBG’s Global War game. All of our 3D printed rule sets are denoted by the letters TDP with a letter after them to indicate the set (TDPA, TDPB etc.…) for easy reference in future rule sets.
In this expansion you’re going to learn how to build an atomic bomb. You and your opponents will be scavenging the globe for resources and constructing facilities in order to “GO NUCLEAR!” Also included in this set are rules to help Axis & Allies 1940 & Global 1939 players connect these rules to those game systems.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Global War 1936-1945: New Berlin – Fall of the Reich
description:
Welcome to New Berlin: The Fall of the Reich (FOR) expansion for Global War. In this expansion Germany has the option to relocate its capital after the capture of Berlin and stage a desperate last stand from Argentina.

Contents:
 1-Neu Berlin Marker

Marker

boardgame: Global War 1936-1945: The Elite of the Third Reich
description:
An exploration of Germany’s Elite SS units in World War II.

In this expansion we will present both a Basic and Advanced version to suit your style of play. In the basic version Germany can build up to 35 unique SS units of different types based on their historic availability. In the advanced version we allow you to follow the historical progression of each unit as they evolve to different types (infantry, mechanized, armor etc..) during the war.

Contents: (60 Markers)
1 ea. of SS Divisions 1-38
1 ea. of SS Paper Divisions: 39-45
6 SS Divs (Generic)
1 ea. of 500th & 600th SS Para Div.
1 ea. of 101, 102, & 103rd SS Hvy Tank
1 Z.v SS Div
1 ea. of 1st & 2nd SS Cossack Div

-description from publisher

Partisans were an integral part of World War II. These citizen soldiers took up arms in occupied territories, causing terror and mayhem for their oppressors, and ultimately helping in some cases to liberate their homelands. In this set you’ll be busy rolling for Partisans in captured territories, and trying to keep enemy Partisans from disrupting your conquests.

Set contents:
Partisan (US) x6
Partisan (UK) x6
Partisan (France) x6
Partisan (Germany) x6
Partisan (Japan) x6
Partisan (Italy) x6
Partisan (USSR) x10
Supply marker x6

-description from publisher

In this set collection programming card game, your old mentor has invited you to the unveiling of his masterpiece, a modular perpetual motion machine, only to disappear just as the event is about to begin! It is up to you, his former apprentices, to save the day and demonstrate this strange machine in his absence. Wow the audience, earn Prestige for your mentor, and discover the bizarre secrets of Doctor Smuglfreud's Marvelous Machine!

The premise is simple: module cards can interact with each other to generate a perpetual cycle of movements. There are many ways to get it right...but even more for things to go horribly awry! As you experiment with the temperamental machine, you will learn how to assemble sets of cards to make sensational combinations.

Three modes vary gameplay and win conditions:


     Rival Mode: compete with your rival to earn the most Prestige within the round limit.
     Demo Mode: work together or alone to earn the goal amount of Prestige (or more!) as quickly as possible.
     Puzzle Mode: work together or alone to create one spectacular design.


This game was first developed as an entry in the 2018 9-Card Nanogame Print and Play Design Contest, where it won several awards:


     #1 Most Innovative Mechanic
     #2 Most Thematic Game (Steampunk)
     #3 Best Artwork (Giampiero Randazzo)
     and more...


Features:

     Award-winning print-and-play game
     Art from the MARVELOUS Giampiero Randazzo
     Three game modes with distinct flavors and playstyles (competitive timed, cooperative/solo timed, cooperative/solo untimed)
     Ultra-portable components, including an optional custom tuck box
     Design codes for sharing configurations or asynchronous play
     Top hat not included


—description from the designer

description:
The Green Horde Crossover set brings all of the retail characters introduced in the Zombicide: Green Horde Kickstarter campaign into Massive Darkness. It contains 64 cards converting 17 Heroes, 5 Mobs, 2 Agents, 7 Roaming Monsters, and 3 Familiars to use them in Massive Darkness. As this is not a Kickstarter Exclusive item, it does not include any of the Kickstarter Exclusive characters from the Zombicide: Green Horde campaign, and those will not be converted to Massive Darkness.

Green Horde Crossover Contents:

Zombicide: Green Horde Core Box
         - 6 Heroes: Asim, Berin, Johannes, Megan, Rolf, Seli
         - 2 Mobs: Orc Walkers, Goblin Runners
         - 1 Agent: Orc Necromancer
         - 1 Roaming Monster: Orc Abomination

No Rest for the Wicked Expansion

         - 1 Roaming Monster: Necromantic Dragon

Friends and Foes

Adrian Smith Special Guest Box

Paul Bonner Special Guest Box

Sean A. Murray Special Guest Box (Include in all Horde pledges)

A football game with several air blowers that throws a polystyrene ball around. Launched as a counterpart to Exin-Basket Baloncesto. After the original publisher EXIN went bankrupt, the game was redesigned and launched by Inovac Rima SA, still as Exin Gol.

Who will scale up? Each player puts together a dragon by sliding cards into each other, one by one. The goal is to end up with the grandest and tallest dragon. Easy enough, if the dragon does not topple due to miscalculation of your own or an attack by another player. So beware of the spouts of fire, the lashes of tail or the trembling caused by the dragon dance! The player whose dragon overcomes the attacks and stands tallest when the cards run out wins.

The game is a simple building challenge. The cards are coasters, with slits in them, so you can slide them together and they'll hold fast fairly well - at least until the whole thing comes crashing down.

You won't lose the game (necessarily) if that happens - the game isn't over until all the cards are played. That's a nice change of pace from most dexterity games, where a crash is a loss.

—user summary

Cry Wolf is a fast simultaneous play bluffing micro game for two players. Each player either takes the role of the shepard boy or the wolf. The boy wins by crying wolf as the wolf attacks five times. The wolf is trying to sneak past the boy and eat five sheep.
This game also involves a hand management. Running out of cards as the boy means you've lost credibility and can not defend you flock for a turn. As the wolf running out of cards represents starving off.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Churrasco Carnival is a "who can eat the most" type of game, with a Tetris-like mechanism. Each turn, cards are drawn and players choose to eat or not, trying to fit the pieces of meat (coming in polyomino shapes) in their stomach. For each horizontal row completely filled, that food is digested, and the player gets points depending on what kind of food (meat) the row mostly consisted of. Special stomach sizes grant bonus points. The player with the most points after a certain amount of rounds wins.

The game is also playable as a solitaire endeavor, with you trying to reach the maximum number of points and beating the ghost player.

Who can reconstruct the raven-black scenes of the murderous script?

Total chaos in the film studios! In a burglary, everything was devastated and important scripts were stolen. The players are scriptwriters and have the task to rewrite the scripts. It helps that some scenes that are already shot, as well as the director.

The director draws a director's card and reads the script through. Only he knows the complete murderous script. He reads the screenplay and the introduction to his scriptwriters, puts on the first film scene and off you go! The scriptwriters have to guess with yes / no questions what happened here and what happens next - only then they get the next scene given by the director as a “reward”. If the scriptwriters have guessed all six scenes and the complete murderous script is shot, the next round starts and another player is allowed to play the director.

As a real rogue, you bag plenty of loot. So much that you have to stash it away in treasure troves. If only you could remember where the heck you have hidden it. 18 doors – and they all look identical. But where was that trap hidden again? Can you stay on top of things?

Go through all the treasure troves, one by one. As you go, each in turn says the number of the next treasure trove. Start with “1” and work your way through to “18”. It sounds easy enough, but be careful: some of the treasure troves have traps hidden in them. In that case, you don’t say the number, but must say a code word or make a secret sign instead. That’s the only way to escape the trap. Have your fellow rogues caught you making a mistake? Bad luck, now you collect penalty points. Have you caught one of the others? Good luck, then you receive points. At the end of the game, who is the one with the best memory and the most points?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Angel of Death and Infamous Butcher
description:
Two exclusive characters unlocked during the crowd-funding of the game's first printrun: Angel of Death and Infamous Butcher.

Includes four item cards (two of which are alt. art versions of cards included in the core box).

This is an expansion deck for Pythago. It is is a small deck of 36 thick cards showing new shapes for playing Pythago. The pack contains nothing else, no rules or variants, only 36 new cards.

Jenga, but under the tower you have a base with two handles. On your turn you will hold the base while doing the usual Jenga stuff, and at the end of the turn you pass the tower to the next player.

The game also comes with ten more or less silly extra challenges.

—user summary

Gem Clan grows in power as crystals are claimed. Reduce the cost of crystals with Crystal Seekers. Protect your allies with the Ruby Dancers' whose defenses increase for each crystal your opponent has scored. Use Gem Clan's special ability - Crystal Force to gain power for each crystal in any score area.

Raid and Pillage your foes with the Moon Clan a new clan deck for Crystal Clans. Moon Clan demoralizes its enemies by raiding their resources and invading their home zone. Muster an army of the dead with the Sunken Crew and their pirate ship the Risen Annabelle. Sneak past your opponent’s squads with the monstrous Deep Ones' Submerge ability. Use Moon Clans's special ability - Plunder to force your opponent to discard cards off their draw pile as you defeat their units.

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Super Mutants: Hammer
description:

A game for 2 players, the object of which is to win as many crowns as possible by having 3 stones of the same color leading to a crown vertically or diagonally.
One player takes 23 pink stones, the other player takes 23 green stones. The 5 crown cards are placed on the parapet spaces at the top of the board. Each stone that is placed must be supported by a stone beneath it.
When a player makes a line of 3 stones with one stone directly under a crown, he takes that crown and game continues until all stones have been played.

description:
The Missile program known as the “Aggregate Project”, was the German attempt to develop free flying Inter Continental Missiles (ICBM) capable of devastating cities or large concentrated forces through use of high explosive warhead delivery, and later, in the series, a small nuclear warhead. These missiles were to be based in planned occupied Greenland and captured locations in the Caribbean and South America should the United States be invaded, or, in later parts of the series launched from across the ocean itself from the homeland; a true Intercontinental Ballistic Missile.

-description from publisher

The following rules will help you incorporate these new units into your “AMERIKA” game. Feel free to pick and choose which ones to use so long as all players agree. These can help balance the game when players of differing skill levels play against each other, or add new dimensions and strategy for advanced players.

Through the course of any war, despite its destruction, there is also inevitable innovation. WW2 saw more technological advancement in weaponry than any other war throughout history. The units in this expansion provide the means to bring those advancements to the front lines of Amerika.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Amerika: Cruise Missile Attack – Surface Missiles In Amerika
description:
The following rules will help you incorporate these new units into your AMERIKA game. Feel free to pick and choose which ones to use so long as all players agree. These can help balance the game when players of differing skill levels play against each other, or add new dimensions and strategy for advanced players.

German rocket technology began early on as a means to defend the coasts of Fortress Europe and cause fear or destruction from long range. Learning from the Nazi V-1 program, the U.S. began development of the JB-2 Loon, a near copy of the feared V-1. In Amerika, these technologies have become battle tested by both sides by 1946 and are ready for deployment to the battlefield.

This expansion allows players a new type of weapon on both sides of the battlefield.

Helping both forces to disrupt enemy actions, cruise missiles are used during the Conduct Guerilla Attacks phase.

-description from publisher

The following rules will help you incorporate these new units into your “AMERIKA” game. Feel free to pick and choose which ones to use so long as all players agree. These can help balance the game when players of differing skill levels play against each other, or add new dimensions and strategy for advanced players.

Very few aircraft carrier designs in Imperial Japan or The Third Reich had the capability to launch and recover jet powered aircraft. Any carriers that were unable to do so were relegated to defending the fleets of the Axis navies late in the war. The Japanese had their immense Taiho Kai class carriers, and the Germans developed a fleet of their technologically advanced Grossflugzeugkreuzers. Both nations had their vessels ready for action prior to the imminent invasion of Amerika.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Amerika: Taiho Kai – Great Phoenix of the Ocean

The German army was known for big tanks and big guns. But this incredible mortar redefined “siege cannon”. Used for many years against European and Russian opponents, this fearsome gun is ready for export to the American Front.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Amerika: Defcon 1 – Atomic Defense in Amerika
description:
The following rules will help you incorporate these new units into your AMERIKA game. Feel free to pick and choose which ones to use so long as all players agree. These can help balance the game when players of differing skill levels play against each other, or add new dimensions and strategy for advanced players.

The Axis were aware for some time that the Allies had heavily invested in making atomic energy work as a weapon in hopes of delivering a crippling blow and ending the war. Keeping the Allies from developing this technology into a full-fledged long-range weapon is what Amerika is all about! This expansion set allows the Allies to use whatever nuclear technology they can muster to help defend them while they attempt to finish their work in the wake of an invasion.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Amerika: Ein Grosse Ratte – The P.1000 Ratte In Amerika
description:
The following rules will help you incorporate this new unit into your AMERIKA game. Feel free to pick and choose which ones to use so long as all players agree. These can help balance the game when players of differing skill levels play against each other, or add new dimensions and strategy for advanced players.

It is widely known that Adolf Hitler was a huge proponent of Wonder Weapons and felt they would win the War for Nazi Germany. And while we can see his “bigger is better” philosophy come through in such designs as the Maus tanks and Amerika bombers, it is a far lesser known fact just how large his aspirations were when it came to super-sized weaponry. Weighing in at 1,000 tons, this behemoth was designed to be a mobile platform for a pair of naval cannons accompanied by a super-heavy anti-tank gun as a secondary weapon, complete with Flak guns for its own air defense.

-description from publisher

The following rules will help you incorporate this new unit into your AMERIKA game. Feel free to pick and choose which ones to use so long as all players agree. These can help balance the game when players of differing skill levels play against each other, or add new dimensions and strategy for advanced players.

Another of the mega-sized wonder weapons from the mind of Adolf Hitler, the P.1500 takes the idea of the P.1000 a step further. Replacing the naval artillery from the P.1000 with the 800mm Gustav artillery gun, this unit is more like a jumbo version of the Karl Morser, able to strike targets 20+ miles away – well outside enemy engagement range.

Weighing in at 1,500 tons, this giant was developed to give new life and mobility to an otherwise restricted railway gun. Using 4 submarine engines to position itself, it also housed 2 howitzers for fire support and a host of 15mm autocannons for defense.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Amerika: Target Amerika – The Bomber Projects
description:
Hitler’s fascination of New York City in flames drove Germany’s leading aircraft engineers to begin development of several options for bombers that could strike targets on American soil from European air bases. In 1942, the Amerika Bomber Project was born which produced several contenders vying for the chance to make Hitler’s dream come true. In Amerika, not only was the project very successful, but by 1946 heavily funded to aid in the air supremacy war over the Atlantic. The Horten XVIII B-2 (from the base game) became the best all-around platform of the project, and was the mainstay of Nazi bomber supremacy. The Arado E-555 and Messerschmitt Me-264 (from the expansion Bring In The Heavies) were both very competent designs as well, helping to soften defenses and destroy Allied installations.
But several other designs from the project were also produced in limited numbers to round out the air force and provide mission flexibility. When the decision was made to invade North America, every resource available had to be called upon for a chance at success to even be possible. Help turn the tide of war in Germany’s favor using these Amerika Bomber Project aircraft in AMERIKA!

-description from publisher

The Sänger Silbervogel “Silverbird” bomber developed from the idea of an intercontinental bomber that would glide around the world on the fringes of space and the Earth’s atmosphere. Launched from a base in Nazi Occupied Europe, it would travel down an approximately three-mile long monorail track, launched initially by V-2 rocket engines into space at a speed so fast, it would circle the earth in only a few hours. Its payload was to be a single composite bomb containing nuclear particles in a fashion similar to “chaff”, with an initial detonation in the higher atmosphere above a city to ensure complete coverage of the nuclear material.
The bomb itself was a 5,000-pound high explosive warhead wrapped in special insulated blankets of radioactive silicone, detonating at about 2,000-3,000 feet above the target city. The end result would be radioactive sickness and death for those on the receiving end.
After mission completion, the bomber would glide back to Germany reentering the atmosphere gradually and landing at a specially designated airfield. The cost of producing the bomb and the bomber itself though would render this weapon as a relatively expensive endeavor suited mostly for preemptive strikes against heavily fortified cities with the aim to wipe them out quickly.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Amerika: Allied UFO – The YB-35 Flying Wing In Amerika
description:
Jack Northrop saw the flying wing design as the pinnacle of efficiency in aeronautical engineering. In theory, it would be capable of faster speeds, larger bomb loads, and longer range, expanding the known performance attributes of 1940’s bomber technology.

In Amerika, the Germans have long won the race to flying wing technology and have used it to the detriment of Allied defenses. But now, the Allies will strike back with their own deadly version. Focused more on payload than radar evasion, the YB-35 can deliver a heavy punch at an affordable price.

-description from publisher

Touting the war’s largest payload capability of 88,000lbs, this flying behemoth is best known for its special weapon, the T-12 Cloudmaker – a 44,000lb bomb designed to hit the ground with enough force to have an earthquake like effect! Able to carry two at a time, there is no Axis unit safe from the B-36D in Amerika

-description from publisher

german a-Series Rockets were initially developed as a retaliatory weapon to cause fear and disruption deep in Allied territory. These advanced versions have been designed to turn the tide of battle for an advancing Nazi army.

A-Series Rockets are used preemptively in German attacks. They move (are fired) during movement, and the effects of these attacks are resolved before units are moved to the battle board during the combat resolution phase. To purchase and build these rockets, the German player must have built the previous iteration at least one turn prior. For example, to build the A-12, the Germans must have already built at least one A-11 on a previous turn, one A-10 before that, and so on. If using the V-1 rules, V-2s and A-4s are considered beginning technology and do not require V-1s to be built the prior turn. V-1, V-2 and A-4 are all available during the initial buy purchase and on Turn 1. Rockets are placed in the air support box during the Place Reinforcements phase and can be stockpiled and used as the player wishes.

-description from publisher

Designed to carry attack aircraft in secret across the Pacific Ocean, the I-400 class was the only operational submarine carrier in the world. Each could carry (3) M6A1 Seiran bombers and still hunt for prey after launch. Built in production quantities during the fall of the U.S. Pacific fleets, these will terrorize the West Coast like never before in Amerika.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Amerika: Projekt Ursel Type XXI U-boat
description:
A feat of German engineering, the Type XXI had improved speed and large capacity batteries, allowing it to travel farther and longer without the need to surface. These characteristics made it the perfect platform for Projekt Ursel, a plan to add rockets to the sub and included a guidance system for targeting. With tested accuracy after the Atlantic Campaign, these will terrorize the East Coast like never before in Amerika.

-description from publisher

Historical Board Gaming is proud to present Meltdown 2025 - War in the 21st Century!  
A severe economic recession among oil producers and manufacturing nations has created instability and fueled ethnic tensions in Russia, China and the Middle East.  In a bid to regain control of their economies, China and Russia are about to launch an invasion of unprecedented scale to gobble up markets and raw materials and cripple the West’s ability to intervene.  To this end, Russia has been funneling money and weapons to extremist groups in the Middle East.  There, a new group of radicals, fueled by foreign money now dominates the region, and parts of Africa.   Against this plan for world dominance stands the USA, The European nations (EU and NATO states), and a coalition of Pacific and Indian allies.  The dogs of war are about to be unleashed….

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Russian Super Weapons (RSW) brings you five new heavy-hitting units for the USSR. This set contains all you need to smash fascism once and for all and claim a Global War victory; The MiG-9 fighter, 4302 Rocket-powered interceptor, IS-7 heavy tank, SU-101 tank destroyer, and a Soyuz Class battleship! Rules for developing and using these units Global War 1936-1945 are provided. While many of these units did not enter real-life service until after the Second World War, players can now use the technology development system to add them to their arsenals during the game. Go to HBG Shapeways store to print out the number of models you would like for your game.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Global War 1936-1945: Battle for the Atlantic – The Allies Strike Back
description:
In this Global War 1936-1945 expansion the Allies get a host of new units to fight against German Uboats. Many of the units in this set are exclusively designed to assist in ASW operations – a good counter to all those submarines in our last set! The B-24 Liberator can get out into the Atlantic Gap zones (represented by SZ 21-23 and 31-32). The CAM ship is great for defending against the FW Condor, and the DDE is a low-cost escort option instead of sticking your Destroyers on patrol duty. The Sub-chaser and K-Class blimp are good for inshore duty around the British Isles and Gibraltar.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Global War 1936-1945: Battle for the Atlantic – The German Menace
description:
In this Global War 1936-1945 expansion Germany finds itself with Six new units with one sinister purpose - to disrupt and destroy allied shipping in the Atlantic. In this set you’ll find two new U-boat types, merchant raiders, Milch Cows, and FW Condors – enough to sink more allied tonnage than ever. But don’t worry allies, Battle for the Atlantic: The Allies Fight Back will feature units to combat this dire threat!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Global War 1936-1945: Elite of the Soviet Union
description:
OVERVIEW
Historical Board Gaming is excited to present the Elite of the Soviet Union © expansion. An expansion involving the Soviet Guards Units (Guards)  and the ‘Order of the Red Banner’ in World War II.

SET CONTENTS
-30 Guards Division Markers
-10 Order of the Red Banner Markers
-1 For The Capture Of Berlin Marker
-2 Guards Bomber Regiment Markers
-2 3D printed Po-2 night bombers

1.0 GUARDS

1.1 Overview
The USSR player can award Guards status to units that distinguished themselves in battle to boost moral and create elite units. Over 120 infantry divisions alone were given Guards status during the war.
Guards units were given better and more weapons, ammunition and vehicles than regular divisions. Guards divisions were sometimes reformed for various reasons. Some Guards units knew multiple formations during the war. These promotion rules replace the Soviet Union’s Guards Infantry rules on the Soviet Union’s National Reference Sheet in the Global War 1936 rules.

-description from publisher

Historical Board Gaming is proud to present The Great War (Global War 1914-1918) - the ultimate World War I strategic game.   If you’ve played our Global War series, Axis & Allies or similar games, this game will seem quite familiar to you as basic mechanics and turn structure of the game are similar.   However, there are many changes and new features that make this game unique to the era.

For starters, The Great War is a strategic global (not a euro-centric) examination of the war.  The strategic concerns of many competing powers across the globe have been factored in to create a game that can have long-lasting ramifications.

—description from the publisher
The Missiles of October have triggered a Global War between the US and Russia as well as their Allies. Staying in the flavor of the Global War games, this Cold War gone hot will not let you down.

FateShifters (轉命師) is a card game that combines elements from astrology, astral myth, and tarot to compose several different game rules to suit players' gaming preferences, including storytelling, area control, trick-taking, etc.

Tavernin is a Poker-like game in which players attempt to build the highest valued party and bet against the other players using custom chips. The player with the highest valued hand collects the chips.

Players start with 5 cards and use "uno" like rules to connect the cards into a party. Each card has a banner at its center that asks a requirement to be met before it adds value to your party.

There are 2 different types of value that your party accrues. Levels which are the main tier of value, and Power which are secondary but still important.

EX: The Warrior 4 card asks that you have 2 matching color cards in your party. If you meet the requirement the card grants 4 Power to your party’s total value.

A traditional round goes:


     All players Ante or big/small blinds (Preference)
     All players dealt 5 cards
     Initial round of betting occurs.
     Discard phase.
     Final round of betting occurs.
     Call.


—description from the designer

boardgame: シスターズチェイン (Sisters Chain)
description:
Publisher's summary
シスターズチェイン (Sisters Chain) is a Japanese game of tile placement, published in Japanese an English.

In Sisters Chain, the players race to the goal by linking character tiles according to simple conditions. If you have pairs of the same or sibling character tiles in your hand, you can play them at once.

You can also take the tiles placed by your opponents and aim for a come-from-behind victory by continuously placing tiles in sequence. This simple and tactical board game can be played from 2 to 6 players is recommended for all ages.

The game is themed on Touhou Project characters, but you can play the game without such knowledge.

タイルに描かれたキャラクター達を簡単な条件に合わせて繋いでいき、相手より早くゴールに到達することを目指すゲームです。
手札に同じキャラクターや姉妹同士のキャラクターがあれば一気に使うことができます。
また、相手が置いたタイルを取ることもでき、場合によっては連鎖的に置き続けることができるなど、逆転を狙うこともできます。
年齢問わずシンプルだけど油断はできないボードゲームとなっております。１対１の真剣勝負から最大６人でワイワイ遊ぶことも可能です。
東方Project作品の二次創作ですが、原作を知らなくてもプレイ可能です。

boardgame: こんなブラックな魔王様はイヤだっ!! (Konnna Black na Maou Sama wa Iya Da!!)
description:
こんなブラックな魔王様はイヤだっ!! (Konnna Black na Maou Sama wa Iya Da!!) is a Japanese deck-building game. In English, the title translates as "I Can't Stand Such Evil Satan!!". But the publisher offers "Scout Monsters" instead (although this is not on the published edition). The game can be played as a solitaire as well as a 2-4 player game.

Publisher's summary
Scout Monsters is a deck-building card game whereby each player chooses their heroes and spells and fight to defeat monsters or have them join your team before they reach the final showdown against Satan.

Unlike many deck-building games, the cards are not divided into "treasure" and "point" cards, making it more beginner-friendly. On the other hand, Satan's Attack card, designed to damage all the players' decks, promotes competition with skills and tactics between skilled players.

In the 2- to 4-player games, the players race to defeat Satan. In the solo game, the player fights directly against Satan.

「こんなブラックな魔王様はイヤだっ！！」は、プレイヤーごとに英雄と呪文を選んでデッキを持ち、迫り来るモンスターを倒したり仲間にしたりしながら、最終的に魔王を倒す、デッキ構築型カードゲームです。
一般的なデッキ構築型ゲームと違い、いわゆる""金""と""点数""に分かれていないので、初めての人も遊びやすくなっている点と、全プレイヤーのデッキにダメージを与えようとする「魔王の攻撃」カードがあり、慣れた人同士も技術や駆け引きで競い合える点が特徴です。
より早く魔王を倒す２〜４人用ルールと、１人で魔王と直接戦うシングルルールがあります。

-From The Designer Page
Sarimaw and his Monsters are attacking Sansinukob! You will need to call on the help of the Bagani to defeat the Monsters and save the "Sansinukob" (World).

In Bagani: the Card Game, you will form Combinations of Bagani cards with Lakas (Power), Ganda (Beauty), Lakam (Nobility), Dumakulem, and Mayari. Use the different combinations to defeat the Monsters of Sarimaw. For each Monster you defeat, you will get Dangal (Honor) Points. The player with the most Honor at the end of the game is the winner and savior of Sansinukob!

"Lahat tayo may pinagdadaanan.
Lahat tayo may pinaglalaban.
Lahat may hugot.
Para sa inyo'to."
-Words seen at the box cover (In Filipino)

The card game is pretty straight-forward and takes the mechanics of other familiar voting-party games. The "Hugot Master"(active player) draws a card from the red hugot card pile, which contains specific scenarios such as “describe mo ang crush mo” (describe your crush). The other players would then pick a black hugot card, which contains a single word, from their hand and make a hugot out of it. The one with the best hugot chosen by the Hugot Master wins the round and keeps the red hugot card. The first to gain five red hugot card wins the game.

—user summary

boardgame: Crazier Eights: One Thousand & One Nights
description:
A gateway fantasy card game. This is a stand alone version of Crazier Eights with fifty two cards and an Arabian Nights theme. Every card has a unique ability and the first player with zero cards in hand wins.

Players take turns drawing a card, playing up to one card, and discarding up to one card.

This version can be mixed with other versions of Crazier Eights printed in 2017 or after.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
GAME SYSTEM

    
    
         This entry is to allow for discussion/rating of the game system as a whole. It is not for a specific product or release. Versions will appear on the individual item pages.

    


Luminous Ages: Dragon Dreams is a versatile living card game. Where the goal is to Summon a Dream God (normally a dragon). To do this you have to slowly build up energy while protecting your armies and lands and attacking your opponents.

It comes as four premade decks however players are also welcome to mix cards and create their own personalised decks. The premade decks are designed to be powerful but balanced, giving no true advantage to personalised decks but instead focusing on skill.

—description from the publisher

"Correctly making inferences will help you blast down the mountain to win."
An educational game in which players will:

     make inferences
     understand and analyze text
     read for meaning


(Reading Comprehension: Level 2)

Players read 'question cards' with a short text and answer the accompanying question based on the text. Correctly answering the question allows them to roll the die and move down the ski hill to a new square. Specially marked squares along the way cause special events, such as losing a turn or helping another player.  The first one to the bottom wins.

—user summary

Hildarting is a strategic game of area control settled in the Faroer Islands during the Viking Age.

The board represents the islands and regions of the Faroer arcipelago. Each player starts with a number of regions under their control, and the goal of the game is to conquer other regions. In the end, only one player will remain to be the ruler of the Faroer. The mechanics and the game goal resemble very much the classic Risk.

In turns, players move region to region by sea or by land. Entering other players' regions causes conflict, and the winner of the battle claims the region.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Honey Pot Hop: A Musical Party Game
description:
This game features two unique games, each utilizing a mechanical, talking Winnie the Pooh figure.

Set-up for both games is the same: place Pooh in the centre of the floor. Arrange the six character circles in a large circle around Pooh. All players start on a different character circle.

Bee Bop

Place the Bee Tokens next to Pooh, then push the honey bee button on Pooh once to start the game, and then once more to select the Bee Bop game.

Pooh will then give a direction (such as "bounce like Tigger") and begin playing music. All players must move around the circle, following the direction given by Pooh. When the music stops, each player must stand on a different character circle. Pooh will then name one of the six characters. If a player is standing on that character circle, they collect a Bee Token, and a new round begins.

The game ends when one player has collected 3 Bee Tokens OR when Pooh ends the game, at which point the player with the most Bee Tokens wins.

Honey Hop

Push the honey bee button on Pooh once to start the game, and then twice more to select the Bee Bop game.

Pooh will then give a direction (such as "scratch your head like Rabbit") and then begin playing music. All players must move around the circle, following the direction given by Pooh. When the music stop, all players stop and listen to Pooh. Pooh will then name characters, equal to the number of players minus one. So if there are 3 players, Pooh will name off two characters. Players run to try and stand on one of the character circles named by Pooh. The player who is not standing on one of the characters named is eliminated.

Remaining players continue playing another round. This time Pooh will name one less character. Players continue playing until only one player has not been eliminated at the end of a round.

—user summary

If you're like us, you have too many miniatures and too little time to learn every game out there. Never fear, the Gangfight Skirmish Game System is here! With our all new hardcover rulebook, you will learn how to play games in any setting, with any miniatures you want! Perhaps you'd like to see if a Warjack can kill a Dreadnought? Or maybe you'd like to create a heroic warband with fantasy characters from 4 different game systems? All of this is possible with the Gangfight Skirmish Game System!

The Gangfight Skirmish Game System features unique characters, alternating activations, ongoing campaigns, fast & fun gameplay, lots of skills, custom equipment and more!

—description from the publisher

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
SEVEN is a tile-stacking game that uses polyhexes of 4 hexagons, called tetrahexes. There are 7 different tetrahexes (hence the name of the game).

Players alternate turns placing one of their tiles according to some simple rules, trying to get the most of their pieces on the topmost layers when the game ends.

The rules also include a variant and some puzzles.

This stand alone expansion contains the same components as the base game, but in red and blue:

- 7 red tetrahexes
- 7 blue tetrahexes
- cotton case

As a standalone product, it allows 2 player games, just like the base game.

As an expansion, it allows two types of gameplay:

- 3 player
- 2 vs 2 players; with pairs sitting in front of each other.

The game has been proven to be non-suitable for 4 player games (too chaotic), hence the 2 vs 2 game mode.

Twigs is a tile-laying game that uses polytwigs of 5 segments called ‘pentatwigs’. There are 12 different pentatwigs (discarding symmetries).

Players alternate turns placing one of their pentatwigs according to some simple rules, trying to enclose as many areas as possible.

This stand alone expansion contains the same components as the base game, but in red and blue:

- 12 red pentatwigs
- 12 blue pentatwigs
- 15 red discs
- 15 blue discs
- cotton case

As a standalone game (being exactly as the base game) it allows for 2 player games.

As an expansion, it allows 3 and 4 player games.

-description from publisher

"The animals in the forest are small and shy, they only play with an animal of the same age or their family. "

Forestia is a card game of discarding from 3 years.
The first player to discard all his cards wins a token.
The goal of the game is to be the first to win the number of chips expected early in the game.

     2 chips: for the youngest, and games with a large number of participants
     3 chips: for the most adventurous players.


The duration of a game is less than 5 minutes, which corresponds to the concentration capacity of a 3-year-old child.

Forestia is a fun way to learn how to count and count from 1 to 3. It is an intellectually complex task for a child to count, not recite 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. This game allows you to play with the colors, then gently bring the child to count the candles on the cards to learn how to count while having fun. Then the first tactics with the first special card: the Joker card.

Forestia is an evolving game with the addition of special cards as the child evolves. At 6 or 7 years old, the child can play with all the cards.

Note: it is possible to play up to 12 players with 2 boxes of Forestia.

The ergonomics of the cards have been studied to allow to play with right-handers as left-handed. In addition each color is associated with only 1 symbol, which makes it easy to play with people with visual impairment.

—description from the publisher

Vilar de Mouros 2018 is an expansion for Vilar de Mouros with 8 new cards (7 game cards and 1 bonus card) and a note pad to add the points.
Two of the new game cards (Pretenders and Peter Murphy) are upgrades, so we recommend that you play without the original ones.
If you want to keep them, we recommend that the 2 cards from the same band cannot be held by the same player at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tiny Epic Defenders (Second Edition): Kickstarter Deluxe Promo Pack
description:
This promo pack includes 5 new defenders: The Sullied (Human Necromancer), Brutus (Goblin Assassin), Gamelyn (Guardian of the Order), Kyrie (Valkyrie Avenger) and Methusael (Undead Revenant).

It also includes 2 new artifacts cards and items: Baal's Bait and Gavel of Gamelyn.
Also added are 2 epic foes: Overlord and Fire Elemental and 2 dire enemies: Giant Snake and Basilisk.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Defenders: The Dark War – Kickstarter Deluxe Promo Pack
description: This Kickstarter semi-exclusive pack includes a box sleeve to hold the expansion and base game together. The pack adds 3 new defenders: Conjurer, Armorer, and Mechanic. It also includes cards and items for 2 new artifacts: Aughmoore's Gamble and Skull of Baal. There are 4 new epic foes: Giant Mantis, Ghost Ship, Grotesque, and Vampire Lord (Ice Golem was originally slated to be included, but is in the base game instead), along with 4 new dire enemies: Mimic, Sirens, Nomads, and Wolfrats.

Rezonite is a two player duel where you battle using cards that represent mystical crystals. The goal of the game is to be the first player to reach a victory condition, which are printed on certain cards. This means you must cast a spell that has a victory condition and then use your other spells to help you achieve that condition, while preventing your opponent from achieving theirs first.

You get three willpower on your turn, which can be spent like action points to do things like draw and play cards. Spells are played face down, and remain that way until you manifest them. This gives you plenty of opportunities to bluff and outwit your opponent.

Every card in your deck is unique and every card can be played as either a spell or as energy. The choices you make about which cards to play as energy and which ones to play as spells can have a massive impact on the outcome of the game.

The core set comes with 150 cards, divided into 5 preconstructed decks. You can play with any of these decks, or build your own deck by combining any 30 Rezonite cards. There are no restrictions on which cards can go together, but think carefully about which energy types and victory conditions to include in your Rezonite deck.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Above and Below: Megaland Bonus Cards
description:
A four pack of cards for Above and Below included with Megaland, hidden under the insert.

Shuffle the new key card with the other key cards, and the new star cards with the other star cards. Set up as normal.

An Adventure Battle Game

Build / Quest / Conquer

Your Squad + An Epic Game = Memories that Last

The Rangers: Rise of War is meant to be played as a 4-6 hour epic strategy game, or however long you all want to keep it going! Sure, we have rules to make the gameplay quicker, but this is supposed to bring friends together and battle for the ultimate victory.

Players control an army that has a specific alley in play. Players can choose to focus on their Heroes or begin to diversify their armies by upgrading buildings unlocking more strategic pieces, such as Archers or Calvary.

You may advance out capturing areas of the board that translates into coin and gives you a spell card or keep your hero in place and quest for epic gear and more.

There are many goals to win the game. If you are able to get both swords of power into your capital, bring 4 breeds to your capital or go for complete annihilation by taking out both of your enemy’s main capitals to win. Micro-games may have other added ways to win in order to condense the game time. Micro-games may also start with buildings already in place.

The game is set in the world of Adrasil. The Rangers has had several films projects as seen on The Fantasy Network, related to it as well as a comic, and a live-action event called "Weekend Warrior”.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Realms: Pact Cutter Promo Card
description: Star Realms: Pact Cutter Promo Card is a Blob/Trade Federation Ship promo card for use with Star Realms.
Baseball simulation with manually-operated, mechanical pitching and batting.

A board game based off of the popular app of the same name.

Answer questions about US history, sports, science, geography, art, and entertainment. The first player to collect all 6 character tokens wins.

On a player's turn, they spin a spinner and answer a question from the category on which the spinner lands (ie: lands on Science, the player is asked a question from the top card of the Science deck).

If the player is correct, they move their game piece one space t the right and they spin again to attempt to answer another question.  If their answer was incorrect, their turn is over.

When a player reaches a crown space on the game board or spins the Crown on the spinner, that player has a chance to earn a character token from a chosen category.  If they are correct, they receive the token and return to the beginning space and make their way back to the crown, otherwise they simply return to the beginning space without the token.

Hey! Those Are My Glasses is a children's shape matching game. Each player starts with a lemur face in front of them and the goal is to collect the most shape-based glasses cards. A player flips the top glasses card and then rolls the die. If the shape of the die matches the glasses on the card, they get to keep it.  If not it stays on the table and the die is passed to the next player.  If on their turn a player rolls a shape that matches one or more of the glasses on the tables, they get to collect all of the matches.  Player with the most glasses cards at the end of the game is the winner!

-description from designer

Your tank rumbles into the bombed out ruins of a small town, a forest of jagged walls and rubble. Once a bustling town on a crossroads, it is now used as passage for supply trucks heading to the front line. Your mission is to hold this strategic location at any cost. But you’re not the only one with this mission. Other rival forces have sent their own armored units to secure the ruins. As a tank commander you’ll need to use your superior skills to win the day!

Tank Battles is an easy-to-learn, fast-paced game of tank combat with a surprising amount of tactical depth despite its simplicity. Take command of your battle tank(s) by moving and shooting each turn. Make difficult tactical decisions to stay alive and destroy enemy tanks. To win you’ll need to have the last tank left standing!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fields of Arle: New Travel Destination – Eckernförde
description: Feuerland started a set of new travel destinations. You can get them in the towns printed on the travel destination

boardgame: Fields of Arle: New Travel Destination – Neumünster
description: Feuerland started a set of new travel destinations. You can get them in the towns printed on the travel destination

Cardgame Published by Foundation "Natuur&Milieu"

Light game that involves building menu's with different ingredients, based on the ecological footprint of the ingredients.

Players add ingredients (ranging in ecological footprint from -5 to 14 points) to a dish (set of cards) until the dish contains exactly 21 points of ingredients. The last player to add an ingredient wins the set.
To make the game more varied, some action cards are included that lets players start extra dishes, restrict the use of certain ingredients etc.
The player to finish the most dishes wins.

—user summary

The British Invasion is a Vinyl band expansion that builds on the base game of Vinyl and introduces the Listening Booth and singles (aka 45s). Historically, 45s were purchased to initially and eventually encouraged buyers to acquire the associated LP album or even another album from the band. In BI, players can acquire 45s by matching the band icon. Each 45 has an "A-side" with locked attributes that can be unlocked at the Listening Booth. At the Listening Booth, players will discard a magazine card with one matching icon to flip the 45 to its "B-side" giving the player more points and the opportunity to use the 45 to acquire LPs with similar attributes in future rounds. BI leverages the same collection rewards system (Pure, Mixed, Double Mixed) that comes in the base game and can be further integrated with Top Shelf, Publisher Expansion, Clearance Bin, or even the memorabilia from Masters of Metal!

For 6 the new bands (Half & Half, Lead Balloon, Scarabs, The What, Rolling Bones, Pink Fluid), the following is included in this expansion:

     66 Magazine Cards
     72 LP Album Cards
     24 45s Cards


—description from the publisher

A free Print&Play game, simple to build and easy to carry around.

My Little Castle is a Tile Placement Puzzle, playable Solo, Couple or by 3-4 players.

Your aim is to build the best fief, full of Towers and with a mighty Castle. You do so by picking diamond tiles packed with villages, livestock, woods and fields.

When your domain outline a triangle-shaped void, that's a building site for a Tower. Outlining a hexagon you get to build the Castle.

Laying tiles it's crafty and competitive, get yourself a plan!

Game files can be found on the website for download.

-description by the designer

Up to 4 players can take part in the game. Each player receives one token and must throw a "6" on the dice to be able to start. If a "6" is thrown during the game the player is allowed to have another turn. "Throwing" the dice is done by means of a short, sharp tap on the knob of the dice box. If the figure reaches a number field, there is a special event (according to the game instructions).

—user summary
Nusfjord: Plaice Deck contains 45 new cards for use with the Nusfjord base game. Also comes with metal coins, most useful to replace the coins from the first edition that were printed too small.

boardgame: Caverna: Cave vs Cave – Era II
description:
In the two-player game Caverna: Cave vs. Cave, each player starts the game with only two dwarves and a small excavation in the side of a mountain. Over the course of eight rounds, they'll double their workforce, open up new living space in the mountain, construct new buildings and rooms in which to live, and dig for precious metals.

In Caverna: Cave vs Cave – Era II – The Iron Age, the players start exploring a side cave abundant in ore. Donkeys help you move the ore to the surface so that you can cast iron and forge weapons out of it — to protect your cave from anyone who wishes it harm, of course.

In the meantime, your tribe has grown four primates capable of work. Keep on collecting grains, fibers, and building resources to increase your wealth. Shortly you will be engaging in agriculture...

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Who wants to be an Adventurer? Everyone! That’s why you’re willing to do anything, including sabotage your rivals, to be the best. You might conquer the Dragon’s Hoard or curse your enemies with Gorthak’s Tremulous Bowel. You might become a Boss-Killer or get Kicked in the Head by a Pissy Unicorn. All’s fair in love and adventuring!

Thou Sucketh is a hilarious fantasy-themed deck-building game, in which you take on the role of an aspiring adventurer. You not only buy awesome cards for yourself, you buy detrimental cards to put in your opponents’ decks! They can make life complicated, but in a funny way.

Game play is easy to learn. You generate resources from your hand, purchase cards for yourself or another’s deck, and compete for the most victory points in your deck at the end of the game. If you win, shout “Verily, I Ruleth!”

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – Daggers in the Dark
description:
Defend the Night's Watch with the Daggers in the Dark Chapter Pack for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game! The sixth and final entry in the "Dance of Shadows" cycle, this pack features the return of the Shadow keyword from the game's first edition to create new ways to conspire, as well as iconic characters and events that fully embrace the underhanded shadowplay!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – In Daznak's Pit
description:
Enter the fighting pits with the In Daznak's Pit Chapter Pack for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game! The fifth entry in the "Dance of Shadows" cycle, this pack features the return of the Shadow keyword from the game's first edition to create new ways to lay your traps, as well as powerful cards to counter your enemy's conspiracies!

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Emperor Palpatine Commander Expansion
description:
Sitting at the head of the mighty Galactic Empire, Emperor Palpatine is the supreme authority in the galaxy. A brilliant manipulator who almost singlehandedly orchestrated the fall of the Jedi order and his own rise to power, the Emperor makes a perfect addition to Star Wars: Legion as a commander of your Imperial forces. Within the Emperor Palpatine Commander Expansion, you'll find everything you need to add Emperor Palpatine to your army as a commander — leading the Imperial forces on his own or alongside another Imperial commander like his apprentice, Darth Vader. A single unpainted, easily assembled miniature depicts a cloaked Emperor Palpatine plotting his next move, while three upgrade cards allow you to tap into his mastery of the dark side of the Force. Finally, three new command cards give you more options for determining your battle plans, letting you make use of the Emperor’s abilities as a master strategist.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Imperial Royal Guards Unit Expansion
