description:
Junkyard Derby is a card-driven steampunk racing board game. Players construct a racing machine from components found in the junk pile and battle down the track to the finish line.

There are four components every racer needs to race: chassis, wheels, steering, and propulsion. There are over 250 racer combinations, so players may customize a racer that fits their style. Each component has its own attributes; some are quick and flimsy, others slow and sturdy. Junk cards may be combined to build contraptions and upgrades. Contraptions are single-use items that affect may damage another racer, speed up a racer, or repair racers quickly. Upgrades are permanent add-ons to a racer which improve its stats.

Once built, the cart moves along the track according to its speed rating. There are five hazard spaces on the board. When players hit a hazard, they interact with it by rolling dice and adding the relevant modifier from their custom racer. Hazards can slow down a racer, stop it temporarily, or damage it.

Each damage dealt to a racer is represented by a token which covers a beneficial stat on the racer. While covered by a damage token, that stat isn't added to the racer. As long as you have a positive net speed, players may move with damage on their carts. Players may repair damage by skipping a move step and removing two damage tokens, then continuing to race on the next round. The first player to pass the finish line wins!

Beasts: Edge of Extinction is a 2-6 player competitive card game where players take on the role of an ancient predatory Beast on the very Edge of Extinction. Each game, players face an onslaught of catastrophic events, opposing players, and even a perpetually persistent T-Rex hungrily in pursuit of his next meal...you!

Each character has innate resistances or susceptibility to specific damage types - physical, heat or cold - and a unique special ability only usable once per game, so make it count! Players must choose how to use their action points to attack, obtain resources, increase resistance and attack power, or heal their Beast by feeding on scavenged food or helpless, but useful dodos.

Incorporating a balance of strategy, luck, and player interaction with a unique order of events and blind resource draw, no 2 games are alike.

Survival is critical. Eliminate your competition to be the last Beast standing on the Edge of Extinction!

-description from publisher

Children learn about different fruits and vegetables as they build matching and memory skills in this jolly game with a cheeky twist. Take turns swapping your small veg patch cards to replicate the central veg patch board, be the first to do so and shout "Veg Patch Match!" But watch our for Mr. Sprout; when another player uses this card they can change the veg patch which everyone is matching to! A fun strategic game for all the family.

In Cranium Sculpt-It, all players work together to use their imaginations and win as a team.

One player picks a card and sculpts the object shown, using the included Cranium Clay. Then that player tries to get the others to guess what it is before the timer runs out.

The first player to get 4 correct guesses wins the game.

boardgame: Tau Ceti: Planetary Crisis – The Living Starship
description:
The living Starship is an expansion to Tau Ceti: Planetary Crisis core game that finds a mysterious living starship roaming around the cosmos.  This ship is intent on making your life harder and destroying anything it can. Multiple copies of the expansion can be used with the core game and the expansion is also compatible with all game modes.

The starship will jump from system to system attempting to steal exploration tokens and possibly destroying your orbitals!  You must stop this ship before it stops you!

Outbreak is the biggest shake-up to the Deadzone rules since 2.0 launched in 2016. It’s a vital supplement for any Deadzone player – whether you’re a heroic commander of Enforcers or the insidious leader of a Veer-myn insurgency, this is a book you need to stay competitive in the hostile battlezones of the future.

GENESIS OF THE NAMELESS

Outbreak introduces a brand-new faction into the Deadzone universe: the Nameless. These horrifying creatures are detailed with new background information and a full force list featuring small bug-like minions to lumbering Goliaths. Deadzone will never be the same again.

NEW SCENARIOS AND ITEMS

Four new scenarios help to keep the tournament scene interesting, while the original four scenarios are re-balanced to make them more competitive than ever before. Alongside this, a new selection of items ensures that every faction can uncover something useful in the fierce environments of Deadzone.

ALL-NEW FACTION LISTS

Don’t miss the latest faction lists! We’ve gone through each faction to ensure they’re more balanced than ever before, and introduced new units to help keep your opponent on their toes.

ELITE ARMY LISTS

If you fancy taking your army building to the next level, Elite Army Lists featured a themed leader and their loyal troops ready to take to the battlefield. Choose from the stoic Forge Fathers, implacable Asterians and more, then prepare for war!

boardgame: Commands & Colors Tricorne: The American Revolution – The French & More!
description:
The French & More! is the first expansion for the Commands & Colors Tricorne - The American Revolution  game. The kit adds ten new battles, adding the French army to the mix and expanding your existing forces.  Of course, the two major land battles involving the French Army are included in the scenarios: Savannah and Yorktown. The expansion also takes a detailed look at the Battle of Brandywine, along with the battles of Springfield, Kings Mountain and Pell’s Point.  New terrain tiles, additional blocks, stickers and larger, molded dice, are all included so you can play these new American Revolution War engagements.

Here’s what’s in the box:

3    Terrain and Accessories punchboards containing (these are the thick tiles that have been so well received by players), totaling 45 Double-sided Terrain Tiles and 18 Double-sided Victory Banner counters
8    Battle Dice – Bigger and now molded dice…!
3    Block Label sheets
4    Summary Cards
2    Unit Reference Charts
2    Terrain Effects Charts
1    Rule and Scenario Booklet
Over 100 wooden blocks

You must own a copy of Commands & Colors: Tricorne base game to use this Expansion Kit.

By design, Commands & Colors Tricorne - The American Revolution is not overly complex. The game is based on the highly successful Commands & Colors game system, where the Command cards drive movement while creating a “fog of war” and the battle dice resolve combat quickly and efficiently. Commands & Colors Tricorne - The American Revolution, however, introduces many new game concepts, which will add historical depth and provide even the most veteran Commands & Colors player many new play experiences and challenges.

The scale of the game fluctuates, which allows players to effectively portray some of the larger American Revolution battles, as well as smaller size skirmish actions. In some scenarios, an infantry unit may represent an entire brigade, while in others a unit may only represent a few companies of soldiers. Still the linear tactics of the period, that you will need to execute to gain victory, conform remarkably well to the advantages and limitations inherent to the various American Revolution Armies of the day, and the battlefield terrain features on which they fought. To further emphasize the differences in battlefield doctrine between the British and Continental forces, each army has its own unique deck of Combat cards.

Players, that are familiar with other Commands & Colors games, will soon note that unit combat losses in a Tricorne game are typically not as great as other games covered in the Commands & Colors series. This is a direct result of the linear tactic fighting style of the armies that fought during the American Revolution. Unit morale is the main thematic focus in a Tricorne battle as it was historically. Knowing that an entire unit, that has only taken minimal losses when forced to retreat, may actually break and rout from the battlefield, will definitely keep players on the edge of their command chairs during an entire battle.

Due to the rising tide of witchcraft that is threatening to overtake the burgeoning town of New Salem, the founders have elected a Constable to help mediate the situation. Because it is feared witches may have infiltrated the founding group, the Constable will rotate among them to avoid any witch getting too much power.

The 2nd Edition includes The Constable expansion. The Constable will be in charge of triggering the plentiful array of new events that have become available, such as Seance, Purge, and Hysteria. There will be a random assortment of 7 events (from a pool of 25+ events) in each game to create a different witch hunt each time.

The Gloom and Law buildings have been replaced with new sets that add or remove Despair with each building, but have an end game effect of adding or removing a lot more if you collect the set of 3.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The zombies have risen from their graves in this epic 2 player game.  Ready Set Battle is easy to learn but takes a zombie lifetime to master.

Play as the humans and try to hold off the zombies to build up your military power and eventually overtake the zombies.  Play the thunder down under card to control the pace of the game, and use your dreaded upgrade card to destroy the zombies forever.

Play as the zombies and come out hitting hard and fast.  Zombie cards keep coming back each round with larger and larger attacks.  Use the zombie dog to give flexibility in the game, and turn your opponent's cards into zombies which really frustrates the opposition.

Win the game by reducing your opponents life to 0.  Will the zombies win with their early attacks or will the humans win by building up their military power?  Get ready to battle in this exciting game!

—description from the designer

This exciting expansion features two new campaigns for Deadzone. One introduces solo-play - 'Artificial Stupidity' for a series of games against the Zombie hordes. The second campaign focuses around Nexus Psi and the hunt for the origins of the Plague outbreak.

In addition, there are also two new Elite Army lists, introducing new leaders for the Plague and Enforcers - the mutated Dr. Gayle Simmonds and Enforcer Recon Pathfinder N32-19.

This set contains:

1x Dr. Simmonds Exclusive metal model
    10x Plague Zombies
    Nexus Psi Source Book
    2x Urban Scenery A
    1x Urban Scenery Accessories
    1x Landing Pad Scenery B
    1x Defense Line Scenery A
    1x Defense Line Scenery B
    1x Expansion Scenery A
    1x Expansion Scenery B
    1x Connectors

This set contains enough components to build 21 bespoke hard plastic and premium plastic multi-part models, including:

15 Hard Plastic Nightcrawlers or Stalkers
    Malignus Chem Thrower and Chem Spitter Weapon Options
    4 Hard Plastic Nightmares
    Chem Thrower, Big Drill, Heavy Chem Thrower and Two handed Drill Weapon Options
    Premium Plastic Progenitor with Chem Grenade Launcher
    Premium Plastic Progenitor with Spitter Rifle
    25mm and 40mm Plastic Round Bases

Can be used in either Warpath or Deadzone

Mysterious and utterly alien, the Nameless are a bizarre menagerie of tentacles, claws and talons. They have recently turned hostile and begun to invade GCPS space, attacking outposts with their powerful technology and strange abilities.
Contents

This set contains everything you need get started with the Nameless in Deadzone, including:

1 Resin Psychotroid
    2 Plastic Riflemen
    2 Plastic Assassins
    2 Plastic Gunslingers
    2 Plastic Inkers
    2 Plastic Scuttlers
    4 Resin Needle Drones
    1 Resin Caratid
    2 Plastic Ogres
    Transparent Plastic Flying Bases
    25mm and 40mm Round Bases
    Mantic Points

Requires the Deadzone 2nd edition rulebook and Deadzone: Outbreak supplement to play.

The Galactic Co-Prosperity Sphere’s expansion has been swift and reckless, and has now reached the perilous Death Arc. When the Plague bursts upon the colonies, GCPS Marines have to fight for their survival in an uncaring, dangerous galaxy.
Contents

This set contains everything you need get started with the GCPS in Deadzone, including:

1 Metal Major General with Officer's Sword or Energy Gauntlet
    20 Hard Plastic GCPS Troopers/Rangers
    Can be armed with either Laser Rifles or Heavy Laser Rifles
    Optional Flamer, Grenade Launcher and Communications Equipment
    1 Hard Plastic and Metal GCPS Engineer
    2 Hard Plastic and Metal GCPS Anti-Infantry Weapon Teams
    Either team can be armed with a Mortar or Autocannon
    25mm and 60mm Plastic and MDF Round Bases
    Rules Leaflet including full stats and rules for fielding the GCPS in Deadzone
    Mantic Points

Requires the Deadzone 2nd Edition Rulebook to play.

Siege of Izmail is a two-player game recreating the storming of the Ottoman fortress Izmail, situated on the Danube River, by Russian forces under the command of General Alexander Suvorov. This battle occurred on 22 December 1790.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
1. Roll the die. Move as many spaces as the die indicates. (Boats, planes, and Whales are the same as squares and circles, and are counted as one move.) When starting, a player may move in any direction they choose. from villages or cities a player may move in any direction, even retracing the previous route, but a player may not change direction between cities or villages (the circles are not the only points of direction change.)

2. When a player lands on a city or village ( circles) they receive the Commerce or Adventure card for that place. The points or dollars are then added to that players score. Each card is worth different amounts of dollars or points. The points are converted to dollars for scoring purposes as follows:
1 Adventure point = $100.00

If a player rolls the die and lands on a place already occupied, the player occupying that place is "bumped" to the nearest Hazard. The "Bumped" player then has to select a Hazard card and follow the instructions on it for his/her next move. A player may choose to "Homestead". (Decision can be made after a roll, and only twice in each game.) lose one turn each time you become a Homesteader.

3. Any player Landing on a plane may "Fly" to any city or village on the game board.

4. Any player landing on a Hazard selects a hazard card follows the instructions. Once used, the cards are returned to the stack face up on the bottom.

5. Commerce and Adventure cards are kept by the players when received (if the game is not complete by the time the cards are out, players will return their cards to the Banker and continue the game to completion.)

6. The first player to accumulate $5,000.00 and returns to starting point wins the game!

(Note)
If two players are playing, we suggest the score be raised to $10,000.00 to make it more interesting and challenging.

When one player reaches the $5,000.00 or ($10,000.00) point, it's up to the rest of the players to prevent him/her from returning to the starting point by whatever means they can.

Canary islands are independent... The next day, Spain strikes. Each turn they head towards your cities and each turn you try to stop them. If you hold on them for 2 days,they"ll retreat and you will win. If they occupy your main city by the 2nd day,they'll win.

—description from the designer

It's been 30 years since the last uprising of the Zraxii Empire was beaten down by the brave troops of the Terra Republic. Applying for the mission with the codename "Elite Peacekeeping Shockwave" sounded glorious at first, but turned out being a little bit dull as patrolling a remote part of the galaxy without any upbeat outposts did not motivate the crew greatly. Instead, one of the most stimulating activities became the weekly BBQ on the electric fire in the dust-heavy unused launch bay, within the protective shield of the XP95 fighter craft. Today's menu included some deep space frozen Milky Way cow meat, discovered on an abandoned vessel near the dwarf planet Taplor 4.

"RRAARRRAARR!" — The ship's alarm was blazing.

Only after a few important wasted moments did the squad realize this was not a test as they found themselves between the run wild XP95 fighter, filled with enough explosives to destroy a small moon, and the marching forces of the until now assumed defeated Zraxii Empire trying to board the ship. Luckily the squad's exceptionally extensive combat training and experience came in handy, if only they had kept up training regularly...

Launch Bay Escape is a ridiculous co-operative game featuring planning that's enriched with a small deck-building mechanism. It's suitable for space soldiers from the age of 10 and takes about 30-45 minutes. The difficulty level is easily adjustable per game as you increase the complexity by adding more and more aliens, obstacles, and madness.

The game is played in rounds. At the start of the game a rectangular game board divided in rows of squares is placed. One end features the launching ship and the starting area for the players, the other end features the Launch Bay exit. All players have their own character sheet and corresponding deck, and all players have their own special abilities, number of hit points and shield. Players start with six cards in their hand. At the start of a round, all players plan three cards from the available cards in their hand, face down. Next, the first card of all players is revealed. All cards show a number, movement options, and special abilities. In order of highest to lowest number, every player follows the instructions on their planned card. When all cards are resolved, the ship moves one row towards the Launch Bay exit and all aliens move once towards the ship and attack. This is repeated for both remaining planned cards. At the end of the round, all planned cards and remaining cards in the player's hand are discarded and a new hand of six cards is drawn. Once damage accumulates, some cards will be planned randomly.

Players can gain experience by killing aliens. Experience enables the players to buy extra cards to add to their deck. In addition, players may find items on the game board, usable only once or multiple times.

The players lose if the ship or all the players are destroyed before reaching the escape pods at the end of the Launch Bay.

—description from publisher

The aim of the game is to jump the wooden pieces over a wall into the holes of the playing board and to score as many points as possible.
On her/his turn, each player has 3 attempts to flick one of the pieces into one of the holes. In order to do this, the player has to slide one of the provided springboards into the slits in the wall, place the pieces on it and flick it over the wall (hopefully into the net).
Jumping height, distance and direction depend on the following:

     How deep and in which angle the springboard is slid into the wall
     The placing of the springboard in the wall
     The power with which the springboard is pushed down


—user summary

boardgame: Mind the Gap (fan expansion for MBT: Second Edition)
description:
NOTE:  This game is no longer for sale.

***All briefings and maps can be downloaded from the files section of this page for free.  The maps should be printed on 12 x 18 inch paper to produce an 11 x 17 inch map.***

"Mind the Gap" depicts a never fought 1979 engagement in the Fulda Gap using Jim Day's and GMT’s MBT Game pitting a reinforced US Armored Cavalry Troop from the 11th Armored Cavalry Regiment “The Black Horse” against a reinforced Motorized Rifle Battalion equipped with BMP-2 and T-64BV Tanks.

The scenario reflects my experience as a Platoon Leader in the gap as a tank and armored cavalry platoon leader and troop XO  in the 1st Squadron, 11th ACR stationed in Fulda from January 1977 – December 1979.  The game is not balanced although players may balance it as they deem fit.  The actual fight was not; we had a healthy respect for the quantity, if not the quality, of the enemy.

To play the game, you must have a copy of the initial GMT MBT Game.  The expansion includes a new map of four 11 x 17 inch map sheets scaled at 100 meters per hex (the same scale as the MBT game).  The map is based on a period map of the battle area and utilizes contours to depict elevation.

The Scenario Briefing, a B Troop and Soviet Battle Books with deployment instructions, and alternate order of battle listings, a planning map and terrain key are downloadable from the files section.

Expansion sets are not required (BAOR, FRG, and the forthcoming Canadian supplement) provide additional vehicle counters as well as leaders.  A list of substitute counters is provided to enable the player to field the complete order of battle for both sides or the players can use some of the expansion set vehicles or combine sets to complete the order of battle.

Mind the Gap can be played with two players but may be more interesting if played with up to 6 players on the US side (CO and the five platoon leaders), and 6 on the USSR side (Battalion Commander and 4 company commanders), and a referee.

The gamer can also simulate a 1978 engagement by downloading a Vehicle Card for the M60A1 tank from https://boardgamegeek.com/filepage/146576/official-bonus-data-card-um-13b-m60a1-aos and by deleting the M901 Improved TOW vehicles from the US Order of Battle

Mind the Gap© 2018 StrongPoint Simulations and Modeling, LLC.  •    MBT™ is arbitrable to James M. Day.
The MBT Game ©2016 GMT Games.
Counter images are used with permission of James M. Day.

boardgame: Not Yet Lost: The Defense of the Polish Post Office in Danzig
description:
Not Yet Lost (NYL) is a quick playing solitaire print-and-play game depicting the heroic defense of the Polish Post Office in Danzing during the German invasion in September, 1939. Using a chit-pull system, the player defends against increasing numbers of German attackers over the course of 15 turns. The player's success is measured by the length of time (in turns) that the Germans are repulsed. With each fighting turn that the defenders survive, Poland is not yet lost.

-description from designer

Super Meat Boy Rival Rush is a one versus one competitive card game where Players race to the finish. Players assemble twenty card decks comprised of actions and skills that will get them to the end, along with some nasty tricks to trip up their opponent. Beloved characters from the Super Meat Boy Cinematic Universe make appearances for the first time as trading cards, each with their own unique abilities and powers to help navigate the levels ahead at breakneck speeds. Rival Rush is a nonstop fast paced no holds barred finger blistering every card for themselves showdown where only the swiftest and smartest survive.

Taking heavy influence in the micro-deck builder genre, the game puts the players in the task of creating a deck of cards that matches the symbols that pop up in the "stages" in front of us. The trick? Your rival might guess which card you have chosen for this turn and could block your moves through the stages; and in Rival Rush there will be only ONE winner.

Taking in consideration that the game offers the possibility of a fresh experience every time it's played thanks to it's random generated levels (constructed from the cards themselves), Rival Rush seems like a very fun and competitive way of enjoying Super Meat Boy; hopefully not as frustrating as it's digital counterpart.

GFY is an easy-to-learn, fast-paced card game in the spirit of Mario Kart. The rules are simple, but winning is never easy, especially at a table with experienced players. GFY makes a perfect gift for anyone in college or the military.

Each player start with 7 cards and your goal is to be the first to get rid of your cards, that is how the game ends.

On your turn draw 1 card and play 1 card, doing exactly what the card's rules say to do. Typically, this will be to discard more cards, draw more cards, or give or take cards from another player. If you can't do it exactly, you can not play it. If you can't play anything, draw a 2nd card and end your turn.

This game was made as a form of giving back to our fans and supporters. Yes, it's FREE! You can get the Print N Play file simply by subscribing your email to Medieval Lords. We love to spread fun and joy (perhaps death and destruction too, in this case) It is wild, so expect to laugh lots when you play - more so if your friends are horrible actors! Alternatively, you can get the game professionally printed over at The Game Crafter here: https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/i-know-how-you-died1 (Cost-to-cost price by Game Crafter with zero profit for us) This game is intended for pure utter rubbish type of casual fun, don't go in expecting any serious game play! Play this with the wrong crowd and you'd end up in an awkward evening!

Players are issued 3 random secret cards depicting various deaths, and they must choose one of them to re-enact. Then after everyone has done a quick 5 to 10 seconds re-enactment of their chosen scene, they toss these cards (still face-down) into a common pile in the middle of the table. Shuffle the pile and reveal the top card to everyone. If anyone wants to guess at who had acted out the death scene of that particular card, simply say 'I know!' and call out that player's name. This is on a first-come-first-serve basis. Note that the actual actor of the card cannot guess his/her own card, but must pretend to participate so as not to give the answer away.

If a scene was guessed correctly the first time, the guesser gains 4 Point. If a scene was guessed correctly the second time, the guesser gains 3 Point. The actor also gets 3 Points. All wrong guesses will deduct 1 Point from the guesser.

If no one got the correct answer to a scene after two attempts, the next scene is drawn and the game continues. After 3 scenes, the entire pile is discarded completely and a new round begins. This also means there will be some scenes that do not show up despite having been acted out. This is to increase the difficulty of the game.

The game ends when the last card is drawn, or when someone achieves 20 or more Points. Rules can be found at the Files section of this game page.

Have fun dying...

The photo safari on the game board is open, so keep your eyes open and cameras on!

In Take a Pic, dice indicate which animals can be seen this round and you must be the first one who takes a picture. Don't forget to protect pictures on your flash card because competitors don't slumber!

The game includes two big dice with animals and a photographer (joker) on their faces, and the animals on the round cards represent what you see through your camera lens. Now it's time to find out who is faster. Say cheese!

Suddenly a harsh voice crashed over the mountains: "I will spare the pity life of every minor creature in this land who obeys me! I could even like some of you, if they stay calm in my herd and supply me well. The rest of you should try me and suffer my anger!" Very large and gorgeous miniature of the Giant: Velik-an, who demands tributes from the players every day in order to leave them be... But not everyone is obliged to pay his price, you can always try to fight him until the giant's total defeat or temporary repel.


    NCBC is a collection of mini expansions based on neutral big scale figurines, which are designed to enlarge the immersive Battalia universe, introducing a whole bunch of new strategic possibilities to the players and adding completely new adventure elements to the basically competitive plot. In general they don't belong to any faction (resp. to any player) and can be played in different combinations with the basic game or with the BIG!Expansion. We don't recommend to mix more than 3 different types of NPCs in your games in order to avoid higher complexity. But you can mix them in totally different combinations, which ensures the long-term replayability and excitement in this game universe. Normally the NPCs have small complementary rules, which are provided only online on Battalia's home page battalia.eu as high resolution printable PDF files. The miniatures are distributed in small packaging boxes with custom artwork as separate items or possibly in bundles.

He is unstoppable 
He is the pure evil and 
He wants to feed on your soul forever!
Give it a chance! Save it... run!
.... cause he is the SOULEATER: The Hunger Himself!

boardgame: BATTALIA NCBC: Mistress of the Mist
description:
Large and gorgeous miniature of the Mistress of the Mist. The wandering enchantress, always attracted by the building and expanding of cities... She was born as the only daughter of a Cloudborn master-builder and her love for architecture and building arts remained forever. But the fate brought her later to an eastern magical school, where she learned the arts to cover and defend huge places with a misty veil, the perfect protection for cities against enemy attacks. But every coin is double sided, that's why sometimes her positive intentions cause more trouble than real good...

Ok, it's not always easy for the master, because we're a bit erratic too and we always want to be fed, fed, fed... otherwise unhappy and this means, bad support for the master and short list of possible wishes.... But if we're happy and in a good mood - master will be satisfied too, this means - long list of wishes for happy master... And last but not least - we are very jealous and just need to be "THE ONE AND ONLY" for the master!"

boardgame: BATTALIA NCBC: Krampling vs Oracle Knight
description:
Battalia is the perfect land to live on with its beautiful nature on the surface... but down there, deep underground rules the Blight and Kramplings are dwelling in the tunnels. Now they want to crawl out from their forsaken wells, spoil the Stormgates and taint the vicinity. All living creatures and plants shall die or become weaker... All movements in their range will be harder and the worst part is - they fear no ordinary hero, but only the Oracle Knights....

... Only a few of them left during the years - not every man is able to bear this burden. Their life is not their own, their cause is the eternal pledge - they are the noble order of the Oracle Knights. Only they posses the secret formula, the power of the ancient spell, which heals the corruption and frightens the Kramplings to death. Their weapons were forged with a magic adjuration able to overcome the aura of the beasts and to destroy them... Eternal secret since the dawn of time...

[English]
Masterplan is a competitive city building game where the players impersonate a construction entrepreneur who is willing to be the most influential one in the urbanization process of the new neighborhood.

During your turn, you can use your workers to perform different actions: you can auction for new building plans or construction sites, start a new building using those plans and sites, or use special actions from neutral buildings.

Each building you finish will modify its surroundings, because no one wants to live near the noisy factory, but having a mall just across the street will make your tenants happy. Plan your neighborhood carefully so your buildings are happier than your rivals. Go for a business area or develop the factories, but be ready to modify your plans when the other players start building near you.

After a set number of rounds, all of the points from your constructed buildings will be tallied, and the best architect will we declared winner.

-description from publisher
 [Spanish]
Masterplan es un juego de construcción de ciudades competitivo en el que los jugadores se pondrán en la piel de un empresario de la construcción que quiere ser el más influyente en el proceso de urbanización del nuevo barrio.

Durante tu turno, puedes usar tus trabajadores para realizar diferentes acciones: puedes entrar en subastas para conseguir nuevos planos o solares, comenzar nuevos edificios con dichos planos y solares, o usar acciones especiales de los edificios neutrales.
Cada edificio que completes modificará sus alrededores, ya que nadie quiere vivir cerca de una ruidosa fábrica, pero vivir justo enfrente del centro comercial hará tus inquilinos más felices. Planifica tu vecindario cuidadosamente para que tus edificios sean más felices que los de tus rivales. Céntrate en crear una zona de negocios, o desarrolla las fábricas, pero estate preparado para modificar tus planes cuando los otros jugadores comiencen a construir cerca de ti.

Tras un número de rondas determinado, se contarán los puntos de tus edificios construidos, y el mejor arquitecto será declarado ganador.

boardgame: BATTALIA NCBC: The Order of the Half Oracle
description:
Dishearter - She is tough and she is pretty, but cold as a stone and fully committed to her pledge. She belongs to the Order of the Half Oracle and would always try to "turn" you. Dishearter will charm you first and try to convince you to follow her path... but if you reject... She would take your courage away, consume your faith and moral and leave you wretched and miserable. She is able to do that... The main rule of the Order teaches: If you are not with us, you're against us!

Truth-squeezer - Are you willing to tell the truth? Dare you to hide it? It's your choice, just always be prepared to meet the Truth-Squeezer ... Do you believe you're ready to face him and listen to him, hissing in your ear: Knowledge is power, but the truth could be your weakness...  I know how to squeeze it from you.... The Order will prize my efforts, since I always hear their call: Remove all obstacles!

Challenger - Large and very detailed miniature of a fanatics challenger, another great NPC, which offers new style of adventures... Meeting you, he will challenge you, will always try to seduce your natural born greed for success, he will play with your moral and conscience and finally he will let you no choice, but to make one very hard decision.... It's always about the same old dilemma: Victory or Defeat - and are you willing to pay the price for it?

Large scale miniature of the almighty lord of battle, the master of all surges, The Missing Element himself. The Keeper emerges always on every battlefield to reward the victor in the combat with one of the elemental keys.

Alternative game end condition - playing with this figure introduces new game end condition! The game ends when someone gathers the necessary number of keys, plus 4 cities level 4. Moreover, the game does not end when the map is fully covered.

Alternative win condition - in this game it is not enough to have the most points. Only the players that have gathered all necessary keys can claim the victory - they are the only ones who compare their score.

An ancient abstract board game from Italy, the Valle Cervo. It's an asymetrical hunting game. Three hounds hunt a bear, moving from point to point on a special board. The bear needs to escape getting cornered for 40 moves.

Large and gorgeous miniature of the wild and restless NPC mercenary leader, who enters the map randomly through roads from outside the border lands and automatically attacks every hero on his way….To deal with him, every player has a couple of choices: Of course you can fight him, or you could try to bribe him or even hire him for a week...

The figure will come out of the ruins from time to time and will be moved according to a special rule. The guards will try to curse you, making troubles on the map, and to collect back all the Great Artefacts from the players.


    NCBC is a collection of mini expansions based on neutral big scale figurines, which are designed to enlarge the immersive Battalia universe, introducing a whole bunch of new strategic possibilities to the players and adding completely new adventure elements to the basic competitive plot. In general they don't belong to any faction (resp. to any player) and can be played in different combinations with the basic game or with the BIG!Expansion. We don't recommend to mix more than 3 different types of NPCs in your games in order to avoid higher complexity. But you can mix them in totally different combinations, which ensures the long-term replay ability and excitement in this game universe. Normally the NPCs have small complementary rules, which are provided only online on Battalia's home page battalia.eu as high resolution printable PDF files. The miniatures are distributed in small packaging boxes with custom artwork as separate items or possibly in bundles.

The aim of the game is to get your family of 3 across the valley of death to the safety of their new home.

To do this you have 2 dice, you can use the number as one whole number for one Lemming or you can split the dice and move two lemmings (sticking to the numbers on the dice please!!) as you move along you must avoid rivers, canyons, forest fires and the ubiquitous Random Encounters which can be good or bad! However, you can navigate the terrain using Save or Play cards specific to each obstacle. For example, rivers can be used as normal squares if you have a boat, Lem Cardashian's floating but, or a Child's birthday balloon. Save or Play cards can also save you from predators such as the Lemming Chainsaw Massacre which prevents you from being eaten by mountain lions, snakes, or being trampled by an angry bull BUT you can only use one card per one lemming (then the card goes back to the bottom of the pack) so use them wisely to get your lemmings to safety.

As you move around the board the risks are greater AND if at any point you land on a space with another teams lemming you must battle it out for territory by rolling one die each, the one with the least has to move back the number they rolled, but beware of landing on Random Encounters and other Lemmings because you must keep going until you are safe.

The game builds in tension as the players begin to reach their new home because you are now in a position where you don’t have the option to choose which lemming you move, beware friends and family as you get closer to winning they might have a mean streak you didn’t know about and use a Save or Play card against another player.

This board game pits you against your family and friends in a fun but strategic game, it’s your job to know which Lemming to move and when to strike in order to get home first. As the game progresses the stakes are higher - Lemmy is counting on you!

-description from designer

Starcadia Quest: Showdown is a brand-new expansion that complements not only Starcadia Quest but also plugs into the Arcadia Quest series as well! Showdown is not about going on an arduous quest to defeat evil monsters, no siree! Showdown introduces a new mode: full Player-versus-Player deathmatches that will see players draft & pick all-new Upgrade tokens and enter a vicious arena to battle enemy Crews!

The action in Showdown takes place in a Double-sided Game Board, with one side designed for 3-4 players, and a 2 players version on the back. With its tight corridors and sharp turns, the Crews (and Guilds) will see themselves immersed into non-stop action! Only 1 Stimpack, 1 Recharge Token and 2 Computer Terminals are still functioning in the arena and sometimes become contested hotspots. In Showdown, the Crews don’t claim Victory by accumulating Victory Points: the first Crew that manages to score 3 Kills is deemed Victorious!

Thanks to the Hollow Deck technology, Starcadians can conjure up the legendary Heroes from ancient Arcadia to test their mettle! With 60 Hero cards and 4 Crew Dashboards (sporting an appropriately battle-damaged look and slots for Pets and Mounts!) players with any Arcadia Quest core box (Classic or Inferno) or with the Starcadia Quest core box can join the Showdown!

—description from the publisher

Highly Effective city walls defense weapons. A must have in some situations, especially when playing with the rampaging Rammoth Deleo-arx. Increases the security of your city against random NPC attacks and could be used in player vs. player interactions in different scenarios.

Ready for defense! The rampaging Rammoth called Deleo-Arx is about to smash your city gates and bombard with ballista arrows the city walls at the same time. You will need special support of defensive weapons in order to hold the city level status.

The miniatures are distributed in small packaging boxes with custom artwork as separate items or possibly in bundles.

Say hello to the deal of the century! This guy operates almost like a genie in a bottle. He is able to exchange old artefacts for new ones at the best possible rate... and for now he is the only one who has access to the most precious substance in the universe called STAR-DUST. It is similar to the supply cards, but with double value! He is always strolling around the Gates. Catch him if you can!

18 round inventory marker tokens that are meant to be placed directly over the existing inventory spaces on the six market stall tiles included with Tower.

Includes instructions for use.

It randomizes what the markets will sell in the game.

With 112 cards to enhance your Super Munchkin game, The Narrow S Cape is much more than just this season's favorite fashion accessory! It teems with new villains like the contemptible Cheese Wiz, Gothzilla (with earthquake-causing platform boot action!), and the Ratwoman. And lest you feel overwhelmed by villainy, this expansion also features new items, new Powers and a new Class: the Brain!

This game combines Super Munchkin and Super Munchkin 2.

Früher war es ein niedliches Munchkin-Spiel. Aber dann wurde es in mysteriösen Energien gebadet, von einem radioaktiven Chihuahua gebissen und fasste ein uraltes Götzenbild an. Nun fliegt es durch Super-City und kämpft für eherne Werte wie Wahrheit, Gerechtigkeit und noch höhere Boni! Schlüpf in die Rolle eines Superhelden und sei ein Mutant, ein Exot, ein Mystiker, ein Techno oder ein Superhirn. Je höher deine Stufe ist, desto mehr Superkräfte kannst du haben. Bekämpfe niederträchtige Teufelsgenies, verheerende Monster und aus anderen Dimensionen einfallende Außerirdische – vom läppischen Explodoman über Jürgen von der Grippe bis hin zum Großen Alten Planetenfresser – UND NIMM IHR ZEUG! Wenn du deine Unterwäsche drüber trägst, eine Lizenz zum Verwüsten hast und alle mit deinen Monologen langweilst, kann kein Feind vor dir bestehen!

Die Erweiterung Coole Capes ist in Super Munchkin bereits enthalten.

boardgame: Spiel mir das Lied vom Munchkin 1+2
description:
The Good, the Bad, and the Munchkin 2: Beating a Dead Horse contains 56 new Wild West-themed cards specifically for use in The Good, the Bad, and the Munchkin, although they can also be added to any other Munchkin game.

This expansion includes a new class, Cavalry, that allows you to sacrifice your treasure to gain a level when helping a non-Cavalry player. Since this level is gained as part of a combat, it can be the winning level.

This game combines The Good, the Bad, and the Munchkin and 
The Good, the Bad, and the Munchkin 2: Beating a Dead Horse.

Als ich aus Alabama kam, fi el der Regen dick und schwer, und ich hatte keine Waffe und auch gar kein Goldstück mehr. Meine Braut hab’ ich verlassen, doch ich konnte nix dafür. Denn das größte Glück des Munchkins lauert hinter einer Tür … Oh Susanna, das Munchkinleben ist nicht schwer. Jetzt hab’ ich schicke Sachen an und ein geiles Schießgewehr!

Spiel mir das Lied vom Munchkin verlegt das klassische Munchkintum in den Wilden Westen! Spielt Cowboy und Indianer, Gesetzlose und Städter. Tragt Duelle mit Daniel Boom, dem Wolpertinger oder mit dem Mördermäßigen Jalapeño aus! Setzt euch den Großen Cowboyhut auf und steckt euch den Sheriffstern an … Bewaffnet euch bis an die Zähne mit der Kanone aus dem Bürgerkrieg und dem Kuhfl aden … Sattelt dann noch die Rösser und trommelt eure Handlanger zusammen, und sogleich könnt ihr wieder staubige Türen eintreten! Auch hier gibt es wieder Monster zum Töten und Schätze zum Einsacken – Yee-HAW!

Die Erweiterung Zu Tode geritten ist in dieser Ausgabe bereits enthalten. Wir machen sie damit erstmals auf Deutsch zugänglich.

boardgame: Beasts of Balance: Kickstarter Promo Battle Cards
description:
This Kickstarter Promo contains 2 new Battle Cards, Divine Rewind and Friendly Fire.

Divine Rewind: Select a Beast. It transforms into an earlier version of itself.

Friendly Fire: The next 15 seconds of volcano damage will instead give life to your Beasts.

The prototypes of these cards were called Theory of Devolution and Lava-Va-Voom! respectively.

boardgame: Blood Red Skies: German – Junkers JU-88 A Bomber
description:
Originally designed as a Schnellbomber (fast Bomber) the Junker JU88 became Germany's most successful multi-role combat aircraft in World War II. The production ran from 1936 to 1945 and over 16,000 were built, more than any other twin-engine plane in the Luftwaffe. Miniatures are supplied unpainted and some assembly may be required

Contents:

3 Junkers JU-88 A German Bombers
3 Decal Sheets
3 Advantage Flying Base & Extension
3 Double-sided Pilot Skill Discs
1 Aircraft Card
1 Assembly Instruction Leaflet
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description:
Something Is Missing is a picture book written and illustrated in Taiwan that has now been adapted into a memory dexterity game. This game not only provides you with the book's images faithfully represented, but also brings you back to experience Taiwan's rich traditional culture.

Through the game, players are invited to carefully appraise the images, and the one to first successfully recall the missing object scores. Through this lighthearted 3-7 player game, everyone can experience both fun and nostalgia for the past.

Streaking Kittens is the second expansion for Exploding Kittens, the award-winning card game that made Kickstarter history as the most-backed project, ever.

This expansion deck includes 15 cards, featuring 8 new types of actions and a Streaking Kitten which allows you to hold an Exploding Kitten in your hand without exploding.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Urban Insanity is the game where the board changes every time you play.

128 game-play variations are available, from endless destruction to mind-bending strategy... and all with the same basic rules!

Work together to complete a city map... until it's time to score points! Compete to complete city blocks, streets, suburbs, and stadiums. Dial up the madness with one or more of the 6 expansion packs. Create a mobster turf war, an alien invasion, light your city on fire, and much more.

Each turn consists of drawing an action card and playing a tile. In a single round everything can change!

This fast-paced game combines strategy, luck, and something something something.

We debuted in the game room at DragonCon 2018, and were an instant hit!

We've created a couple videos for your enjoyment, so take a moment to see what we've cooked up!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rune Walker: A Solitaire Rune Game
description:
Rune Walker is a game of discovery and adventure using a standard set of 24 runes. It draws upon the ancient myths of the Teutonic peoples in order to present a world of heroism and challenge.  Each step matters in this world where Jotuns intend to thwart the Rune Walker and her attempt to reclaim the wisdom of the nine worlds.  The Rune Walker will face opponents of immense and growing power armed with lore and weapons of great strength.  The object of Rune Walker is to succeed in walking all nine worlds and to rebuild the great world tree – Yggdrasil.  When all nine worlds have been placed upon Yggdrasil the game is won and the Rune Walker has claimed the wisdom of the Runes and worlds.

- A Game for One Player

—description from the designer

Champions of the Internet is a party card game with real pics of the biggest champs to ever grace the internet!

WHAT'S IN THE BOX
People Cards: the most diverse, awesome deck of real people! You never know what type of person you're gonna get- fitness fanatics, body modifiers, drag queens, moms, models... you name it, we've got it!

Question Cards: Our Question Cards are hilarious and cover a wide range of topics- scary stuff, sexy stuff, sweet stuff. Plus, our added hashtags make them even funnier!

HOW TO PLAY
All players draw 5 People Cards. One player acts as the judge for the round and reads aloud a Question Card. The other players put down one People Card that they think best answers the question. It's up to the judge to pick their favorite played People Card. The player whose card was chosen wins the round! Play as many rounds as you desire.

5 GAMES IN ONE BOX
There are actually multiple games you can play! Inside the box, you’ll also learn how to play Slap That Ass, Bed Wed Dead, Identity Crisis and The Final Rose.

-description from designer

Welcome to Mythos, a H.P. Lovecraft inspired 1920’s horror skirmish game.

In Providence U.S.A a City like many other, the stage is set for what could be the first battle for the very survival of every last man, woman and child of Humanity. A war is coming, though not like the one that ended but a few years ago. An ancient being stirs in the deep, something so powerful that if it were to awaken, it would wipe out Humanity in the blink of an eye.

​The Sea Queen has been given the task and sent forth her best, they have made their way to land and now walk amongst you attempting to blend in as they scout this world above the water.

Other creatures born in the wild have been drawn to the area, the woods and forests are their realm and they are fiercely protective of them. They strengthen their numbers with sacrificial magic and awaken the old things of the forest best left sleeping.

Guardians from a long past age have been shipped by chance or fate to the town, their chittering growing in volume as they begin to awaken from their hibernation.

Cultists find key points of power for summoning. Their decades long work finally paying dividends as they close on the secret to moving beyond human form and finding the path of enlightenment that will see them become gods.

And finally, the defenders of Humanity, long since forgotten but still ever vigilant. The prophets have spoken, the signs of something dark and powerful are plain to see and they will not stand idly by but will fight to their dying breath against the coming darkness.

This skirmish game will see two or more players take to the table top to play one of the core game types, or one of the story solo/campaign games of Mythos.
 Each faction member is unique, coming not only with their own stats, Mythos abilities and traits but also a second side to their nature. Some will reveal this as insanity takes hold, others will be triggered by certain effects and interactions. Each turn players will alternate their activation's using up all the action points of each character. ‘Live combat’ will see that not every fight goes your way even if you started it, blasting your foes with ranged or arcane attacks may see them dodge away, taking that valuable item with them as they do.

Balancing your Mythos use will be key, as each point spent also takes away a point of sanity and once you go insane there’s no coming back but it’s not always a bad thing.

So, are you up for the challenge, to take the fight to your faction’s enemies, to put the objective before all else and push past your sanity threshold until all you see is red and insanity takes you?
​
Yes, then welcome to Mythos!

—description from the publisher

The First Word Climbing Game

Captain Can Spell is a word game with innovative game rules. It is easy to learn and fun. Besides this game will test your word skills, it also involves layers of strategy for you to discover. This game will fit all word game lovers, from beginner to expert.

Gameplay overview (2 players):

Each player is dealt 13 cards, the aim is to get rid of all cards, players take turns to play a word. All words are ranked, from lowest to highest, by their first letter in alphabetical order. Player in turn must play a word of the same ranking or higher than the previous player. For example, the previous player played "book", the next player can play "bat" or "cat, dog, eagle...zoo", but this player cannot play "apple" due to "A" is lower in rank than "B". If this player cannot or does not want to play a word in his/her turn, then he/she has to pass and draw two cards from the draw pile. After a pass, the next player can play any new word.

Scoring system:

The player who gets rid of all cards wins the round, the losing player has to take the penalty points which is the number of cards in his/her hand plus the number of cards remains in the draw pile, 1 point each. This will add up to his/her total penalty points. When one of the players reaches the total penalty points of 100, the player with less penalty points wins the game.

3-4 players game:

This game can be played up to 4 players with the slightly modified game rules in which involves some different strategies of playing.

—description from the designer

Players or teams compete to see who can complete the most tasks while using the small, doll-like hands provided.

There are three different ways to play the game: Relay, Battle, and Charades. You can choose how you want to play depending on how many players you have. Each of the Challenge cards includes a challenge that can be used in Relay or Battle gameplay and one Charade.

In Relay play, which is for four or more players, teams compete to be the first to complete five challenges using the tiny hands. Player 1 on the team does the first challenge, and then passes the hands to player 2 who completes the second challenge and passes the hands to the third player for the third challenge, and so on. The first team to finish five challenges wins.

If you only have two players, you can Battle. Players go head-to-head to see who can complete a challenge first. You score one point if you do. The first player to earn five points wins.

And for three or more players, you can play Charades. The acting player uses the tiny hands to act out the clue on the Challenge card while the other players try to guess the answer. You can add a one- or two-minute time limit, if you want. The player who guesses right gets one point, and so does the acting player. The first player to earn five points wins.

Rumors are told that gold can be found in the mountains!

Build settlements next to the mountains in Montana: Goldrush to claim your spot and get a chance to gain some extra wealth. New settlements can be built, which can be used to gain extra benefits or to hinder others. Also, new landscapes with their own special rules bring more variety to the game. Finally, events will change the flow of the game. Some events give players a bonus while others make actions harder.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Middle-Earth Strategy Battle Game: Armies of the Lord of the Rings

description:
The monkeys can do it all. Upside-down. Mirroring. On one foot. In the air. They have balance, flexibility, and strength... but so do you.

Monkey Moves is the ultimate game to test your trust, coordination, and ability to have fun. Pair with a fellow monkey and get your body moving to mimic the monkey moves on each card. The game is in your hands as you and your partner strategize to earn more points than your opponents. Calibrate on Level 1, risk it with Level 2, or jump straight into challenging Level 3 moves; higher level moves are harder, but worth more points. And remember, it’s always worth trying the move, or your opponents can try to steal.

Are YOU up to the challenge?

—description from the designer

Starcadia Quest: Build-a-Robot brings not only a whole new branching campaign, monsters, heroes and Upgrades, but it’s also a modular expansion that introduces companion Robots, a new system that can be added to any Starcadia Quest Scenario!

—description from the publisher

Contains:
- Rulebook
- Campaign Sheet Pad
- 13 Highly-Detailed Plastic Miniatures
- 38 Cards
- 30+ Tokens
- 3 Game Tiles
- 4 Robot Dashboards

A card driven Wargame, winning several awards in the 2018 Wargame PnP Contest:
 3rd Place: Best Overall Game
2nd Place: Best Artwork
2nd Place: Best Overall Rules
2nd Place: Best 2+ Player Game
2nd Place: Best Short/Quick/Small Game
2nd Place: Best New Designer
3rd Place: Most Original Concept

SUMMARY
Japan in 1567, at the height of the Sengoku Jidai – the Age of Warring States. Powerful Samurai families each have conquered large parts of Japan, and the days of the last Ashikaga Shogun in Kyoto are numbered. By land and sea, many more battles will befall the already war-torn country. And soon, one of the many Daimyōs – the Samurai Warlords – might unite enough regions of Japan under his banner, to call himself the new Shogun. Who will be the first to quench his thirst for power, and start a dynasty to shape Japan for the centuries to come?

GOAL
Become the greatest of the Samurai, by controlling Kyoto and large parts of Japan.

GAMEPLAY
Players draw cards to place armies, attack or fortify provinces and send out agents to sabotage their enemies. The more powerful players become, the more cards they may draw, but the less control they have over which cards to keep.

Every now and then, an Event Card will bring change to the war. The biggest of which is the arrival of the Europeans, who bring firearms and Christianity with them, opening up new possibilities to the players.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The game of skill, style and sticky whiskers! There have always been countless reasons why having a beard is better than not having a beard - the beard gives you a strange power that will improve your life, your thoughts will look deeper and more developed with a beard, a beard can make a hipster’s nerdy shirt look cool. Now there’s one more for the list – Beards make the perfect backdrop for a fun party game!

The game centers around completing stunts with three different fake beards. On a turn, two players (either as a team or two cooperating competitors) will try to complete one of the many stunts represented on the cards in the game. Each card provides details about the challenge, the number of attempts allowed, and how many points the stunt is worth.

One player puts on a fake beard with a set of glasses, while the other grabs the plastic balls that are covered in Velcro. The team then follows the instructions given on the card, often trying to get the ball caught on the beard. If they succeed in the designated number of attempts, then both players (or the team) score the points!

Play continues until one player (or team) reaches 11, and he/she/they are the winners!

Contents include - 50 beard ball stunt cards, 5 beard balls, 3 stylish beards of varying difficulty levels, 1 pair of glasses, 1 Bearded die, 1 game board, and instructions

A new one of micro-game series made by Jog Kung.

In this game, each player try to arrange the production line in his/her factory, the one who completes more lines and makes the longer pipeline transport will earn more points and win the game!

The game contains two or three rounds according to the number of players. During each round 9 cards are drawn to form a common card row.  Each player then takes a turn to pick a card from either end of that common row, and then places the card to expand the production line in his/her factory.

When there are 10 cards in each player's factory, the game ends and compare each players' score.

Negotiator Cards each add a power that can be used once per game. The Tin Man allows getting additional Conversation card before pivotal event.

This card is a Secret Promo Card for Hostage Negotiator.

boardgame: Mini Madness Cthulhu: The Arkham Investigation
description:
Deep inside the vicious mind of the elder gods, resides the madness of mankind. You are not an ordinary person, have seen many things, gaze at the abyss of your own mind, many must called you liar, crazy, even a fraud, but now, the madness that you spoke of, trapped in the nightmares of your soul, broke free into our world, and bring something ancient, evil... and hungry.

Welcome to the Mini Madness Cthulhu - The Arkham Investigation - a mini 9 card game self published, that have elements of Cthulhu's and other H.P Lovecraft histories, as well cosmic horror creatures and elements.

As a Free PNP (print an Play), Solitaire or cooperative game, your goal is to save the world from being devoured by the Ancient Gods, to do that you must collect artifacts and clues, yet it seems simple. To "win" (survive) the game you'll have to fight the cultists, monster and the "Chaos Die", and be able to stay unharmed and mentally fine.

In this game, you have resources, and have to travel from the cards and resolve it rolling dice. Be aware of each ability from every one of the four investigators. As the game proceed the Ancient God will slowly awaken, and make things more difficult. As you collect the symbols "elder signs" you may have the specific amount of the chosen Elder God, when you do so, you beat the game, and save the world - HURRAY! - but... for how much time?

---

No fundo das mentes viciosas dos Deuses Anciões, reside a loucura da humanidade. Você não é uma pessoa comum, viu muitas coisas, olhou para o abismo da sua própria mente, muitos devem chamar-lhe mentiroso, louco, ou até mesmo uma fraude, mas agora, a loucura de que você falou, presa nos pesadelos de sua alma , se libertou em nosso mundo, e trouxe algo antigo, maligno ... e faminto.

Bem-vindo ao Mini Madness Cthulhu - A Investigação Arkham - um mini-jogo de nove cartas auto publicado, que tem elementos de Cthulhu e outras histórias de H.P Lovecraft, bem como criaturas e elementos de terror cósmico.

Como um PNP gratuito (Imprima e Jogue), jogo solo ou cooperativo, seu objetivo é salvar o mundo de ser devorado pelo Deuses Antigo, para isso você deve coletar artefatos e pistas, parece simples, eu garanto a você que não é. Para "vencer" (sobreviver) o jogo você terá que lutar contra os cultistas, monstros e o "Dado do Caos", e ser capaz de permanecer ileso e mentalmente bem.

Neste jogo, você tem recursos e precisa viajar dos cards resolvê-los rolando os dados. Esteja ciente de cada habilidade de cada um dos quatro investigadores. À medida que o jogo avança, o Deus Antigo despertará lentamente e tornará as coisas mais difíceis. Ao coletar os símbolos "Elder Signs" você pode ter a quantidade específica do Deus Ancião escolhido, quando você faz isso, você vence o jogo e salva o mundo - OBAAAA! - mas ... por quanto tempo?

Have you always dreamt of being one of the thieves in Ocean's Eleven? Or perhaps with participating in the Sting? Would you like to be a cat burglar and live la dolce vitta? Then Theft of the Century is the game for you!

Your mission in Theft of the Century will be to escape the police hunting you at every turn. The police will be faster or slower depending on the cards played by your opponents. With a hand management system where you can draw or play cards to make your thief run faster, that make the police quicker when chasing your rivals, and that will combine with each other to earn you points. Play hidden cards to solve at the end of each round, trump your opponent’s strategies and get the most valuable booty!

The game is played in a variable number of rounds, with an objective card that marks the end of the game. Players will use their cards and solve their effects, up to a maximum of 4 cards each round per player. The player who achieves more objectives will be the winner of the game. Will you be the thief who performs the Theft of the Century?

—description from the publisher

¿Siempre soñaste con ser uno de los ladrones de Ocean’s Eleven? ¿O tal vez con participar en El Golpe? ¿Te gustaría ser un ladrón de guante blanco y vivir la dolce vittta? ¡Entonces El Robo del Siglo es el juego para ti!.

En el Robo del Siglo tu misión será huir de la policía que intentará darte caza en cuanto tenga ocasión. La policía será más rápida o más lenta en función de las cartas que vayan jugando tus compañeros. Con un sistema de gestión de mano de cartas donde podrás robar o jugar cartas que harán que tu ladrón corra más, que la policía persiga más rápido a tus rivales, y que harán combos entre sí para conseguir puntos. Apuesta cartas ocultas para resolver al final de cada ronda, entorpece a los ladrones enemigos y ¡consigue el botín más valioso!.

El juego consta de un número de rondas variables en función de una carta objetivo que marca el final de la partida. Los jugadores irán jugando cartas y resolviendo sus efectos, hasta un máximo de 4 turnos por ronda y por jugador. El jugador que consiga más objetivos será el ganador de la partida ¿Serás tú el ladrón que consiga realizar El Robo del Siglo?

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

User summary
ギャンブルゼブラ (GAMBLE ZEBRA) is a Japanese card game. English rules are on the designer's website.

In the game of GAMBLE ZEBRA, players try to accumulate victory points by betting on black or white.

Each player receives an identical deck of eight bidding cards. Any of these cards can be played in two ways: bid on black with a value of X or bid on white with a value of Y. In any card, X+Y is always 9. For example if the upper half of a card is 8 on black, the lower half is 1 on white. In addition, each player receives two random special bidding cards.

Each round, a victory point card, whose value ranges from -1 to 5, is shown up. Players guess whether the back of the card is black or white, and play two cards from their hands. When the card's color is revealed, the highest bidder on that color takes the victory point card and a random victory point modifier card.

After five rounds, the highest scorer wins the game.

Designer's summary
3つの要素があるカードゲームです。
1つ目、ギャンブル要素：白と黒、どちらに賭けるのか
2つ目、駆け引き要素：カードを2枚出して相手より大きい数字を狙う
3つ目、運要素：駆け引きで勝てても結果が更に変化する

勝利条件：5ラウンド勝負終了後、一番ポイントが多い人が勝ち

October, 1888 — Whitechapel, London: Jack's shadow still hangs heavy over the back-streets of the East End. Scotland Yard has assigned Inspector Abberline to take over the investigation, aided by five new detectives with extraordinary abilities. The new recruits include the formidable Professor Moriarty, who uses his intelligence and cunning to impersonate the other detectives. Is he trying to put Jack off his stride, or to outwit his rival Sherlock Holmes? Or could he even be Jack himself, disguised as Moriarty? Who is the elusive Jack? The unrelenting manhunt continues!

Mr. Jack: Extension is an expansion for the Mr. Jack deduction game that features refreshed artwork for the tenth anniversary of the game as well as an original character as strong as it is enigmatic: Professor Moriarty, whose special ability is to imitate one of the characters who is not at stake.  Moriarty is quite the chameleon, impersonating the other detectives by mimicking their appearance and using their abilities!

This expansion lets you play with six new characters: meet the sultry Madame, flee the terrifying Pizer, avoid the revolutionary Joseph Lane's barricade, dodge Inspector Abberline's probing questions, bound along London's streets with the Spring-Heeled Man, and watch out for Moriarty. At the start of the game, choose which of the eight characters to play with via a draft and determine their starting locations. These new possibilities add fun and strategic depth.

Whether playing as Jack or the Detective, master them all to win!

—description from publisher

Mr. Jack Pocket: Extension, the first expansion for Mr. Jack Pocket, features three new characters and a new detective: Mycroft Holmes with a special ability.

With three new alibis, the game will be played on a large London consisting of twelve areas (street tiles) instead of nine as in the base game. More suspects? The investigation looks harder... Fortunately a new detective comes to lend us a hand: Mycroft, the famous brother of Sherlock Holmes!

—description from publisher

Beginning with a search for two missing Miskatonic University professors, your inquiries in The Dunwich Legacy launched you on a campaign that led from the decrepit streets of Dunwich to the infamous Clover Club and realms beyond. But questions from these strange investigations still linger, compelling you to board the train to Dunwich once more.

Return to the Dunwich Legacy invites you to re-open your old cases and experience the first full-length campaign for Arkham Horror: The Card Game with fresh eyes as you encounter new challenges and new surprises. Across this expansion’s 81 new scenario cards and 23 new player cards, you’ll explore new locations, face more ghastly treacheries, and enhance your investigator’s abilities as you face even more unspeakable terrors. Finally, Return to the Dunwich Legacy also features a premium box and thirty-two divider cards to conveniently store your complete campaign in one place.

description:
Snakes and Ladders: Unleashed is a fast-paced dice game with a continuously evolving board layout and movable snakes and ladders.

Players try to improve their chances of gaining ground faster, whilst at the same time trying to block their opponents’ progress. This is done by placing their cards on the board and moving snakes and ladders around.

Two dice make the game more tactical, and the chain of potential events makes for a fun and sometimes chaotic game.

Dusk is a dark fantasy combat game based around large models, tactical movement, and deck construction mechanics.

Players will field a single Phantom avatar then take turns constructing decks from a selection of Phantom specific gifts, universal skills, and universal weapons. Each option has 9 potential picks of which players will choose 4 cards to add to a basic deck. All basic decks are 13 cards and identical for parity and balance.

Once decks are constructed players will use the numbers on cards to engage in arena combat at exact ranges while managing their hand size and card abilities to leverage their specific construction strategy.

Fighting in teams or (1 v 1) the game ends when you have eliminated your opponents and established your dominance in the Arena of Shadows.

Frontier Badlands is the add-on expansion to Frontier The Card Game!

Frontier Badlands features more player interaction, including dueling, stealing player's supplies using the Lasso, trading every turn, rigorous challenges once you've beaten the game, fun variants, and much more!

When you explore the Badlands, you'll encounter Martians, Ghost Bandits, the Thunderbird, and a slew of other opponents! Manage brand new supplies such as Cattle, the Gatling Gun and even the Martian's Vaporizer! Ward off calamities with Horseshoes, take down enemies with the Shotgun, and earn Legendary Notoriety; they don't call this the Badlands fer' nothin'!

The Flames Of War 'Start Here' booklet is designed to get you playing Flames Of War as soon as possible. Glue your tanks together, grab some dice, a tape measure and an opponent and follow the instructions in this booklet to play a simple game. Once you get the hang of the basic rules in this booklet, you can delve into the full rules and start playing larger, more detailed games.

Once you've played a few games and have a solid grasp of the rules you might find yourself wanting to expand your force with infantry, artillery, aircraft, or even a second unit of tanks! The most fun and tasty game, now playable with up to 4 players! Gather all the different candies and hurry up to be the first to collect the cherry. Test your creativity and create a magical chain of candies to reach the cherry in the middle of the game board. But be aware of your opponents, because when 3 of the same candies connect, your plan will fall apart! Is luck on your side and are you tactical enough to win the candy crush board game?

Candy Crush the board game is a 2 to 4 players strategic board game. The game is played with 6 dice all containing 6 different colors of candies. Players roll all dice a maximum of three times in their turn and try to roll as many of the same colors. Depending on the dice result, players place a number of candy tokens on the board. These can be place either on their own board or that of their opponents. The first player to reach the cherry in the middle of the board by placing enough candy tokens wins the game. But remember that your candy tokens will disappear when an opponent places 3 of the same candies on your board.

-description from publisher

You and your hippo friends have just discovered a restaurant in the jungle. The kitchen is fully stocked and none of those pesky humans are around! As culinary connoisseurs, you decide to take a shot at the menu. However, as you know, too many hippo chefs can ruin the sushi. So choose your meals wisely, and you just might end up with the fattest belly.

Hippos Love Sushi is a bluﬃng/memory game for 2-4 players ages 5 and up, where players take turns adding secret ingredients to Meals. Afterward, each player chooses a Meal to eat. Each player scores 1 point for each correct ingredient in his Meal. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

-description from designer

Gobs of Jobs is a game about earning money and what to do with it! Save it? Spend it? Give it? Yes to all three!

Save up for something big and important. Treat yourself and have some fun. Help others and make a difference.

The goal of the game is to earn the most money and use it for Saving, Spending, and Giving. The player with the highest money total on their cards wins.

Welcome to Gelatinous Cubes Devour The World from Wrectangle Games! In this game, you are finally able to live out your life-long fantasies of being a Gelatinous Cube. Famished from your travels underground, you emerge to devour the most delicious delicacies you can find – people. Travel from village to village eating everything in your path, or test your slimy grit against the heavily fortified Castle. The first player to masticate their way to 500 points wins!

Players create a world map (random each time) then move their Cubes through this world eating the most villagers by rolling dice to beat skirmish cards drawn when entering a village or castle.
