WHAT’S IN THE BOX?
30 People Cards (all of Max, the random dude)
20 Question Cards

    Add to the main deck


—description from the publisher

Some people make and sell bread for a living, while other people make and sell shoes. You, on the other hand, are a witch and you make and sell potions with other witches. You and the other witches are competing against each other to be the first to make each potion. Each potion requires a specific balance of ingredients. All you have to do is add in the right final ingredient to make the potion, and the potion is yours! Whoever has the most potions when the game is over wins.
​
Witchful Thinking is a mathematics-based card game of mixing ingredients of different numerical values in a cauldron to produce a potion of equal value. Each Ingredient card has a number ranging from -5 to +5 and each Potion card has a number ranging from -19 to +18. Players race to adjust the formula in the cauldron by adding ingredients until the correct potion value is made. Once a player has the correct value, they win that potion!

—description from the publisher

In a land full of darkness, the cruel Dragon rules over all. With its evil army doing its bidding, the Dragon causes nothing but pain and suffering to the people. The Dragon has built up its vast army over the decade, ruling over the land as it sits atop its colossal tower. The people know nothing but fear and dread. That is until 4 Guardians of the realm were mysteriously sent: a valiant Knight, a cunning Archer, a great Mage, and a wise Cleric. No one knows for sure who sent them or where they came from, but together they are the only hope to slay the Dragon. Will they be enough?

Ready to save the world?

Defeat Monsters to level up and your Guardian allies have enough Magic and Health to defeat the dreaded Dragon. When the Dragon is defeated, the game is won!

Players win by saving the kingdom by defeating the evil dragon at the top of the tower before the Dragon's forces destroy the characters.  
Each turn the players use spells like fireballs, pinning arrows, healing or taunting, or attack monsters with their abilities until they are defeated and the next monster emerge. Players control their guardians and dictate what magic to use, who to attack, and customization of their skill trees

Each player sends an umbrella down the stream. 
Who will manage to get his umbrella through the finish line first?

Playing Materials: 1 board, 5 umbrella cards, 5 card holders, 5 colored chips, 3 colored dice, 1 instruction

Winnie the Pooh: Umbrella Race is a racing board game. 
The main goal in Winnie the Pooh: Umbrella Race is for a player’s umbrella to finish first.
Before the game starts, each of the 5 different color umbrellas(red, blue, green, yellow and purple) must be placed on a starting position(the order is randomly selected). Each umbrella is represented by a color chip(each color chip matched an umbrella). The chips are all turned face down, so their color are hidden and then shuffled. Each player takes a chip and looks at the hidden color it represents. All players must keep their chips face down in order to keep their umbrella hidden. The game then starts with the younger player’s turn. The player takes the three colored dice and rolls them. Then he or she must move each umbrella according to the colors that came up in the dice. The umbrellas can be moved only forward and they can’t pass over another umbrella or finish on another’s umbrella’s position. After the first player moves the selected umbrellas, then he passes the dice to the second younger player. The next players turn starts. The game ends when all the umbrellas enter one of the last blocks. Then the chips are turned up and their colors are revealed. The player whose chip represent the umbrella which finished first is the winner.

The game has taken great inspiration from Ravensburger Snail's Pace Race.

Be the fastest to imitate the sound and the gesture of the biggest animal! An original game of war that mixes mimes and sounds of animals!
—description from the publisher (translated)

Soit le plus rapide à imiter le cri et la position de l'animal le plus gros ! Un jeu de bataille original qui mêle mimes et cris d'animaux !
—description from the publisher

description:
At the bottom of the vast lands of Mornifle, is a legendary place... The dungeon of BOURPIF!
You are valiant adventurers in search of fame who will cross the dungeon of Bourpif.
Your goal? Get out with the most fame points, i.e. the most cards.
Test your observation skills, dexterity and speed to become a renowned adventurer!
If you don't have any of his skills then ruuuuuuun!

Au fin fond des vastes terres de la Mornifle, se trouve un lieu aussi malfamé que légendaire... Le Donjon de Bourpif !

Bugman Battle is The World's First Party Game With BUGS. With simple rules and edgy settings, Bugman Battle enables strong interaction between players. Let’s battle and fill your parties with a fun experience.

The goal of the game is simple: Challenge your opponents and make them eat all of their own bugs!

You will get a funny game, yummy snacks and creative package at one time. Our colorful package is designed referencing the shape of the beetle. Hope you like it!

—description from the designer

The new Connection game. Players sit side by side with the board in front of them. Players choos colors and roll dice to decide who starts. Each Player, alternately, rolls the dice and places a Base-Piece on any one of the Sites defined (bracketed) by the 2 numbers that appear on the dice, either above and below, or on both sides of the Site. If all the player's stock of Base-Pieces is already placed on the Board it is possible to Reposition a Base-Piece to the new Site - according to the throw of the dice. Bases under Bridging-Sections can't be removed. A Player unable to place a Base, because all the Sites of the numbers rolled are captures, loses that turn. A "Double" number on the dice earns the Player another turn. A Player may pass his or her turn if numbers are not suitable.

—user summary

The realistic football game for strategists and tacticians. The purpose of the game is to score as many goals as possible through clever play. The players are placed on the corresponding red squares according to the usual football system (4-2-4 or similar). Each team in their half. Each player receives a dice. First is rolled around the ball. Who dices the higher number, receives the ball and thus the impetus. The ball is placed on the base plate of a figure on the center circle. Then both players roll their own die. First, the ball-leading team always pulls, then the opponent. The rolled eyes determine the number of steps of the footballers. The player can draw one or more figures in all directions over free points on the black lines. The defending player can take the ball from the opponent. All football rules such as offside, penalty area, ball, corner or penalty are explained in the rules.

—user summary

boardgame: The Expanse Boardgame: Doors and Corners
description:
The Expanse Boardgame: Doors and Corners expansion contains five new modules — Leaders, New Tech, Resource Tokens, Protomolecule, and Variable Setup — that add an engaging element to the strategy and gameplay as players compete for dominance in the world of The Expanse. You can use any number of modules in any combination in conjunction with the base game.

Leaders go with fleets and can add influence when they move with a fleet. New tech allows variety and surprise in what powers are available because when a tech is earned, you have three random choices for that tech. Resource tokens can be spent in several ways, including helping to pay CP costs, adding up to 4 AP to a card, moving up the initiative track, and earning bonus points for having the most at final scoring. Protomolecule adds a new scoring opportunity. Variable set-up allows fleets and influence to be placed anywhere at the start of the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A letter game with luck (roulette) and creativity, quick thinking and imagination. One of the players turns the roulette wheel and throws in all 6 bullets. The letters, groups of letters and free fields marked by the 6 balls when the wheel is stationary are called by the game master. All players now occupy the corresponding fields on their board with their chips. Freifelder allow the choice and use of any letter. Now we turn the hourglass and each player now tries as quickly as possible to form a common noun before the clock expires. The more letters or groups in a word, the higher the scoring. The winner is the one who has scored the most points after an agreed playing time or after a certain number of laps.

—user summary

Germania 250 CE

When the Roman province of Raetia was invaded by Alemanni tribes, refugees fled through forests and mountains to the safety of the city of Brigantium?

Fast moving race and dice game.

A die roll determines the movement of your refugees over land tiles. Your supplies should be used carefully to change your luck. Will you have the bribe needed to get past the guards at the city gates?

RAETIA IS ONLY AVAILABLE AS PART OF THE ANCIENT WORLD MULTI GAME SYSTEM FROM PLAYFORD GAMES.

—description from the designer

The battle of Molins de Rey was a battle that took place on december, 21st 1808 near Llobregat River close to Barcelona (Spain). It opposed French forces of general St.Cyr to the Spanish forces of general von Reading.

This game would be a Jours de Gloire series game.

The Spanish army tries to maintain a position apparently strong but with a weak flank. The French must try to coordinate a flank attack with a frontal attack to try to capture the entire enemy army. Historically the Spaniards were defeated but they avoided being surrounded.

The different divisions are activated by chits drawn from a cup, but the activation does not guarantee that the player does everything he wants.

The turns are 1 hour and the game lasts a maximum of 10 turns.

Rules: Jours de Gloire 
Scale: 1 hex. = 330 m - 1 counter = 1 regiment/battaillon
Map size: A2

Spanish army: 15.000 men - 21 counters
French army: 25.000 men - 21 Counters

description:
In Town League Hockey, YOU are a newly arrived General Manager of a team in the Town Hockey League.
Somewhere in the far north... hidden among the great Canadian wilderness... where the snow falls year round, the bitter cold bites your face, and the sound of the wolves in the forest lull you to sleep at night... lies the hidden hamlet of Town.

Town is a humble, kind, hard-working community, but once a year, the people of Town become rivals... and they compete in an annual hockey tournament for the prestigious Frosty Cup! In Town League Hockey, you will control either the Gold or Silver team and guide your players to the ultimate prize!

Town League Hockey is a mid-weight game about playing a match of hockey. It was created to fill this niche because I found all hockey board games were either very light or very heavy. Town League Hockey is simple enough to learn in a few minutes, but deep enough that you will be mulling over your choices as you decide how to respond to on-ice events, where to skate with the puck to get the best shot, and when to rest or play your best players.

—description from the designer

User summary
マギアルマ (Magiarma) is a Japanese speed, dice rolling, dexterity game. Players must construct their chosen weapons by stacking small cubes in the form shown on the weapon card. Once this is completed, the weapon is activated for the fight.

Gameplay summary - 
This real time dexterity game tasks players with protecting the kingdom of Charme from Apocalys.

Each player chooses one of the 5 witches and then takes the corresponding character card and die. Mana cubes in five colors are placed within easy reach of all players. The game then proceeds through three rounds consisting of: Preparation, battle, and totaling points.

Preparation consists of laying out the enemy character for the round along with their damage cubes and each player taking a weapon card in their color. Four stacks of weapon cards are then placed on table. After one player shouts ""magiarma"", play moves onto the battle phase. Players then race to grab weapon cards, build them using the mana cubes, and then roll their die to inflict damage on the current enemy. The battle comes to a close when all the enemy damage cubes have been collected by the players. Points are then awarded to the players based on the number of damage cubes they collected.

The player who has amassed the most points is then named the winner.

Publisher's summary
マナクリスタルにより人々が生活を営んでいる魔法使いの国、シャルム王国。この国に突如として世界を終末へと向かわせる敵、「アポカリス」が現れた。このシャルム王国を救うために若き５人の魔法使い達が立ち上がる。

プレイヤーは魔法使いの1人となり、対アポカリス必殺用の武器を魔法で生成し、「アポカリス」にダメージを与えろ！「アポカリス」にトドメの一撃を刺せば多くの勝利点が与えられる。一番勝利点を稼ぎ、この国の英雄となったプレイヤーがこのゲームの勝者だ！！

Description from the publisher:

It’s a lovely sunny morning with Dad smoking his pipe and reading the Sunday paper, Mom cooking bacon in the kitchen, Billy shooting everything in sight with his Yellow Flyer BB gun™ and Sally combing her porcelain dolly’s hair.  But then the silence is broken by the blare of the local air-raid siren.  Who knows what this could mean?

Will the Reds invade? 
Will the Atomic Bomb Drop?
Or, perhaps, THIS IS ONLY A TEST?

Find out as you and your friends jockey for supplies and nudge the doomsday clock ever closer to the brink!

GAMEPLAY

"This Is Only A Test" is a pre-Apocalyptic game of civil defense. Panic is building and you must negotiate for supplies in anticipation of three unique endings. bomb. Players can use there actions to investigate the timeline, or (better yet) manipulate it to press for there preferred ending by visiting City Hall.  Each location in City Hall allows players to manipulate the timeline in different ways.  Want the bomb to drop?  Visit the Mayor's office and start adding Bomb cards to the timeline!   Here's the catch those same actions are used to gather supplies to win.  So, while you're out ensuring the bomb will drop, the other players may be gathering consumables to put in there fallout shelter!

When the game ends, the player who has prepared the best, for the correct ending will win! So, if the Atomic bomb drops, you had better have the most supplies in your Fallout shelter, but if the Reds invade, self-defense items will rule the day.  What happens if the Bomb doesn't drop, or the Reds don't invade?  Well, This is Only a Test, of course!  And, the golden rule applies - the person with the most glorious riches wins!

WHY IT'S UNIQUE

"This Is Only A Test" uses a unique timeline mechanic that keeps the players in suspense as the game races towards an unknown conclusion at an unknown time.  The timeline may contain the Red Invasion card or the Atomic Bomb card.  Then again, it may not.

WHO IT'S FOR

If you love suspense and hidden information with a dash of bluffing, this is the fallout shelter for you.  Great for 1-4 friends who love to outwit one another in 45-60 mins.

THE AIR RAID SIREN IS SOUNDING!

Will you stockpile heaps of supplies, defend yourself to the teeth, or simply profit from other’s paranoia? The Red Army looms! The A-bomb is armed!  Is it time to duck and cover? Or perhaps..."This Is Only A Test".

boardgame: Draconis Invasion: Hero Pledge Promo Cards
description:
Cards that were exclusive to the Hero level pledge

6x Resurrection
6x Summoning
6x Consuming Fire

6x Beast Master
6x Death Angel
6x Berserker (Secret Set)
6x Sky Rider (Secret Set)

1x Blood Dragon (Secret Set)

30 Defender and Action cards from the 2015 Kickstarter. They were later available as an add-on during the Kickstarter for Draconis Invasion: Wrath.

Includes:
6x Wandering Sword
6x Varcolac
6x Against All Odds
6x Spirit of A Hero
6x Dragon Crusade

boardgame: Draconis Invasion: Victory Pledge Promo Cards
description:
6x Transmutation
6x Born of Flame
6x Reanimation
6x Forbidden Quest
6x Dark Forces (Secret Set)

6x Golden Warriors
6x Frost Knight
6x Tree Ent
6x Iron Guardian (Secret Set)
6x Black Sword (Secret Set)

6x Fallen Sky
6x Strange Magic (Secret Set)

boardgame: Draconis Invasion: Warrior Pledge Promo Cards
description:
These cards were originally available as part of the Warrior pledge level during the original Kickstarter for Draconis Invasion.

They were later made available as an add-on during the Draconis Invasion: Wrath Kickstarter.

Includes:
6x Call to Arms
6x Soul Flame
6x Panda Scout
6x Dragon Hunter
6x Elder Stone (Foil)

In June 2018, Scythe fans were invited to make a design for ONE encounter card using specific art. Jamey reviewed the submissions, selected his favorites (often mixing and matching various submissions), and developed them. Now the Scythe Encounters promo boxed set is a reality!

Scythe Encounters is a boxed set of 32 brand-new promo encounter cards. These cards feature a number of innovations in the encounter system. We recommend that you play with them on their own for a few games; after that you can shuffle them into the regular encounter deck.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Land of the Two Rivers: Iraq War
description:
Land of the Two Rivers is a game of the Iraq War in the post-invasion period starting roughly in Summer 2003. By this point Saddam's government had disintegrated and fateful decisions had already been made regarding official U.S. policy towards powerful factions within Iraq. The players are faced with the situation as it existed at that time, as the Sunni insurgency was building momentum, the Shia were taking advantage of the chaos to flex their long-suppressed muscle, and everyone was realizing there were insufficient U.S. forces to police the entire country. Regional and global entities like Syria, Turkey, Iran and Al Qaeda were attempting to shift events in their favor. The game does not provide the ability to alter what had already been done up to that point, but each player has options on how to conduct operations going forward.

The game is designed for three players, representing:

1. United States, British, Other Coalition, and Iraqi National Forces (INF)
2. Sunnis (Iraqi Sunnis and their sponsors in the larger Sunni world)
3. Shiites (Iraqi Shia and their sponsors in Iran)

The U.S./Coalition objective is to stand up the Iraqi army in sufficient strength and effectiveness to control the majority of the country, with a small number of supporting U.S./Coalition troops.

The Sunni and Shiite objectives are to gain control over enough of the population to give them a decisive advantage in the eventual political and economic solution that must one day be achieved.

Players are free to cooperate and make (or break) any agreements they wish. However, there can only be one winner, so ultimately one player must outmaneuver the other two.

The game has rules for U.S. Morale and Commitment similar to Nick Karp's classic "Vietnam: 1965-1975", training of Iraqi National Forces (INF), Ground Attacks, IED Cells, Ambushes, Air Strikes, Al Qaeda unpredictability, Suicide Attacks, Coalition country withdrawals, Civilian Casualties, and more. There are sub-systems for the hunt for High Value Targets (HVTs), Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMDs), and Infiltration of support from regional players.

-description from designer

Serpents is a tarot sized, secret identity, card game. Serpents puts a fresh spin on classic secret-identity games like Mafia and Werewolf. Play using our rules out of the box, or throw them out and use your own. Our team has taken measures to ensure this deck is flexible enough to play a range of unique hidden-identity games — known or as yet unwritten — and allows you to cater any existing version to your liking. Mix and match your favorite roles and start lying to your closest friends and family.

One player will serve as The Fortune Teller. Remaining players will either be Innocents, striving collectively for survival using logic and deduction, or Serpents, fork-tongued deceivers, eliminating travelers from the game using pretense and lies. Only the Fortune Teller knows the true identity of each player. He alone will attend the players on their journey, acting as both moderator and story-teller.

"On the frozen steppes of Dawn, ALEPH has deployed one of her forward bases to fight against the Combined Army invaders. However, the Ariadnans suspect that it is just the first stage of a plot of the AI to increase her influence over their planet, and they will not allow that!"

This box contains all the miniatures, rules, and terrain you need to play Infinity Operation: Coldfront, and introduce you to Infinity 3rd edition.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mr. Cabbagehead is Away and Neddance Saves the Day
description:
A solitaire postcard game designed by Todd Sanders.

"Mr. Cabbagehead is away on one of his holidays. Neddance has stayed behind to protect Mr. C’s garden from his annoying neighbours and to make sure they do not leave with any vegetables. But alas, for each neighbour she sends on their way, the others become even more determined to snatch a vegetable. Neddance is perfectly polite with all the neighbours but even she has a limit to her patience and only so many excuses she can divert them with."

In Mr. Cabbagehead is Away and Neddance Saves the Day, you play the role of Neddance, trying to defend Mr. Cabbagehead's garden from his ever-so-tiresome Neighbours.

To play the game you need to supply two dice and a standard deck of cards.

In order to win Neddance must send away each of the 8 Neighbours before her Patience reaches 0.

There are 3 phases in each round.

Phase 1: Neighbours Move Through The Garden.
Roll one die and the result is the total number of spaces one or more Neighbours will move towards Neddance.

Phase 2: Neighbours Now Pester Neddance, While She Tries To Maintain Her Composure.
Each Neighbour that reaches Neddance will Pester her in turn by rolling one die plus one more die for each Neighbour already sent away. Then Neddance must keep her Composure and send the Neighbour away by playing cards with higher total value, otherwise she loses Patience. If her Patience reaches 0 the game is lost.

After sending away certain Neighbours, Neddance gains confidence and can make more excuses in order to send the rest of the Neighbours away.

Phase 3: Neddance Is On The Move, Looking For Neighbours To Send Away.
If Neddance has less than 3 cards left, she regains all her discarded cards and then plays 1-3 cards in order to move.

At the end of each round Neddance's Patience is decreased by one.

Oddbods Go-Kards is a new take on the 'family' game genre. It's a family game for 'gamer' families with multiple games in one box.

At its most basic, it is a play-a-card-and-move-the-meeple party race game that lasts 10 to 15 minutes and can be picked up in a few minutes even by small children. The game has players perform fun real-life social challenges to reap in-game rewards.

In its more advanced forms, it features tactical take-that and power-up cards, modular and constructable tracks, a variety of rule-altering pit-stops and an emergent power-up marketplace. Players can setup and duke it out in races that take up to an hour or even more to finish.

The game is based on Oddbods, a Singaporean-British CGI-animated comedy television series produced by the Singapore-based studio One Animation.

The series focuses on seven different, sometimes battling characters called the Oddbods - Fuse, Pogo, Zee, Jeff, Bubbles, Slick and Newt.

-description from designer

Our century is an original game for all who love good fun! You practice your logical thinking, memory and concentration. It is intended for 2-6 players and the length of one game is usually 30 minutes or more. You can examine your knowledge in five basic areas: the Czech Century, food, culture, places of interest and various.
The game was released on the occasion of the celebration of the 100th anniversary of Czechoslovakia by Albi and Albert.

Game preparation: Put the game board onto the table. Each player takes a meeple of one color and places it on START. Place the decks with knowledge and tipping questions on the marked place on the plan.
Game objective: The game objective is to pass all the fields on the board and the first to reach the finish field.
Game progress: The youngest player starts and then the players turn clockwise. The player on the left of the drawer draws the card from the top of the question pile. The player on the turn throws a dice and the player on his left reads the question and three possible answers from a topic whose color matches the color of the dice. If the color is purple, the player can choose the topic himself. If he answers correctly, he moves his meeple forward, if he is wrong, he stands.

Naše století je originální hra pro všechny, kteří milují dobrou zábavu! Perfektně si s ní procvičíte logické myšlení, paměť i soustředění. Určena je pro 2-6 hráčů a délka jedné hry je obvykle 30 a více minut. Své vědomosti můžete prověřit v pěti základních okruzích: české století, jídlo, kultura, zajímavá místa a různé. 
Hra byla vydána u příležitosti oslav 100 výročí Československa firmami Albi a Albert.

Příprava hry: Rozložte herní plán na stůl. Každý hráč si vezme figurku jedné barvy a umístí ji na START. Na vyznačené místo na plánu položte balíčky s vědomostními a tipovacími otázkami.
Cíl hry: Cílem hry je projít všechna políčka na herním plánu a jako první dosáhnout políčka CÍL.
Průběh hry: Nejmladší hráč začíná a poté se hráči střídají po směru hodinových ručiček. Hráč po levici hráče na tahu lízne kartu z vrchu balíčku s otázkami. Hráč na tahu hodí kostkou a hráč po jeho levici mu přečte otázku a tři možné odpovědi z okruhu, jehož barva odpovídá barvě určené kostkou. Padne-li fialová, hráč si muže okruh vybrat sám. Pokud odpoví správně, posune svoji figurku vpřed, pokud špatně, stojí.

This a rare and unusual Japanese game about the European theatre published during WWI.

It is a roll-and-move game of maneuver for 2-6 players, pitting Germany, Austria-Hungary and Italy against France, Britain and Russia.

The former start in the upper left of the board, and the latter in the lower right. Various tracks connect the two, and the objective is to capture the opponent's headquarters. Each of the countries starts with two playing pieces, whose initial placement space is determined by a die roll.

This Edo-era (early 1800s) game is perhaps the earliest to focus on the famous story of the 47 Ronin or Chūshingura (The Treasury of Loyal Retainers).

It is a Mawari-Sugoroku style of game, with die rolls determing movement around the board. Each panel illustrates an episode in the story of the group of samurai who were declared ronin after their master Asano Takumi-no-kami Naganori, the daimyō of Akō, was forced to commit seppuku by the Shogun in 1702.

They eventually got revenge by killing their former master's treacherous opponent Kira Kōzuke-no-suke Yoshinaka, and then turned themselves in to the authorities knowing that they in turn would be forced into seppuku, which they did as a group.

The game contains two variants: 1. Liberation Game, 2. Happy Families (a quartet game). The first variant involves the following:
It is an exciting game for 2 players. The cards are dealt as follows: one player uses the carts with the red backs and the other player the cards with the blue backs. Each player thus receives 26 cards. Each player arranges the cards in 4 rows of 6 cards and below this a fifth row consisting of 2 special cards (the "Magic Potion" and the "Normans"). All cards are placed face down. The aim of the game is to keep Britannia out of Roman Hands and find the magic potion as fast as possible!

Throw the die and turn over a card from your opponent's first row. If this card matches the symbol on the die, you can remove this card to clear a path to the magic potion. If the symbol and card do not match, turn the card face down again. Play now passes to your opponent. You can place "steps" horizontally, vertically and diagonally. You and your opponent take turns to roll the die and, if you get lucky, remove a card. But watch out for the "Julius Caesar" Cards!

Five Goblin Pirates, marooned on a deserted island by treacherous crewmates, cobble together a raft and set off towards home through the Goblund Gauntlet, a dangerous pass full of sharp rocks, shipwrecks and merciless fellow pirates. Surviving the trip would be great. Collecting a big pile of loot would be better.

Goblins, Guns and Grog is a solitaire roll-and-write adventure game in which players attempt to navigate a treacherous sea route to make it to the mainland. Each turn, the player rolls dice to "Scavenge" (add items to the inventory), "Attack" (if in possession of cannons), "Loot" (in case of a successful attack), "Move" (along the map track, always straight forward towards the destination), "Adventure" (if the turn has ended on one of the X's scattered along the track). Every turn ends with eating, depleting the meager store of food that, if exhausted, causes the player to lose the game.

Arriving at the end of the track is somewhat easy and is nominally considered a "win." But points are only scored for looting the many ships along the way. To engage with these potential prizes, however, the player needs both cannons (to attack) and chests (to store loot). The catch? At the start of the game, the player's "ship" is large enough only to hold a pair of cannons. The ship can be expanded through adding Tetris-like pieces to the hull, slowly expanding the size until the raft has become a formidable warship. But focusing on acquiring lumber, cannons, and chests means passing up opportunities to acquire food. Players must therefore decide when to push their luck and when to play it safe.

Will you be satisfied to return to the mainland, safely and with a fully belly? Or will you risk it all to answer the siren call of gold?


Released in the March 2018 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

Classes are almost out at Cliffspire Academy. As a first year Arcana student, your final exam is to create a functional spell scroll. The more impressive and varied the spells, the higher your marks.

Of course you can't get your cauldron tuned quite right, and your quill of inscribing is on the fritz, and what does "Aveum" mean again?

Spellcraft Academy is a solitaire roll-and-write game played on a grid of letters. The player rolls a die to navigate the grid, selecting letters and copying them on a separate, blank grid. Points are scored for each completed word, chosen out of a word bank. The words in the bank are classified as either "aspects," "elements," or "methods," and combining them -- having one word use letters from another -- is the way to score big points.

After there's no space left on the grid to inscribe more letters, the game ends, and points are tallied. A grading rubric tells the player whether they've passed the test... or failed, and need to take it again.


Released in the April 2018 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

You are Citan the Gold, Dragon guardian of Merchants and Bankers.

There isn’t a coin of gold, silver, tin or jade in all of Dsyx that hasn’t been touched by your subtle hands. You had a busy day of treasure counting planned. Until the blasted heroes showed up looking to take it from you.

Dragonvault is a solitare roll-and-write game in which players take on the role of a dragon protecting its keep from treasure-seeking adventurers. Each turn, the player will roll dice to place traps at strategic locations along a winding path. At regular intervals, heroes (chosen by a die roll from a table) will show up and proceed along the path, triggering the player's defenses. Each hero has a special skill, however, giving them resistances and abilities to better thwart the beleaguered dragon.

Some heroes will fall; some will make it to the treasure store. After the time track has been exhausted, the player is scored based on how much treasure remains!


Released in the May 2018 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

The Mesozooic: Triassic Mini Expansion introduces three new advanced cards types, all of which utilize the beautifully-sculpted topiary found on certain basic cards as well as on every card back. Build cafés near shady topiary, place lovingly-cultivated conservatories, and utilize skilled groundskeepers to provide a touch of elegant flora to your dino zoos.

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.  All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.  Everything else can be printed from this ruleset. 
A player uses a flight of four aircraft represented by two counters.  Aircraft counters move by means of curved "performance rulers" that represent the aircraft's historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
Each performance ruler is unique to each aircraft, and contains all the speed, cornering, and weapon information needed.
TAC is designed for portability; the entire game can fit in a plastic bag. Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

The CR.42 Falco was one of the last biplanes used in combat. Extremely agile, Falchi were Although the Regia Aeronautica replaced the Falco with monoplanes like the MC.200, these biplanes continued to serve throughout the war.
The Falco was also exported to nations such as Belgium, Sweden, and Hungary.
Μαγνητικό Πάκμαν (Magnetic Pacman) is a fun 2 player roll and move game themed after a great 80's hit Pacman.

Roll the foam Lucky Ducky Dice and add up your ducks... highest total after three rounds wins. Try for special 'duck combos' for added bonus points!

History of rubber ducks included.

MAUW: Reactions adds three new creatures to the game, with different effects:

     The Kiwi lets you steal a cat Warrior from your opponent, and use it right away.
     The Ninox lets you take a Spirit cat from an opponent's stack, and place it flipped to the Mauw side in your area.
     The Tuatura lets you ward off an attack from an opponent trying to take one of your Warriors, and you get the attacking card in your hand.


Each card has 4 copies in this expansion, adding 12 cards to the game.

boardgame: 精霊回路ドライヴEXE (Spirit Circuit Drive EXE)
description:
User summary
精霊回路ドライヴEXE (Spirit Circuit Drive EXE) (Seirei Kairo Doraibu EXE) is a new professional publication by Hobby Base in Japan of the original doujin game 精霊回路ドライヴ (Seirei Kairo Drive). The development of the base game (its own 3rd edition was in 2016) includes:

     English rules and card list included.
     12 unit cards added.
     1 enemy card added.
     2 boss cards added.


The rules set has been slightly modified and the balance between the cards has been adjusted. A set of rules for a short game have been introduced, and a hard mode included. The artwork is slightly altered and some components are changed.

The publisher says "Spirit Circuit Drive EXE, the publisher edition, is quite different from the doujin editions. Especially, the number of unit cards, the core of the game, greatly increased from 20 to 32, substantially influencing the game play. In addition, English rules and card list are included for overseas players. Spirit Circuit Drive EXE is produced as another game and not another edition. Nonetheless, there are no major differences in the rules, number of players, victory conditions, etc. Also, all doujin editions (including the expansions) are currently out of print. If new expansions are to be released in the future, they are likely to be for the EXE edition."

NOTE : whilst the game includes English rules, there is still substantive JP text on the cards themselves.

Publisher's summary
The players choose unit cards of various spirits to form parties. Each unit card has unique effects and can be played in various combinations.

Holding circuit cards with numbers 0 to 4 and 4 colors as your hands, play the cards with numbers and colors matching those of the cards in the play area. If you have a unit card matching the played card in your party, that unit will attack the enemy.

Thus, play cards to attack and destroy the enemies that appear one after another. You can also gain magical power by card plays and use that power to activate your unit's special ability as well as raise the unit's level to lead the battle advantageously. The enemies' final boss is very powerful. The players need to cooperate and support each other for victory.

プレイヤーは様々な精霊のユニットカードを何枚か選んでパーティーを結成します。
ユニットカードはそれぞれ固有の効果を持っていて、様々な組み合わせで遊べます。

ゲームでは、0～4の数字と４つの色が描かれた回路カードを手札に持ち、場札の数字や色と同じものをプレイします。
プレイしたカードと同じ色のユニットカードが自分のパーティーにいれば、そのユニットが敵に攻撃を行います。

そうやってカードをプレイして、次々と現れる敵に攻撃を行って撃破していきます。 
またカードプレイによって魔力を獲得でき、その魔力を使ってユニットごとの特殊能力を使ったりユニット自身のレベルを上げていくことで戦いを有利に展開できます。
最後に登場する敵ボスキャラクターは非常に強力で、プレイヤー同士で協力・サポートし、力を合わせて勝利を目指してください。
artists: Jewel Saver FREE (ジュエルセイバーFREE)

description:
4tec is a classic 3D 4 in a row board game that was invented by John Dunne. The aim of the game is to match 4 counters of the same colour in a row in any direction diagonally, vertically, or horizontally. The first person to match 4 in a row wins the game. Like chess and checkers, this game is an instant classic and although new, it will feel very familiar. The game has 32 red and 32 yellow counters included. It is easy to assemble, easy to play, and there are billions of combinations to get to 4 in a row. This skillful game will provide you with hours of fun and entertainment. 
     
It is also known as Qubic and its various versions.

Six Sided Summoner is a cooperative tactics game for 1-4 players.
Players control a group of Guardians that must defend a light crystal from waves of AI controlled Shadows.

Shadows are summoned in waves at one end of the board and move down lanes toward your crystal in the style of moba video games. When a new wave spawns, modifiers are added to the shadows that make them more powerful as the game goes on.

Players defend their crystal (represented by a set of tiles at one end of the board) using guardians, each of which have unique movement types and abilities. All Guardians can attack by rolling a six sided die, but their special abilities make them suited to different roles. Some guardians can lay traps for shadows to fall into, others can heal allies, others focus on damage and maneuverability. While these are powerful, they are also limited, so you must use them strategically. Try to manipulate the board to slow down enemies and maximize your damage.

Abilities and shadow modifiers are determined randomly at the start of the game,so every game is going to play a little differently.

Additionally, while the game is focus on cooperative play, it also includes a 2 player competitive mode in which both players defend their own crystals from each other.

—description from the publisher

Fetus Feud takes you into the womb for a fast paced strategic battle to be the first born. Players take turns playing cards like Ultrasound, Overdue Baby and Pregnancy Cravings to collect contraction cards. Whoever has the most contraction cards when the Labor card is played is "crowned" the winner.

The deck contains attack, defense, and special cards that players can use to attack fellow wombmates, defend themselves and draft their hand. The key is to end the game by playing the Labor or Induce Labor card when you think you have enough contraction cards to win.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Malifaux: The Undying – Story Encounter & Adventure Box
description:
A bitter rivalry spills out onto the streets of Malifaux in the Undying! Will you help Minako Rei defeat the dreaded Manos, or will you harness the immortal necromancer's power for your own?

This is a Resurrectionists/Ten Thunder Story Encounter Box Set, containing eight 32mm 'heroic' scale miniatures made of high-quality plastic, and Stat Cards.

Set Contains:

Adventures

2x Malifaux Encounters
    1x through the Breach Adventure

Models
4 Ten Thunders Models
  ‣ 1 Minako Rei
  ‣ 1 Wanyudo
  ‣ 2 Katashiro
4 Resurrectionist Models
  ‣ 1 Manos the Risen
  ‣ 1 Grave Golem
  ‣ 2 Mourners

Stat Cards

1x Minako Rei
    1x Wanyudo
    2x Katashiro
    1x Manos the Risen
    1x Grave Golem
    2x Mourner

Please Note: Miniatures supplied unpainted and some assembly will be required.

Charm Warders are powerful Chi users who have dedicated their lives to warding off evil spirits. By harnessing and focusing their Chi, they are able to create mystical barriers that prevent spirits from passing. The most powerful of their number can even create powerful Chi Blades, which can cut through many of the defences of otherworldly creatures.

Since last year's Battle of London, some Charm Warders have felt the barriers between the worlds weakening. These men and women have travelled to Malifaux in search of some way to repair the damage being done to the world.

As with most spellcasters, the magical talents of the charm warders have increased significantly since they passed through the Breach. Most end up selling their services to the Ten Thunders in exchange for the ability to study the Oni, ghosts, and other strange creatures at their beck and call.

As a group, the charm Warders are starting to realise that Malifaux itself might be the problem. They have started to research a way to strengthen the barrier between worlds in the hopes of saving Earth... even if it means the loss of Malifaux.

The resurgence of magic on Earth since the opening of the first Breach has led to some odd events. Perhaps one of the strangest was the sudden awakening of the Obsidian Statue that guarded a small, forgotten temple in Tibet. The statue could only move its mouth, but even this was enough to draw pilgrims from far away lands.

Shenlong, upon learning of the statue, arranged for the statue to be brought to Malifaux. It was a massive undertaking, but the eventual journey through the breach completed the awakening and granted the statue full movement.

Now, the Obsidian Statue serves those who gave it life. It owes them a debt that can never be repaid... and the Ten Thunders know this.

Theme
Caution: This is not just a game, it is a LIFE STORY.

You have a stable job, but also notice that there are lots of hidden opportunities around you, and you cannot wait to show to the world achievements you can make. To be a famous Business Giant? Or to be a wealthy Property Tycoon? How much can you accomplish within a decade? They are all of your choices. Occupation, skills, opportunities and so on of each Player are different but remember, there is one thing always fair to everyone - time. Make the best use of your time, in this limited life, and make all your dreams come true!

Game-play
Every player will receive a set of career card, a player board and 11 time tokens. Players can spend time on the following actions each turn:-
Training: Increase one's skill level so to get a promotion to increase the salary
Opportunity: Pick an Opportunity Card which has various useful effects
Start a Business: Pick a business card and start it to earn more income
Upgrade a Business: Upgrade a business card to make it more profitable
Purchase Property: Pick a property card and decide whether to buy it
Sell Property: Roll a dice and decide whether to sell a property on one's table
Remaining time tokens will be used on sleeping, gathering and travelling to adjust one's Pressure Index.

Goal
By managing one's time and money, Players need to achieve as many Dreams as possible, and at the same time try to form a family while not to get over-pressurized. These all constitute to Players' Life Satisfaction Point and the one with the highest win the game.

boardgame: By This Axe: Medieval Fantasy Battle Rules
description:
By This Axe: Medieval Fantasy Battle Rules presents a simple, fairly standard wargame ruleset to handle small- to medium-size miniatures battles (optional rules allow play without miniatures).  The rules assume two or more armies will be involved, each with two or more units.  Infantry units contain 3 to 25 figures; cavalry units contain 2 to 15 figures; figures may be arranged as desired but only up to the first three ranks are involved in combat.

The game assumes 1:5 to 1:20 scale ratios though any type of (consistent) basing is allowed.  Action resolution uses a modified six-sided-dice resolution method; 1 is successful, 6 is failure.  Unit movement takes place in inches; various terrain effects are noted.  The mass combat is fairly abstracted.

An optional "Command and Control" method relies upon card drafting from a standard pack.  The standard command method assigns a Leadership level which limits actions.

The game does support individual figures that may represent generals, leaders, and "characters" (generally - mages, priests, etc.); these figures may duel each other.  The game does support (but does not require) fantastic elements and magic, and a variety of non-human "monster" units are described in "Appendix A: Fantasy Supplement".  Numerous unit types have special abilities that allow units to take special actions or make special movements.  Generally, these all work within the basic framework of the game.  Opposing forces are constructed using a "point cost" method that allows disparate but balanced armies to be fielded.

Play involves turns, with both players executing some portions of the turn simultaneously, other portions in alternating order.  Each player turn is executed in five enumerated phases that take place in order.  The phase orders are:


        Phase 1 (optional) - draw cards
        Phase 2 duels between individual units are resolved
        Phase 3 Ranged fire and magical attack is resolved
        Phase 4 Movement (each side, in turn)
        Phase 5 Mêlée is resolved


Units must test morale after sustaining damage; units may route; leaders may attempt to rally.

The game does not elucidate a winner or formulaic victory situation; presumably this becomes evident through play.  After the battle is concluded, a simple process is used to determine Battle Results.  This process may determine a "victor" for post-game results.  Opposing armies are impacted by the Battle Results, both in survivorship and field location (presumably this allows multiple engagements in a campaign).

—user summary
Halma and checkers, but on hexagonal boards.

It was first available at SPIEL Essen 2018 at Repos Production's booth.
It was the first wonder board that came without a wonder card, for easy random selection as recommended in the base game rules.

Siracusa's wonder board resource is Glass. It has three wonder stages on both of its sides. It has the special rule that its stages can be built in any order.
The wonders stages grant the following (according to the official rulesheet from the publisher; see link section):

Side A:
Stage 1: The player freely moves one of their Ships one space upward on their Dockyard board. In addition, this step grants 1 Naval Shield.
Stage 2: The player freely moves one of their Ships one space upward on their Dockyard board. In addition, this step grants 3 Victory Points at the end of the game.
Stage 3: The player freely moves one of their Ships one space upward on their Dockyard board. In addition, this step grants 1 Ground Shield.

Side B:
Stage 1: The player immediately gains 6 Coins. All other players lose 1 Coin per Commercial Level reached by their Yellow fleet.
Stage 2: The player freely moves two of their different Ships one space upward each on their Dockyard board.
Stage 3: This step grants 1 Ground Shield and 1 Naval Shield.

boardgame: Fog of Love: It Will Never Last
description:
This is the worst date you've ever been on. How could your friend think you would be right for each other? You have nothing in common.

Relationships are hard. This expansion is harder. It Will Never Last is an expansion for Fog of Love that offers the game's most challenging story yet. How will you navigate a relationship that is doomed from the start? It requires you to interpret what your partner wants like never before. It also comes with 24 new feature cards which you can mix straight into the base game to bring new flavor to your characters, no matter what story you play.

This expansion was originally included in the Kickstarter version of Fog of Love as Mismatched Love.

Paranormal Romance is an expansion for Fog of Love that tells a powerful, cinematic love story that will leave a lasting impression — and the best way to experience any great movie is to not know how it will end, so we won't give any spoilers away. This expansion is divided into two parts, and you can't open the second part until you've experienced the first. Once you've played it, the Paranormal Romance will echo through future plays of the game.

This expansion also comes with 24 new occupation cards that you can use to expand your character options with any story.

Paranormal Romance was included in the Kickstarter version of Fog of Love.

boardgame: Fog of Love: Trouble with the In-Laws
description:
"Honey, your parents have arrived! And they've brought their opinions."

There's no longer only two people in this relationship because now your in-laws are sticking their nose in. They have strong opinions on how you should be living your life, and you have to consider their feelings with every decision you make — but that doesn't mean you have to respect their wishes. Each game, you can decide whether to play the golden child, or ignore what they want completely. Be careful, they might drive you apart, for good.

Trouble with the In-Laws, an expansion for Fog of Love originally included in the Kickstarter version of the game, journeys deeper into the hilarious complications of modern relationships, delivering richer stories and a new way to play.

description:
Rushin' Roulette is a high-stakes card game of strategy and luck.

Each player starts with a posse of 3 characters that line up in a hidden order. One-by-one, they’ll take turns “pulling a trigger” hoping nothing is in the chamber. But your partners aren’t completely at the mercy of lady luck because YOU control what’s loaded into the revolver!

In this simplified game of poker, you can load Blanks to save your own team or Bullets to take out your opponents. But be careful! If your strategies are too obvious, your friends might use them against you. The last one standing wins, riding off into the sunset with the rest of their (most likely unconscious) crew.

Each player has a hand of Ammo Cards (Like Blanks or Bullets). They load one of these cards in front of them each turn and try to figure out what opponents loaded. Flip your opponents' cards when you think they're playing it safe (blank) and load dangerous cards (bullets) when your friends trust you too much and think you're playing it safe.

Rushin' Roulette is a bluffing game. You can load safe cards to keep your own characters safe, but if your strategy is obvious, your friends will flip your card to save themselves. So if you think they'll take your card, load a bullet instead. Each character has a Safe and Shot ability that activates depending on the cards you flip. Generally, shot characters go to the grave while safe characters stay in your lineup. The last player with characters in their lineup wins.

—description from the publisher

Caesar Augustus issued a decree that a census should be taken in the entire Roman world. This was to aid military conscription and tax collection. Luckily, they were able to gain encouragement and support from others making the journey.

Individual battleships and carriers, otherwise usually 2-cruiser, 4-destroyer and 6-transport divisions, air squadrons, and regiment or brigade size land units. As in Desert Campaign for tanks, air-to-air combat determined by aircraft type matrix - not factors. Also, a simple but elegant ground combat system enabling the attacker to risk greater losses to win.

Unknown Dutch secret airfields and secondary Dutch ground unit strengths aid solitaire playability.  Concealed (until contacted/exposed) and dummy units for deception and bluffing.

description:
In the trick-taking game Beutelschneider, players compete to gather as much gold as they can, but the gold is on the lowest-valued cards in the game, so you can rarely win with your own gold and instead need to grab it from others.

The deck includes 36 cards in four colors, as well as nine trump cards in a fifth color. On a turn, someone leads a card, and players must follow suit if possible. For each Beutelschneider (cutpurse) you collect, you must subtract 20 points from your score for the round, which could put you in the red; the Beutelschneider is the highest ranked card in each of the non-trump suits, though, so ideally it will allow you to collect the 10-point gold sacks that rank far above the cards worth 1-4 coins.

Many of the trump cards in Beutelschneider have special powers, and the deck even includes a bunch of violet special cards that can be added to gameplay if desired. To do this, shuffle the deck of special cards separately, then give each player one before dealing out the other cards like normal.

If you are reading this then fate has found you. 
My voice in this text is from the year 3045.  What you are reading on your screen is a simulation of possible change in what ever time you may find yourself in. This simulation is a meditative practice of change for our Aina(land) and Uhane(spirit). A change that that I dare say has gone to the worst in the time I am from.

We call this simulation Huli.

Huli, was created with several goals in mind, to teach our keiki’s (children) the importance of land stewardship, of reminding our elders that it is never too late to change your narrative, and finally to act as a guide and pathway for alternate timelines.

In Huli, you will take the mantle of one of these Avatars, KONOHIKI: The Steward of the Ahupua’a, KUMU: THE Steward of Knowledge, MAHI’AI: The Steward of Cultivation, and LAWAI’A: The Steward of the Sea.

Your goal is to practice removing all Eha (hurt) from the landscape through time.

They have agreed and joined the Aliens in their invasion, giving Humanity another enemy to stand against.

Includes one (1) new double-sided character card, seventeen (17) new equipment cards and eighteen (18) robot and cyborgs cards.

GET LOOT! is a 2-5 player competitive hacking game where your goal is to exfiltrate (steal) loot while sabotaging your rivals with strategic crypto-locks and Hak5 themed cyber attacks!

Win by being the first with 5 unlocked loot cards.

Each turn you may Exfiltrate or Attack, then you may Lock or Unlock.

Exfiltrate: Take the top loot card from the loot draw stack. If it is locked, you must take the lock with it.

Attack:Follow the instructions on the attack card, then discard and draw a new one.

Lock: You can lock one loot card in your stash, or the top card from the loot draw stack if it's unlocked.

Unlock: Remove a lock from one loot card in your stash if it matches your key's suit. Return lock to inventory.

Once it's time to open the door on the game board, prepare to meet the companion of your dreams and your dating scenario. But watch out! You just might get catfished instead!
