Anarchess takes place in the land of Chess long before the enemy White and Black kingdoms were formed. Back when the land was not ruled by kings and queens, neither structured armies with bishops, knights and rooks existed. The world was unexplored and amorphous and it had not been squeezed yet into the rigid cast of the contemporary chessboard. There were just 2 proud tribes constantly struggling to expand their territories to ensure survival in a barren and harsh land. And those who today we (somewhat degradingly) call "pawns," were free entities with dreams, fears and hopes who defined their own destiny.

The game is played with 32 light- (‘white’) and 32 dark-colored (‘black’) square tiles and 8 light- (‘white’) and 8 dark-colored (‘black’) pawns.

Update: Anarchess has now been playtested and confirmed to also be highly playable and fun with 3 and 4 players. In a 3-player game each player gets 6 pawns, while in a 4-player game each player gets 5 pawns.

It takes just 5-10 minutes to learn how to play. A game lasts about 30-60 minutes.

On a turn, a player can perform 2 actions:
A. Tile Placement
B. (Optional) Pawn Action (placing a new pawn, moving or attacking)

The game ends after a player places the last available tile. Players earn points by controlling areas (i.e., having the most pawns in them) comprising two or more tiles. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dragon Castle: The Look-Out / The Glass Fortress Promo
description:
Originally available from the Game Boy Geek Season 6 Kickstarter.

Double sided promo featuring The Look-Out (best for 2 players) on one side and on the other side The Glass Fortress (best for 3-4 players)

Biff! is a lighthearted, quick-fire, take-that boxing game for 2-6 players. Easy to learn, easy to play and ultra-portable – Biff! will have you bopping and blocking your way to becoming the ultimate Biffing Champion!

Outwit your opponent with your mind, brawn and moves until they can no longer respond and win the round. The player with the most points after 12 rounds win the game!

Each player shuffles their deck and draws 2 cards. In turns, play a card from your hand into 'the ring' (according to the card chart) and draw a card. If you can't play a card you're knocked out of the round.

-description from designer

Objection Overruled is a quick-fire, take-that courtroom battle game for 2 players.

Easy to learn, fun to play and ultra-portable – Objection Overruled pits you as two corrupt lawyers arguing high-profile court cases before Judge and Jury.

More concerned with winning than the truth; you must bamboozle the Judge with science and facts to “prove” your version of events. And if that doesn’t work, throw in some dodgy evidence, bribes and lies to cheat your way to victory.

Justice is overrated.

Each player shuffles their deck and draws 2 cards. In turns, you must play a card and draw a card - mounting up evidence to discredit your opponent and win the case. If you can't respond according to the card chart you loose the case and move on to the next trial.
First player to win 5 case makes partner and wins the game.

For an extra challenge, you can use the "Lawyers never lie" rules: where you can play evidence cards face down. Get caught lying, or falsely accuse someone of doing it, and you'll loose the round.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ミッキー&フレンズ 5ファイブリンクス (Mickey & Friends 5 Links)
description:
User summary
ミッキー&フレンズ 5ファイブリンクス (Mickey & Friends 5 Links) is a Japanese card game for children using the famous Disney characters. The deck shows the animated friends with some cards having more than one character. There is a bank of Mickey coins (including two secret gold coins worth more). Players start with one coin, and the first to get up to five coins wins.

The deck is shuffled and players are dealt three cards each. Some cards are placed face up to start the tableau. On your turn, you draw a card from the deck (no hand limit) and then you must try to add a card to a set on the table. You can play up to three cards, but each must be a different friend.

If you add the five friend to a set, you draw a coin from the pool. If you cannot play this turn, you pay a coin to the previous player (or from the pool if you have none).

プレイヤーは自分の番がきたらカードを1枚引きます。
ミッキー＆フレンズの5キャラクターのカードを1枚以上場に出していきます。
最後の5キャラクター目を場に出した人がミッキーコインを獲得します。
このコインを誰よりも早く5枚集めたプレイヤーが勝ちとなります。

boardgame: ファインディング・ニモ (Finding NEMO)
description:
User summary
ファインディング・ニモ (Finding NEMO) is a Japanese game for children with the famous Pixar characters. This card game reunites Nemo and Merlin on the game mat.

Each player gets five cards. taking turns, players draw crads from the deck and discard cards. The aim is to make pairs of the same characters. You can play out yellow Nemo cards and green Merlin cards. The player who can join Nemo and Merlin at their spot is the winner.

ゲームマット上の「ニモ」と「マーリン」を再会させるカードゲームです。

プレイヤーに5枚の手札を配ります。自分の手番では、山札もしくはカード捨場からカードを補充し、同じキャラクターのペアを作ります。黄色カードは「ニモ」を、緑カードは「マーリン」を動かすことができます。自分の場所で「ニモ」と「マーリン」を合流させることができたプレイヤーが勝者です。

In the history card game 俺は豪族 (Orewa Gozoku) (I, Clansman), you play roles of the lords of ancient Japan and lead your clans for 16 generations -- from the 4th to the 7th Century A.D.. You compete each other in collecting political powers. Victory Points are earned when you contribute to strengthen the kingdom in military, economy and other fields. At the end of the 7th Century, the player with the most VPs is the winner and becomes the highest rank aristocrat in the newborn empire.

The game is played in 16 rounds. Each round, players take turns placing their workers on production buildings in a counterclockwise order, and then buying cards from the market in a clockwise order. When you buy a card, the production buildings you own and the one occupied by your worker in total must produce the resources sufficient for the card's cost. Some cards become your own production buildings, and others provide VPs.

—description from the publisher

The beloved game of quick sketches and hilarious guesses now features three innovative, new ways to play! Mirror, Mirror: trace on one side of the window while your teammate follows along with a marker on the other side; Action: pull your clue, then use the window to act it out; Classic: the Pictionary game you know and love. It also features a large group display, stand, timer, and pen. With 750 clues, the guesses can be just as hilarious as the sketches! For 3 or more players. Ages 8 and older.

A whole new way to play Pictionary
3 ways to play: Action, Mirror Mirror and Classic
Trace along with your teammate or act out your clue
Or play a game of Classic Pictionary
Includes frame display, stand, timer, pen and cards with 750 clues

Sternbild is a roll and move game on a board depicting our solar system and a little bit of what's beyond. Players roll four dice to move to the outer edges and eventually to the center of the galaxy. Certain fields have certain effects, and players can strike each other and send each other back to the center, although pawns which went through that get a bonus during the rest of the game. 
From the outmost field players must roll a sequence of four numbers to reach the target field before being sent back by other players. The board shows names of planets and other orbital objects in many languages.

—user summary

The Earth has become a giant and contaminated dump, ruled by a chaos and violence without limits. One of the safest places in the planet is New Babylon City, a enormous city fortress.

The only way to maintain a rigid social order is to display the state’s violent capabilities through codes of law. That's why in New Babylon City if a citizen breaks any law (it doesn't matter what law) he will be executed in a public execution in 24 hours.With this new law, they avoid judgments, lawyers, bureaucracy and prisons. Besides making a public show of the execution impress on the watching multitude preventing future crimes.

The execution takes place inside the Presidium, and Unique Corporation has become the executioners. They transform death and suffering in Dantesque spectacle. Beheading, mutilations, hanging... every unimaginable human aberration, Unique Corporation is willing to give it to the people.

Right now what is been drawing immense audience rates is the "hell pit", a show that used a dark technology for opening Portals, trough those portals appears some hideous blood thirsty creatures. They are quick, lethal and they have an incredible destruction capacity.

Some people claim to the heavens saying that those portals are the gates of hell. But who cares where those creatures come from if the audience is unbelievable and the crime rates are descending like never before, transforming New Babylon City in of the safest city estates on Earth?

Welcome to the future...
Welcome to New Babylon City...

-description from designer

A Series of American Games of War Based Upon Military Principles and Designed for the Assistance Both of Beginners and Advanced Students in Prosecuting the Whole Study of Tactics, Grand Tactics, Strategy, Military History, and the Various Operations of War. Illustrated With Numerous Diagrams. To Which Is Appended a Collection of Studies Upon Military Statistics as Applied to War on a Field Map. By Charles A.L. Totten, First Lieutenant, Fourth United States Artillery.

—description from the publisher

This two volume work was a set of rules designed for simulating battles on the tabletop, in the style of Kriegsspiel. Rules are included for The Minor Tactical Game, The Grand Tactical Game, The Battle Game, and The Advanced Game. Units represent corps. A map scale of 5-10 inches to the mile is recommended.

—user summary
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Muse: Game Boy Geek Season 6 Kickstarter Promo Cards
description:
Originally available from the Game Boy Geek Season 6 Kickstarter.

Two new masterpiece cards.

Oktet is a card game in which each player competes in the most riotous way to make a set of eight identical cards and shout "OKTET" out loud.

In the game, players may trade their starting cards once per round and must give a card to other players and bluff each other with "wild cards" to achieve victory.

In the esoteric world of Dalhalla, once you reach the rank of Adhipa there's so many things to do, yet you have to polish your ability to control Vishra. Therefore a mystical method of training was invented, and later on this method became popular even among the youngest of Dalhalla.

This training is Varna, and this game of the mind, each Adhipa tests their mental strength against another Adhipa to conquer the power of Vishra. In Grumantra of Dalhalla: Varna, you can either create or destroy elements of opposing forces by adding more force to your elements. This process works differently for each element, depending on an elemental diagram.

—description from the publisher

One day, the peace in a distant kingdom of Luwak (chivet) is broken by the disappearance of the Raja Luwak (the king of chivet). Each member of the Luwak tribe begins to compete to claim the throne of the Luwak Kingdom.

In Raja Luwak, you dive your way deeper into the kingdom by pressing your luck in dice rolling and gathering materials to outwit other competitors and claim your true worth of the throne of Luwak.

—description from the publisher

In the esoteric world of Dalhalla, there's a hereditary ancient ritual to achieve greater understanding to the mysterious power of Vishra called Mahapatha, a ritual that can be achieved only by sacrificing oneself to the underworld.

In Grumantra of Dalhalla: Mahapatha, fight your way against the dungeon master or become the dungeon master in this turn/action-based strategy board game. Learn new techniques, master your inner strength, and return with your comrades to Dalhalla to master the rank of Adhipa.

—description from the publisher
2018 Origins Convention promo character screen. The artwork is the same as the base set, however, this promo screen has a Feature "Savage Endurance" only found on this promo and already pre-printed.

Who is the best barista in this coffee shop?

Prepare yourself! Manage your workers in Kedai Kopi and use the best strategy to serve customers. Be the fastest and best barista in Manikmaya's coffee shop!

—description from the publisher

Smong is coming! Run to the hill and save yourself.

The peaceful island of Simeuleu is being threatened by Smong, a gigantic water dragon awakened by an earthquake. Make the right decisions to save as many turtles as possible, while waiting for Nabang, The Whale Rider to arrive and turn Smong back to sleep. Be careful for each step you take will also trigger the movement of Smong. At the end of the game, be the bravest and help Nabang in pushing Smong back to the ocean and gain the most victory points to win the game.

Smong is inspired by a folklore from Simeuleu island that warns the natives about the danger of tsunami. By learning about the story since their childhood, the local people of Simeuleu learned about tsunami and the necessary steps to take during an earthquake and incoming tsunami.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel Super Heroes Collector's Club Trading Cards
description:
Published by Scholastic Entertainment Inc. in 2006-07 as part of a monthly subscription service, and appears to be a mechanically simple card game with 5 types of cards

     Hero (blue)
     Villain (purple)
     Resist (red)
     Power (green)
     Wild Card (yellow)


There are also Ultimate Villain foil cards that had a slightly different structure than the standard Villain cards.

The game was distributed as fixed packs in each monthly shipment to club members, with a metal tin in the initial shipment.  Unfortunately, no copies of the rules are to be found online, so most people who have come across the cards appear to be unable to actually play the game.

Nine centuries ago, there was a young man called Ibn Batutah. He left his homeland to explore the world. After walking the scorching desert, he crossed the sea, then passed through the coldness of the snow. Here's a great adventure throughout history! His great journey was written in a note entitled "Rihla".

A few centuries later, you and your friends discovered Ibn Batutah's journey. Inspired by "Rihla", you decided to trace Ibn Batutah's adventure, and the journey has just begun.

The Ibnu Batutah board game was based on "Rihla" and another journey notes from Ibn Batutah.

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Hydro Fury Theme Deck
description:
The Hydro Fury Theme Deck is from the Celestial Storm expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the seventh expansion in the Sun & Moon series. However, it also contains cards from earlier expansions in the Sun & Moon series. It predominantly focuses on Water and Fire type Pokémon. The main Pokémon is Swampert.

Like other Theme Decks, Hydro Fury includes damage counters, a custom coin, one two-player mat, a Swampert deckbox and a TCGO deck access code. (User summary.)

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Tropical Takedown Theme Deck
description:
The Tropical Takedown Theme Deck is from the Forbidden Light expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the sixth expansion in the Sun & Moon series. However, it also contains cards from earlier expansions in the Sun & Moon series. It predominantly focuses on Alolan Pokémon, with the main Pokémon being Alolan Exeggutor.

Like other Theme Decks, Tropical Takedown includes damage counters, a custom coin, one two-player mat, an Alolan Exeggutor deckbox and a TCGO deck access code. (User summary.)

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Twilight Rogue Theme Deck
description:
The Twilight Rogue Theme Deck is from the Forbidden Light expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the sixth expansion in the Sun & Moon series. However, it also contains cards from earlier expansions in the Sun & Moon series. It predominantly focuses on Fighting and Psychic type Pokémon. The main Pokémon is Dusk Form Lycanroc using its Dangerous Rogue attack to punish the opponent for having a large bench. The secondary Pokémon is Dusknoir which uses its Dark Invitation ability to force the opponent to fill up their bench.

Like other Theme Decks, Twilight Rogue includes damage counters, a custom coin, one two-player mat, a Dusk Form Lycanroc deckbox and a TCGO deck access code. (User summary.)

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Mach Strike Theme Deck
description:
The Mach Strike Theme Deck is from the Ultra Prism expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the fifth expansion in the Sun & Moon series. However, it also contains cards from earlier expansions in the Sun & Moon series. It predominantly focuses on Dragon and Fighting type Pokémon. The main Pokémon is Garchomp.

Like other Theme Decks, Mach Strike includes damage counters, a custom coin, one two-player mat, a Garchomp deckbox, and a TCGO deck access code. (User summary.)

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Imperial Command Theme Deck
description:
The Imperial Command Theme Deck is from the Ultra Prism expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the fifth expansion in the Sun & Moon series. However, it also contains cards from earlier expansions in the Sun & Moon series. It predominantly focuses on Water type Pokémon. The main Pokémon is Empoleon.

Like other Theme Decks, Imperial Command includes damage counters, a custom coin, one two-player mat, a Empoleon deckbox, and a TCGO deck access code. (User summary.)

boardgame: Dragon Quest Dungeon II expansion pack
description:
An expansion for Dragon Quest Dungeon II that introduced a new game mode.

14 new monster figures
2 hero figures

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Destruction Fang Theme Deck
description:
The Destruction Fang Theme Deck is from the Celestial Storm expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the fourth expansion in the Sun & Moon series. However, it also contains cards from earlier expansions in the Sun & Moon series. It predominantly focuses on Darkness type Pokémon. The main Pokémon is Hydreigon.

Like other Theme Decks, Destruction Fang includes damage counters, a custom coin featuring Hydreigon, one two-player mat, a Hydreigon deckbox, and a TCGO deck access code. (User summary.)

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Clanging Thunder Theme Deck
description:
The Clanging Thunder Theme Deck is from the Celestial Storm expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the fourth expansion in the Sun & Moon series. However, it also contains cards from earlier expansions in the Sun & Moon series. It predominantly focuses on Lightning, Fighting, and Dragon type Pokémon.

Like other Theme Decks, Clanging Thunder includes damage counters, a custom coin featuring Kommo-o, one two-player mat, a Kommo-o deckbox and a TCGO deck access code. (User summary.)

Winter, 1846. Experience the harsh and fateful journey of America’s favorite cannibals, The Donner Party. Pick up and own the game today… IF YOU DARE!

May the best cannibal win:

Brave your way out of the snowy slopes of the Sierra Nevada mountains and stagger to safety in Fort Sacramento. Collect Fingers Of Meat along the treacherous Emigrant Trail whilst avoiding Toil, Trouble and Doom (and learnin’ a little history along the way). The one who gets to the Fort with the most Meat wins!

—description from the publisher

Baby Boum is a board game in French designed to answer to a popular scientific objective. It was created by five doctoral students and produced by the Nancy student association Big Brother. This game was created by Quentin Dumont, Mickaël Lebdar, Géraud Le Falher, Tania Louis and Camille Paoletti, advised and supervised by Stéphane Goria (researcher at the Mediation Research Centre of the University of Lorraine) and Marie-Laure Lenertz (Communication Officer at the Institute of Genetics and Molecular and Cellular Biology of the University of Strasbourg) as part of a training for doctoral students during the "Science & You" days at the University of Lorraine in Metz in 2015. Using black humour, this game highlights the problem of infertility in some parts of Africa (contrary to stereotypes and other preconceived ideas, research shows the health and social urgency associated with this problem). The scientific elements conveyed by the game are based on the research work undertaken by Emmanuelle Simon (researcher at the Centre de recherche sur les médiations of University of Lorraine).
It is a humorous game in which each player plays a character or a pair of characters whose particular winning conditions and other characteristics are represented on a tablet on which the cards drawn during the game are placed. The game also requires 2 dices.

Take your friends to a journey discovering each other as you never imagined...

Two teams of both boys and girls face embarrassing and exciting challenges to achieve victory. Two decks for different levels of difficulty and naughtiness: one to break the ice and another to get into the heat of the action.

The game has two different set of rules
Each card presents a challenge to the player to be performed alone or with a partner. Accepting a challenge awards the player's team with points, whereas refusing it will decrease the team score.

The cards decide how the two partners should be dressed (or undressed!) and if the partner is to be picked randomly or at player's choice.

The team with the highest score, wins the match!

—description from the designer

Ryan is on a fence bordered by a different color rocket ship on either side. Both players simultaneously slide "power pucks" attempting to hit the target under the bridge. Ryan will move closer to a player's rocket with each successful hit of the target by that player. The winner is the first player who gets Ryan to reach their ship five times.

Enter the dungeon and leave it as first with the most loot. But sadly it isn’t that easy. While building the dungeon card by card, you need to fight against monsters and pass some silly traps.
Raus aus dem Verlies! is an easy card game for the whole family.

Opticall is a strategic bluffing card game.
Players take turns playing six different colors on a central pile until someone is caught bluffing by another player. The final player left in the game becomes the winner.

Like Bull Sh*t/Cheat with intricate strategy.

—description from the publisher

Speak Out Showdown is the electronic 'head to head' mouthpiece challenge game with ridiculous rambling and random interruptions. Players simultaneously try to get their teammates to correctly guess more correct phrases than their opponents, but the electronic interrupter unit's hand randomly determines when it's your turn to speak out and when it's not! One player from each team sits in front of the unit and tries to say different phrases while wearing a speech-inhibiting mouthpiece that keeps their lips open. Their teammates try to guess what they’ve said when the hand is flipped down in front of them, indicating that it’s their turn to talk. The phrases on the cards utilize specific letter combinations that are hard to say without the ability to close their mouth. Cards are collected for correctly guessed phrases. The two players switch so that their partner gets turns saying phrases. After two rounds, the team at the end with the most cards, wins!

—description from the publisher

The World At War 85 series of games (WaW85) are fast-paced, platoon-level simulations of combat during the Third World War. The series’ action begins in 1985, and brings to life the troops, tanks, artillery, and aircraft of that time period, as conflict erupts around the globe.

These Core Rules are designed to be used with every game in the series, and so, once learned, you will be able to play any game in the series. Individual game Volumes (also called Modules, but each is, in fact, a complete standalone game) may add rules for use in that module, and individual scenarios may introduce rules that apply only to that scenario. But the Core Rules remain the same. When in doubt, Module Rules override the Core Series rules and Scenario Rules (SRs) override both the Core Series and Module Rules.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: World At War 85 (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

boardgame: Seekrieg (Fourth Edition): Ship Data II – Refits & Modernizations World War 2
description: Additional ship data after its most recent refit prior to the outbreak of the war and any refits or alterations.

boardgame: Seekrieg (Fourth Edition): CNO Series – Naval Operations Pacific 1 and 2
description:
Ready to play scenarios for naval wargames.

Contains 15 different operations that are historically accurate and give a complete breakdown of the units involved. The operations are varied to include several types of actions as well as an umber of different sizes of operations.

This set includes Pacific 1, CNO #3 and Pacific #2, CNO #5 which were previously published separately.

Lost in Canton is a game that has a revolutionary structure as it uses boards as pages in a book, and the game itself looks like a book.

The game is about a fairy tale in Canton, China. Within the tale, the Dragon fish kids are born and put into human society. They will learn all the wonderful knowledge and every handcraft skill of Canton culture, and eventually go to the Temple Gathering to ascend as a real Chinese Dragon.

During the game, players each act as a newly born Dragon Fish kid that has just landed upon Canton's land. The players need to find different masters to learn the knowledge and handcraft skills. On a player's turn, they can ask any other player a question about the location info of the master. The asked player can answer the truth or use their ability to avoid answering or confuse the asking player. The first player that found their master's location wins.

A hilarious drawing game designed by a psychologist to foster creativity.

Research shows that childhood creativity is on the decline and we should all be concerned. This game is designed to foster creativity through an old-school tabletop game that’s simple, fun and expands your mind. While the decline in childhood creativity was the impetus for designing the game, it is extremely fun with or without kids and promotes creativity in all. To spice things up a bit for adult game nights, there is also an optional Adults Only Expansion pack.

Each round of Starting Lines provides a creative prompt (a category) and a constraint (the starting line). What happens next is creativity. Everyone gets the same simple Starting Line, but inevitably takes it in a completely different directions. You will be surprised by what kids see in a Starting Line and be brought to tears laughing at what your weird friends find. A judge reviews all players drawings and select the one they like the most. That player collects the category card from that round. Play continues until you decide to stop. The player with the most category cards at the end is declared the winner, and of course deemed the most creative.

The game play can be as quick as 15 minutes or can go on for hours. The game is great for large parties. While the game includes enough markers for 6 players, with more markers you can play with as many friends as you can find.

—description from the publisher

A shroud of darkness has enveloped the long dormant Obsidian Tower. As it begins to creep unto the surrounding area, skirmishes rife with foul creatures are making a once fertile land desolate and it appears that the kingdom is on the brink of war.

The King has confirmed that this malice is an ancient evil - the Witchlord of Agaroth, also known as the Darkbringer - who has taken refuge within the confines of the sable keep. All willing heroes have been summoned to aid in the battle against this villainy.

Ancient tales surface of secrets buried with the kings of old; travelers whisper of mystical light sources which can penetrate the depths of any abyss; and that magical items are capable of exiling any evil but are protected within the depths of dark labyrinths.  However, an ominous shadow soars overhead as fire and brimstone erupt upon the horizon …

Will your party be able to fend off vast insurgencies, make it to the Tower alive, and prevail against this ancient evil?

Adventure awaits only the bravest!  Gather your party of heroes in a quest to save a kingdom under siege. Discover 3 hidden relics and make it to the Obsidian Tower to defeat the Witchlord before his minions can lay waste to all of the land.

The Obsidian Tower is an exciting fantasy-based co-operative adventure that supports action for as many as six players, ages 12 and up.

—description from the designer

Fall Classic Baseball brings all of the thrills of managing a big league baseball team to your kitchen table.  Call the hit and run, steal a base, is it time to pull the pitcher?  All of these decisions are yours to make as you guide your roster towards a pennant. Play today's stars against Hall of Fame greats in what-if match ups. You're in control, so enjoy the show and see if your team can go home with a victory in the Fall Classic.

Each individual batting card is calculated to match the player's stats for that season to within one of the actual number for all of the hitting stats ... singles, doubles, triples, home runs, walks, hit by pitch, and strikeouts.

Each individual pitching card is calculated to match the player's stats for that season in all of the major pitching stats ... hits allowed, walks allowed, home runs allowed, and strikeouts.  So, if your star struck out 300 batters that year, the card will produce the same given an even distribution of dice rolls. Also, pitchers are rated for endurance and wild pitches.  Even those pitchers with minimal hits and walks allowed can perform to their true abilities in an easy to play format.

Errors are also accurate for each different position played.  Also,  Infielders are rated for turning the double play, outfielders for assists & runner advances on hits, and catchers for controlling the running game.

This is a promo card for Champions of Hara.

The card is a new item card that you add to the item deck. Specially, "Bloodstone" counts as a new dreamstone (a level 0 item card). It a discard type item, with the following effect: "Pay up to 5 Health; look at that many cards from the top of any World deck. Choose one and spawn it on your space. Shuffle the other cards back into that deck. Only usable while on an empty space. [i]Bloodstone is considered a dreamstone.[/i]"

This card has item number IP01.

Right Hand Path is a single page roll & write game. Collect relics and craft a protective salt circle to keep yourself and your home safe from invading dark and demonic forces. You need a writing utensil and two six-sided dice to play.

An expansion to the game Coop da Chickens.

A 20 card deck. 10 cards to add into the standard deck and a 10 card egg deck used when hatching eggs.

-description from designer

Red Sparrows, Yellow Cranes, Green Owls, Blue Crows...which SCRUM Team will be first to release the hottest new app idea "Woobie - The Search Engine for Pets" to the App Store and win the $1 million dollar company bonus?!? As Chief SCRUM Master you control the team, the money, and developing the software. Be the first to complete all six phases of the Agile Lifecycle, deploy your app to the App Store and win the game!

Scrum: The Card Game is a 1-4 player hand and resource management game that plays in 20-30 minutes. Players take turns spending money to improve their hand before playing cards to theirs and other players tableau. Blockers and Software Bugs can slow your progress so be sure to spend your funding wisely! The first player to get all 6 cards of their player color into their tableau and has $100k remaining to deploy their app to the App Store wins the game!

It’s now a family game with a built in raunchy expansion for adults. You get to draw a scenario, while others respond and generate laughs.

4 decks. 3 of them are used together to form a complete sentence creating just 1 of one million possible scenarios.

The 4th deck is used for responses. One player will read the scenario while the other players respond. The reader will then read the responses out loud to choose their favorite response. The goal of the game is to win 5 scenarios or to just have a good time laughing with your friends. Familiar playing style, but has two ways of being entertained. Through the scenario's as well as the responses. Has a well balanced blend of pure ridiculous to the infamous raunchy card styles.

—description from the publisher

The ancient Huarpes who habited the region of Cuyo performed different competitions to see which tribe was the favorite of the Gods. Of these competitions, the most daring one was mastering the Zonda wind. The most skilled of each tribe spent years practicing to master the aspects of the wind and other elements of nature that surrounded them. With these skills they faced each other in great competitions to obtain the favor of the Gods. Let the Ancestral tournament begin!

The objective of the game is to eliminate the other players, filling their hut with leaves.

—description from the publisher


Los antiguos Huarpes que habitaban la región de Cuyo realizaban distintas competencias para ver que tribu era la preferida de los Dioses. De estas competencias, la más osada era la del dominio del viento Zonda. Los más habilidosos de cada tribu pasaban años practicando hasta lograr dominar los aspectos del viento y de los demás elementos de la naturaleza que los rodeaba. Con estas habilidades se enfrentaban en grandiosas competencias para obtener el favor de los Dioses ¡Que comience el torneo Ancestral!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Bam-Bam Race is a wacky racing game to see who can get to the finish line and manage to stay there until the end of the round. Follow Bam-Bam and his friends to celebrate Indonesian independence day.

Designed to be a grab-and-play game, Bam-Bam Race is easy to set up. Also, it is easy to learn, so your new friends can jump in and play, but still offer strategies to keep you entertained.

While out on patrol on Mars, you were caught in a seismic event, trapping you underground and cutting you off from any rescue. Your only hope now is to dig your way out.

Luckily, your Mars exploration suit comes with some useful terraforming tools that will help you. But it doesn’t have unlimited energy and you might be buried under too much rock to escape.

Still, try not to let that ruin your day.

Below Mars is a small-footprint, variable set-up (for length & difficulty), sci-fi themed pattern-matching Print-and-Play game for one player.

—description from the designer

The zombies are coming! Run for your life ..
The fast running game with the ultimate adrenaline rush! By rolling the dice successfully, you‘ll proceed steadily on the placed course. 
If you are afraid and often save your path, you‘ll risk that the others reach the safe hut in front of you .. Who can do it first?

A entertaining quick game with coasters. Fun for everyone!

—description from the publisher

Game play:
The start field will be laid open first. Then all running fields are placed one after the other in any order (from the start field) as a continuous course, always edge to edge. Finally, the target field is placed to the last running field. The number tokens are mixed loosely and placed open on the running fields as desired (with the yellow side open), so that exactly 1 number token is left each running field.

The player rolls the dice and can move his figure forward (towards the target) as the shown number of the next coaster is rolled. 
After a player has reached a new coaster, he always has the choice to continue (again up to 2 rolls to get to the next coaster) or secure himself on the current coaster. Then the number token will be turned to the red backside and the player must finish his turn immediately. A secured number token is saved forever and applies in the rest of the game for all participating players!

Object of the game: To reach the target field in front of all other players. If there are several players (at the end of a complete round), there are also several winners!


    


Die Zombies kommen! Lauf um Dein Leben ..
Das schnelle Laufspiel mit dem ultimativen Adrenalinkick! Durch erfolgreiches Würfeln kannst Du Dich auf dem gelegten Parkour stetig voranbewegen. Bist Du ängstlich und sicherst Dich oft ab, riskierst Du, dass die anderen vor Dir die sichere Hütte erreichen .. Wer schafft es zuerst?

Ein kurzweiliges schnelles Spiel mit Bierdeckeln. Spaß für jedermann!

—description from the publisher (German)

Spielablauf: 
Das Startfeld wird als erstes offen ausgelegt. Dann werden alle Lauffelder nacheinander in beliebiger Reihenfolge (vom Startfeld aus) als fortlaufender Parcour angelegt, immer Kante an Kante. 
Abschließend wird das Zielfeld an das letzte Lauffeld angelegt.
Die Zahlenmarker werden lose gemischt und beliebig jeweils offen (mit der gelben Seite oben) auf den Lauffeldern ausgelegt, so dass dann pro Lauffeld genau 1 Zahlenmarker ausliegt.

Der Spieler würfelt und kann mit seiner Spielfigur einen Deckel vorrücken (Richtung Zielfeld), wenn die ausliegende Zahl des nächsten Deckels erwürfelt wird. Nachdem ein Spieler einen neuen Deckel erreicht hat, hat er immer die Wahl fortzufahren (wieder bis zu 2 Würfe, um auf den nächsten Deckel zu kommen) oder sich auf dem jetzigen Deckel abzusichern. Der Zahlenmarker wird dann auf die rote Rückseite gedreht und der Spieler muss sofort seinen Zug beenden.
Ein gesicherter Zahlenmarker bleibt dann für immer gesichert und gilt im weiteren Spielverlauf für alle teilnehmenden Spieler!

Spielziel: Vor allen anderen Spielern das Zielfeld erreichen. Stehen dort (am Ende einer kompletten Runde) mehrere Spieler, gibt es auch mehrere Gewinner!

Damned pirate souls want to take possession of you ..
You want to get rid of these soul markers as fast as possible, but also the other players have the same fate. It goes from island to island and the lucky 6 always helps you. All other numbers can have bad consequences. Let the sabers rattle! / ~ based on the classic dice game Zeppelin ~

A entertaining quick game with coasters. Fun for everyone!

—description from the publisher

Game play:
The island coaster with the 6 is selected and placed on the table. The remaining coasters are mixed well and placed one after another open in a circle around the 6. Each player gets all 6 soul tokens in a chosen color.

The player rolls the dice and can place exactly one token on the Islands “1” to “5” according to the rolled number. However, if there is already a token on this island, the player must take this and finish his move immediately. 
Island “6”: On this island, any number of tokens can be placed with a rolled 6.
Special coaster “Skull”: On these islands the limit of only one possible token is lifted. The number of the respective island now shows the maximum number of tokens that can be placed there. If this number is exceeded, all tokens located there must be taken.
Special coaster “Saber”: If players pick up tokens from these islands, the player must additionally also take all possible tokens of the same-color from island “6”.

Object of the game: The players are gradually trying to get rid of all their soul tokens. The game ends immediately if a player places his last soul token. This player wins the game!

—description from the designer

Verdammte Piratenseelen möchten sich Deiner bemächtigen .. 
Du willst möglichst schnell diese Seelenmarker loswerden, aber auch die anderen Spieler haben das gleiche Schicksal. Es geht von Insel zu Insel und die glücksbringende 6 hilft Dir immer. Alle anderen Zahlen können böse Folgen haben. Lasst die Säbel rasseln! / ~ basierend auf dem Würfelklassiker Zeppelin ~

Ein kurzweiliges schnelles Spiel mit Bierdeckeln. Spaß für jedermann!

—description from the publisher (German)

Spielablauf: 
Der Inseldeckel mit der 6 wird aussortiert und mittig auf dem Tisch ausgelegt. Die restlichen Bierdeckel werden gut gemischt und nacheinander offen in Kreisform um die 6 ausgelegt. Jeder Spieler bekommt alle 6 Seelenmarker in einer gewählten Farbe.

Der Spieler würfelt und kann auf den Inseln „1“ bis „5“ entsprechend der gewürfelten Zahl genau einen Marker ablegen. Ist allerdings dort auf dieser Insel schon ein Marker vorhanden, muss der Spieler diesen aufnehmen und seinen Zug sofort beenden.
Insel „6“: Auf dieser Insel können beliebig viele Marker mit einer erwürfelten 6 abgelegt werden.
Sonderdeckel „Schädel“: Auf diesen Inseln ist das Limit von nur einem legbaren Marker aufgehoben. Die Zahl der jeweiligen Insel zeigt jetzt die maximale Anzahl der dort ablegbaren Marker. Wird diese Zahl dann überschritten, müssen alle dort liegenden Marker aufgenommen werden. 
Sonderdeckel „Säbel“: Wird von diesen Inseln Marker aufgenommen, muss der Spieler zusätzlich alle gleichfarbigen möglichen Marker der Insel „6“ mitaufnehmen.

Spielziel: Die Spieler versuchen nach und nach alle ihre Seelenmarker loszuwerden. Das Spiel endet sofort sobald ein Spieler seinen letzten Seelenmarker ablegt; dieser Spieler gewinnt das Spiel!

—description from the designer (German)

If you have a dark sense of humor and occasionally find Cards Against Humanity a little too tame, this is the naughty expansion set you've been waiting for.

Guards Against Insanity Asylum Pack takes your Cards Against Humanity experience to a darker, more uncomfortable place.

The Asylum Pack consists of 448 naughty cards; 98 black question cards & 350 white answer cards.

The Asylum Pack can be played as a stand-alone game, however, it's more fun when combined with Cards Against Humanity and CAH expansions.

ADULT HUMOUR: Guards Against Insanity contains naughty adult humour. So, if you or your friends are easily offended, this probably isn't the right product for you.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Profanity: A Card Game For Smutty People
description:
Profanity is a card game for smutty people. If you enjoy reading some of the really messed up stuff on Urban Dictionary, this is the game for you...

That's because you're going to be creating your own deeply disturbing phrases and giving everyone a glimpse inside that warped mind of yours.

How To Play

The smuttiest person begins the game as the Judge. The Judge chooses a starting word and places it face up in the middle of the table.

Players now choose one card from their hand to pair with the Judge's card.

You may combine your card with the Judge's in any order you wish. For example, if the Judge's card was Swallow, the card 'Dutch' could be used to make the phrase 'Dutch Swallow' or 'Swallow Dutch'.

Once a player has announced what their chosen phrase is, they may provide their own twisted definition.

Profanity is as dirty as you decide to make it, however due to the adult nature of the content, we recommend that Profanity is played by guys and girls who are 17+ years of age.

—description from the publisher

In this game you try to collect families.

The game consists of 40 cards divided into eight families of five. Each player receives six cards. The rest go in a pile.
On your turn you will ask another player for a card from a specific family. You must already hold at least one card from this family. If you get a card, your turn continues and you can ask again (same or other player). If the asked player does not have a card from the family, your turn ends and you draw a card from the pile (until it is empty).

This game is essentially Go Fish, but played with a custom deck.

A balancing game with a "platter" suspended from a gallows.
On your turn you need to place a piece on the "platter". A die will determine which colour piece to place. The pieces come in three sizes worth 1, 2, or 3 points. If you succeed in placing the piece without pieces falling off, you get the points for the piece. If you fail, you get no points.

Play for a predetermined number of rounds. Most points wins.

Corbach 1760 is a game for two players based on the battle of the same name, fought on 10 July 1760 during the Seven Years' War.
Corbach was the first battle of the campaign of 1760.

One player controls the French Army under the command of  Saint-Germain and Victor-François, 2nd duc de Broglie marshal of France.
The other controlls the Allied or “Observation” Army under the Erbprinz of Brunswick and Ferdinand, duke of Brunswick.

Available with French rules, but English rules can be downloaded from Vae Victis website.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A solo game requiring the lone survivor of a space mission to gather up equipment on the space ship to make the journey home by escape pod before the oxygen is depleted within the space ship.

Using up to 3 actions, the player moves throughout the spaceship picking up equipment and solving issues that arise throughout the game. Moving through the spaceship reveals equipment found in the spaceship. The spaceship is represented by cards stacked representing the different rooms of the spaceship.

As the the player completes a turn, they draw a card from the Oxygen Levels deck to represent the depleting oxygen supply within the spaceship. The difficulty level can be adjusted by changing the amount of cards within the Oxygen Levels deck.

The base game includes 4 different scenarios that explain what equipment and other requirements are needed to be meet before safely escaping through the Escape Pod.

"Get out while you can still breathe!"

—description from the publisher

This is a game about around-the-world flight using a map showing the North Pole in the middle. Nominally there's an actual race, although this isn't really consistent with the leisurely sightseeing described in many events and use of contemporary passenger airliners. With its flavour texts and other enclosed information, the game is clearly meant to promote geographical knowledge as well as interest in commercial aviation.

Starting from Stockholm, each player colour has its own path connecting to the main circuit around the world. The spaces on the individual and common paths all have flavour texts, game instructions and/or point gains or losses associated with them. This information is found in player folders and the rules booklet, respectively.

Although most of the time players have no say in what happens, some spaces allow choices such as whether to gain a few points or move a step forward, and in a few spots, players are given small tasks such as drawing a llama, using an enclosed nomogram to calculate travel time, or explaining the significance of the International Date Line, which may gain or lose points as arbitrarily judged by an impartial scorekeeper (termed Traffic Controller) and/or the other players.

In addition to points gained from passing by or stopping in various locations, players also gain points based on the order in which they return to Stockholm. The player with the most total points is the winner. A variant to play the game purely as a race with no scorekeeping is also suggested.

Although only six players can fly planes, the rules include the role of scorekeeper in the player count.

ABA stands for AB Aerotransport, a Swedish airline which was folded into SAS just a few years after the publication of the game. Copies exist with just an ABA sticker on the cover or with ABA and SAS stickers; the contents otherwise seem identical.

—description from the publisher game submitter

boardgame: Rally Point Volume 15: Special Study I of the Korean War
description: Ten scenarios from the Tampa ASL Group to be used with ASL module 15 Forgotten War: Korea 1950-53

In the famous little town of Bergues in north France, you move your guard around the city wall following one of the two paths after placing some pavements. Your guard needs to finish his move on a door of his fortress to win. Be careful as you are one of four guards on the way to that door! Remember those pavements side and choose the right path to succeed.

—description from the publisher

The need for Mortals has never been greater, but they've grown suspicious of the creatures we've sent. There's also word on the comms that an Angry Mob has begun to rise up. It's time to tip the favor in our odds. We've cooked up four new creatures they will never see coming!

Campy Creatures: Expansion I adds more Creatures, more Mortals, and more Locations into the mix. The Expansion works as a modular system allowing players to change-in and change-out the Creatures or Mortals they would like to play with for that game.

2nd Edition of base game required.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Harry Potter Miniatures Adventure Game: Dumbledore's Army Pack
description:
Pack of high quality resin miniatures, ready to paint and assemble. Includes character cards and mini cards for the Harry Potter Miniatures Adventure Game.

Includes minatures of Cho Chang, Ginny Weasley and Luna Lovegood

Cyanide & Happiness and Pornhub bring you 50 steamy new cards to add to your Joking Hazard game!
This is an expansion pack! It requires Joking Hazard to play
50 porn-themed cards so erotic they should be banned everywhere except Utah, where they're needed most
Yes, we came up with the name Stroking Hazard first and then worked backwards
If you want to subtly turn your Wednesday game night into an orgy, but are too afraid to ask, this is the way to do it.

—description from the publisher

Press your luck card game where you risk it all for fame.

Select a stunt and roll dice to see if you succeed. Failed stunts give consequences that hamper your future actions. "Death" knocks you out of the game. The surviving player who completes the most points worth of stunts wins.
48 stunt cards
7 character cards
2 standard six-sided dice
1 rulesheet

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blitz Bowl: Dark Elf Player Cards
description:
This mini-expansion was released for free in the September, 2018 edition of White Dwarf magazine

It includes four player cards for the Dark Elf team: Witch Elf, Runner, Blitzer, and Lineman
Paradise is the perfect habitat for all species and provides food for all.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Fire in the Night
description:
Fire in the Night is the third Adventure Pack in the Ered Mithrin cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game! You thought you could rest. After roaming far across Wilderland and slaying two powerful Dragon spawn, you have arrived at the Woodman settlement of Hrogar’s Hill, where you hope to gain some much-needed respite from the dangers that have plagued you throughout your recent days. But it is not meant to be. Shortly after starting a meal, you are roused by the sound of alarm. Outside, a terrifying red-orange glow has cut a swath through the black night. The Dragons’ mother has arrived to exact her revenge.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Ghost of Framsburg
description:
The Ghost of Framsburg is the fourth Adventure Pack in the Ered Mithrin cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game! To kill a Dragon, you need a weapon that is capable of the deed. After battling Dagnir to a stalemate at Hrogar's Hill, it is clear that your weapons are not sufficient to slay the beast. So you have set off for the ruins of the once-great city of Framsburg, where it is said Wormsbane, the legendary sword of Fram son of Frumgar, lies entombed with its master. You hope that this enchanted blade will be enough to conquer Dagnir and leave Wilderland at peace.

But Framsburg has been abandoned for millennia and a feeling of dread consumes you as soon as you set foot in cursed city. Perhaps Fram's sword is not the only thing you'll find hidden in the depths of the city's ancient keep...

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
EZCAPE is a turn based strategy and cooperative card game about Teamwork, Deception, Survival and...Zombies! It is easy to learn, quick to play and will keep you entertained for hours. Each player is tasked with trying to save as many Survivors as possible while simultaneously fighting to stop from being overrun by Zombies. Played in a Russian Roulette style with a single (4 - 6 players) or double (7 - 12 players) deck of game cards ranging in values 2, 3, 5, 8 and 13. There are three types of cards - Red (Zombies), Blue (Survivors) and White (Zombie Traps).

The goal is to keep the attacking Zombies to below 21, while simultaneously taking risks to save as many survivors as possible and the game ends when half the players are converted into Zombies. It is quite fast paced yet provides ample opportunity for strategic gameplay.

boardgame: Age of Hannibal: Fast-Play Battles of Antiquity
description:
Age of Hannibal resolves the largest battles of antiquity in a single evening, on limited table space. Unlike many other rule systems available for the period, Age of Hannibal acknowledges the critical nature of pre-battle decision making in ancient warfare, when commanders sought the best possible ground for their armies. An innovative terrain card system allows players to create unique battlefields, while also incorporating elements of scouting, flank marches, or possible ambushes. This pre-game action resolves quickly, allowing you to get to the battle, where you command vast armies of 50,000 men or more.

For players who would rather replay a famous historical battle, many scenarios are already researched and available, as well as a full campaign system for map-based play. Age of Hannibal also includes a chapter on conducting siege battles, allowing you to design your own citadel, or recreate historical epics like Carthage or Syracuse!

—description from publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: History: Greek & Persian conflicts of the 4th century BC

description:
A mini expansion for the game Carcassonne and part of the Carcassonne series.

With this mini-expansion, you'll send your Meeples to the barber surgeon's. 
A bathhouse had several purposes during the Middle Ages. 
You could take a bath sharing the latest gossip as well as you could enjoy a highly professional medical treatment. 
Of course, your Meeple will be enjoying his time in the bathhouse, so he won't be able to help you. 
To get him back there are various possibilities...

Components: 
6 new land tiles
Rules EN+GER

boardgame: Rome, IInc.: From Diocletian to Heraclius
description:
ROME, IInc. is a game of the Late Roman Empire (the period from Diocletian to Heraclius) for one player, or two co-operating players. A sequel to Rome, Inc.: From Augustus to Diocletian, you again will be running the Roman Empire like a business, but this time the barbarians are well and truly at the gates. Together the payers operate behind the scenes, promoting and removing emperors, governors, and the occasional pope.

When corporate axeman Diocletian terminated his predecessor, no one expected a complete rebranding operation. Realizing that something new was needed to revive the flagging fortunes of a corporate dinosaur he made his friend Maximinus co-CEO in a new east-west operation.

In ROME, IInc., players can accept the challenge solo, or share control of the empire with a partner, one in the East, and the other in the West. Together or alone, you control the mechanics of a failing empire, choosing five distinct “starting points” (286 CE, 363 CE, 425 CE, 497 CE and 565 CE) and run scenarios lasting 10-50 turns, depending on your corporate acumen and endurance. Each turn represents 5-10 years, with 10 turns in each of the five scenarios.

There are 84 Units representing the military forces of Rome; Cavalry, Guards, Legions, Pseudo Legions (with Forts on their reverse), Auxilia, and Fleets.

ROME, IInc. is in issue #61 of ATO

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Spirits of the Forest: Solar Terms Cards
description:
This is a pack of 3 promo cards for Spirits of the Forest.

The cards add 3 more wishes to the game.

The pack was originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 2 Kickstarter campaign in 2018. ThunderGryph made them available to purchase during the pledge manager for Spirits of the Forest.

Each purchase of the pack helped contribute to plant a tree.

Den store dyre quiz is a game, which can be played by anyone interested in wildlife.

Roll the die and answer a question matching the color of the die roll. Answer correctly and you'll collect a colored bottle. But be careful; a white die roll will force you to spin the bottle, and either you or one of your opponents will get a punishment. The first player to collect one bottle of each color wins the game.

A group of desperate adventurers should show their determination, their ability to think strategically and their team spirit in this game, because the stakes are high: either they win or they lose everything. Players have to complete the scenario objectives: to repair the power station that is a key location to protect the entire human civilization. People are aided by the Bobot—electric transformer boxes, where the energy of the Universe lives. They are confronted by the space aliens of the unknown race, Trashmen, who are trying to stop human technological advancements and Energy growth in the Universe. Their dangerous plan will take effect during the super strong solar storm, so the team will have to work within a very limited time frame, before the storm starts. If they manage to repair the Power Station, the Bobot will have enough time to turn on our planet’s protective field that will save the Earth’s energy system and electronics.

Mechanics

In this cooperative game, players should joint efforts to repair the power station. Players can choose among 8 different scenarios that affect the game duration and the set of resources needed to fix the error. Players can also select the difficulty of the scenario, which will influence the starting setup and the number of hours, needed to complete the game. Heroes will have to move around the game board entering different locations, to address situations with the aid of tests and to use their skills in turn. Different dice in the game represents skills. For example, when you test the character Vlad’s Force skill, you use an eight-sided die, and if you are playing for the Bobot, you throw a twelve-sided die. At the end of each player’s turn, Trashmen are making a move. They either try to attack Power Station or come closer to it. More over, any effects, influencing all players, can be triggered during this phase.

Gold doesn't mean much to wizards. Aside from being a smart-ass and always being right, they care about having magic artifacts and spellbooks — but these items aren't easy to get, so sometimes magicians just decide to meet and compete against one another in a refined bidding ritual to take items from others. Whoever first claims two spellbooks wins!

Each player starts Zockende Zauberer with three vials (life points) and three gems: two bearing a pearl and one bearing an imp. Players also receive two spells at random from a shuffled decks of magic cards.

Each round consists of four phases: lay down, call, confirm, and score. First, everyone lays a gem face down, starting with the first player and with this happening up to three times. The active player can call out how many pearls or imps they intend to reveal from among the played gems, and the next wizard can increase this call with a higher number. Once everyone passes on going higher, the player who made the call starts revealing played gems, although they can't reveal two gems from the same pile.

If the call was correct, the active player claims a spellbook; if not, they lose one of their vials. Collect two spellbooks or be the last wizard standing, and you win!

A set of 10 extra cards with (regular) Dutch words for the Dutch version of Codenames.

The cards are:

     Doel/Foxtrot
     Racket/Draai
     Race/Maat
     Horde/Samba
     Diploma/Salsa
     Record/Vloer
     Team/Ritme
     Basket/Disco
     Atletiek/Ballet
     Trainer/Choreograaf

Naked Truth is a social party game. It is a game of fun, personal, edgy questions about our ethics, experiences, sex, attitudes and desires.

One player selects a question and reads it to the group. Everyone then anonymously puts a Yes or No answer card into the center, and also guesses on their own score sheet how many Yes answers are in the group. The reader then reveals the answer cards, and everyone who guessed correctly gets a point.

The key is that players answer anonymously, so they can be truthful about topics that are usually unspoken or taboo.

First designed in 1993, Naked Truth creates funny, powerful and eye-popping moments when you discover how many pee in the shower, cheated on a lover or kissed someone before they knew their name.

In this Canadian abstract game which uses coins of various denominations, players are attempting to move their 4 coins around the board, starting at home and then to the "bank" and finally to the finish space. Players are using dice to move their coins around the board, either using the full total of the dice to move one coin or splitting the dice to move two coins.

There are also rules for capturing other players' coins and protecting oneself from being captured (if you can flip your coins from their 'heads' to their 'tails' side by rolling an odd number).

Players must supply their own coins to play this game. (The game only comes with a felt board to prevent coins from slipping around and two dice.)

—user summary

boardgame: Legend of the Five Rings: The Card Game – Children of the Empire
description:
The land of Rokugan is shifting. Where once there was balance in the elements, there is now chaos. And where once there was peace amongst the Great Clans, there is now conflict. Though mending this wound in the world is of the utmost importance, a monumental edict from Hantei XXXVIII will once again shift the course of history, and it will fall to the children of the empire to find a peaceful way forward for the Emerald Empire.

Children of the Empire is a premium expansion for Legend of the Five Rings: The Card Game, with this being a new level of product that features 234 cards (three copies each of 76 cards and one copy each of six cards), a number comparable to the Legend of the Five Rings: The Card Game Core Set. Unlike previous expansions, premium expansions are not tied to a specific clan, nor do they form part of a cycle; instead, these expansions focus on developing specific themes and mechanisms, rewarding every clan with a wealth of new cards in a single expansion!

In Children of the Empire, you'll find a celebration of the tranquility brought to Rokugan by the Hantei Dynasty. Every Great Clan gets new tools, with themes of dueling and honor persistent throughout the expansion. Children of the Empire also provides a snapshot of characters and events in the Legend of the Five Rings story as they stand today. Look for cards that represent some of the biggest moments from Legend of the Five Rings fiction, and an updated version of an iconic character whose role in the celestial order has shifted!

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Setting: Legend of the Five Rings

boardgame: Fist of Dragonstones: The Tavern Edition – Percy Promo Pack
description:
Fist of Dragonstones: Tavern Edition - Percy Promo Pack is a set of 4 cards and depict characters seen in The Dragon and Flagon.

These are "Special Character Cards" that include: Sir Percival ("Percy"), Wizardess, Barbarian, and Swashbuckler.

These four Special Character Cards can be shuffled directly in the Special Character Card deck in the game.

boardgame: Hora fugit facta manent (fan expansion for T.I.M.E. Stories)
description:
Somebody has already tampered with history during 205 N.T. Your mission is to save the emperor from being killed and thus fixing the timeline.

...
BEFORE YOU PRINT (a note from the designer):
This is a very long scenario, 325 cards, separated in three decks. There are nice checkpoints to stop and proceed at another time. The entire scenario will take about 5-9 hours.

It is playable with 2, 3, or 4 players. It is recommended to play with 4 players, or at least with 4 receptacles.

The scenario might not be for children: in later phases there are some references and also visuals of gore. There is also marginal sexual content, which serves historical accuracy (one of the receptacles is a prostitute).

The scenario will not be sent to Space Cowboys, because I don't think it fits their strategy due to its length.

The scenario is available in English and German. It's also available as a Steam workshop item in Tabletop Simulator.

Imagine standing at the back of a line that you really need to reach the front of… urgently. The line to the buffet two minutes before closing time or the port-o-poop line at the all-day chili cookoff for instance. Welcome to unPerfect Heroes: Battle Lines, the game where you move members of the misfit Waple family against various Bad Guys who have the same goal as you.

You can play unPerfect Heroes: Battle Lines by sharing one deck of 52 cards (consisting of 20 Heroes, aka Waple family members, 20 Bad Guys and 12 Random Crap cards) or each player can have their own deck. Players start by choosing a scenario board and character pieces. The person trying to cut in line plays a Hero card against another player and rolls up to 8 dice to try to fight or bribe his way further up the line. When the odds aren't too good, he may try to chicken out and get a bonus for his next try.

Win by getting your piece up to the finish line first and making your opponents have to wait, "just like you did".

—description from the publisher

Venice Connection: Mint Tide introduces "T" canals in Venice!

While playing Venice Connection, you start with these four "T" canal tiles separate from the sixteen original tiles. These twenty tiles form the reserve; two of the "T" tiles have an I-shaped canal on the reverse side, while the other two feature an L-shaped canal.

The rules for tile placement remain the same as in the original game. On a turn, a player takes 1-3 tiles from the reserve, places them in a straight line with canals intersecting only canals and buildings adjacent only to other buildings, then places this line of tiles adjacent to at least one tile already in play (again, with canals not intersecting buildings. A player can use a "T" tile to add a branch to the canal, or they can use the reverse side for the "I" or "L" shape.

The rules for victory remain the same. If you make a tile placement that "closes the loop" — that is, so that every canal is connected to another canal — you win the game. If you think that the opponent's previous move makes it impossible to close the loop, you can say "Impossible!" If it is indeed impossible to close the loop, you win the game; if the opponent demonstrates how they can close the loop with the tiles that remain in the reserve, then they win.

In the magic land of Choosydragons, children are taught to chase and catch dragons from a young age, but it is not easy. First, you need to get basic hunting equipment and gain experience with smaller dragons, then ultimately you can dare to catch an adult dragon.

Thankfully all local dragons have one weakness. In the local gardens, juicy fruits grow ripe, and the dragons can easily get lured. Do not hesitate to try to catch your dragon!

Chyť Draka! (Catch the Dragon!) is a simple and short game for little children. Players roll a special fruit die and collect dragon or equipment cards.

Ancients Battlelines Clash (ABC) is a set of fast-play battle rules for the ancient and medieval periods. Units are classified by type (infantry, chariots, etc.), role (battleline, skrimisher, etc.), and fortitude (combination of morale, skill, and equipment), and can be further differentiated by special rules giving the missile attacks, special formations, etc.

Play is IGO-UGO, with each player activating all his units during his turn, then the other player activates all his during his. An activated unit can move, charge, melee, etc., and the whole activation is resolved before going on to the next unit. (In other words, there are no separate phases where the whole army first moves, then shoots, etc.)

The suggested basing is similar to the WRG standard, but the rules only really care that all units have the same frontage.

—user summary

In A.D. 2358, due to dramatic development of Artificial Intelligent, it is not hard to find AI in our life. However, mother AI with its own ego was born and the mother AI created the algorithm resembles humankind. Now, the position of humankind and AI is reversed. There are a few rebel forces known as ‘Anonymous’ who tries to destroy the mother AI and reclaim the Earth. They fight with mother AI under a mask and the mother AI deploys AI hackers to eliminate them all. A confrontation between mother AI who tries to have control over the Earth and ‘Anonymous’ who tries to regain the Earth is on.

The objective of Anonymous is to decode the codes of the adversary and reveal the identity before the other team does. If a whole team got caught, they lose and the survivors and their team members get victory points.

‘Anonymous’ is a new edition for Internal Affairs released in 2015. Enjoy the quick and easy plays of the game with the new theme and the system!

—description from the publisher

Move around the Circuit Board using your controller. Playing as Pixoid? Try to escape the Bugs for as long as you can! Playing as a Bug? Do your best to coordinate your moves with the other Bugs to catch Pixoid as fast as possible!

Each round, Pixoid is played by a different player. Once each player has played as Pixoid, whoever has the most victory points wins.

—description from the publisher

Using your controller, choose the route you’ll take for the next leg of the race. There are many possible strategies, and you’ll need to be cunning to be the first across the finish line. Managing your fuel level, accelerating at just the right time, and hindering your opponents will be the keys to success. Be in the lead at the end of the final tile to be declared the winner!

Using your controller, decide the amount of energy you would like to spend on the current Trial. The game is played over a series of rounds that involve each player taking 1 action each round accounting for little or no downtime.  The game can last up to 45 minutes with 4 players. Fly and Gather
Fly and Fight
Rest/Heal

Players roll dice for combat but can heavily influence their die results by discarding magical coins with special powers. Hockey-Dice Challenge is a unique and fast paced game. It's an exciting "head to head" match that leaves everything to chance - the luck of the dice!

Here's how to play:

The home team controls red and blue dice and gets first roll. The visiting team controls black and yellow dice.

The red and black are offensive dice and the blue and yellow are defensive. When the home team rolls red, the visitors roll yellow die to try and stop the scoring attempt. When visitors roll black, the home team rolls blue to try and stop the scoring thrust.

Both offensive dice have power play goal side as well as penalty sides.

The score sheet allows each team 6 offensive rolls per period. If there is a tie after 3 periods, sudden-death overtimes comes into play.

Drop the dice and the game is on!

—description from the publisher

A variant of western chess with a large variety of special pieces.  The main setup remains the same, but two additional musketeer pieces (all powerful, non-standard pieces) are selected by the players and setup on a phantom row behind their own back row.

Musketeer pieces enter the game when the position right in front of them is freed by a move. If the piece in front of a musketeer piece is captured before it entered the game, the musketeer piece is also removed from the board.

The extra pieces create a more dense and cluttered board, but their odd method of entry creates early game information about where your opponent is likely to develop.

Computer based analyses have been run on the musketeer chess pieces providing estimates of their valuation.  These valuations help unlock the early game by giving players an idea of what an advantageous trade would be.

description:
You are a treasure hunter and you enter the deep forests that surround a small town. You hear about the legend of the pure gold coins that are hidden there. According to villagers, these are guarded by a creature of the night, a big werewolf. You should look for coins and be alert for threats, get objects to advance in the search and hopefully catch the werewolf ... If he does not catch you first.

About the game:
It is a card game in the style of "Memory Game" for 1 to 4 players.
The main objective is to find the 6 Gold coins that will be hidden among the game's deck.
Along the same we will be stalked by the Werewolf, the protector of the coins that will try to eliminate you, to avoid it we must find 4 objects: Wolf Trap, Rope, Shotgun and Cartridges.

Game play:
Four cards are placed in a row and face down, one after another in 3 columns, thus completing some 12 cards (4x3). For each turn, the player must turn (reveal) 2 cards from among these 12. If the 2 cards we reveal are not from the same object, we place these cards face down in the same place. Otherwise, if these 2 cards are of the same object, we can obtain one of those cards and the other is placed in the discard deck; that is, we now have that object in our inventory to use. Once this situation has happened, we must replace the two cards that we took from the playing field (the 12 cards) with another 2 cards that we are going to draw from the game deck; there always have to be 12 cards on the table. Once the turn is over, we repeat revealing another 2 cards and so on until we get all the coins or be eliminated by the wolves. When we reveal 1 coin, it becomes part of our inventory and we start a new shift, that is, it is not necessary to reveal 2 coins to obtain it. In the same way we must replace 1 card to take its place in the field of play.

—description from the publisher

description:
A new city rises out of the seafloor. Pumice brick, ceramic and live coral form massive spiraling towers, bustling with the energy of thousands of beings. The merfolk have always lived a quiet agrarian life below the waves, but times are changing and the way forward is building up.

Seascrapers is a solitaire roll-and-write city-building game in which players attempt to develop an underwater metropolis. By rolling dice, players can build facilities to house builders(which allow for the rolling of additional dice), priests (which allow re-rolls of dice), and scholars (which allow for the creation of more advanced buildings). Players can also construct more specialized facilities, which can modify the effects of adjacent buildings or award points. The most lucrative points, however, come from buildings that scrape the very surface of the sea and emerge into the alien world above -- but these first require the development of vast infrastructure below.

Each turn, after dice are rolled and buildings are built, a space on the time track is crossed off. The end of this track represents the conclusion of the merfolk's efforts, and the city's score is tallied up for evaluation.

Can you balance the various classes of merfolk society to build towering seascrapers, or will your city end in a heap on the ocean floor?

—description from the publisher
